text: I beg you, don't go into Silus's museum.
Not a student of history I see. It's for the best. They're a group that should be forgotten.
The only thing you need to know is that they almost destroyed the world, and they were stopped. A long time ago.
Silus comes from one of the oldest families in Dawnstar. They have a complicated history.
Several of his ancestors belonged to the Mythic Dawn, the cult that almost destroyed Tamriel.
His family's involvement was only found out well after the crisis had died down, but it still ruined their reputation. They were outcasts.
And now Silus is back and this museum to the Mythic Dawn is his way of trying to rebuild his family's pride. It's misguided.
Your loss, but if you change your mind, I'll be here.
Yes. My collection of artifacts from a group that toppled an Empire.
Why don't you come in? You can browse the displays, and we can talk. I have a job you look perfect for.
And here comes my first visitor. The Museum of the Mythic Dawn is open, friend!
Some old priest buried here long ago.  I've been searching for his tomb for a few years now.
Maybe you could help find it, I just know it's in here.
I'm sorry, but I can't let that happen.
Hmm, it's encrypted.  It'll take a while to figure this out.
It's probably meaningless to you, but according to family legend it links our bloodline back to Ysgramor himself.
And here's a little something for helping me out.
Here she is.
Fjotra!
If that's how you choose to end your life, so be it.
There are many valuable artifacts all through the temple.
What are you doing in here?
Stay where you are. The mother will deal with you.
Now, come with me, child. We'll prepare you for the goddess's presence.
Oh, I... I'm sorry. I just can't... you'll have to speak to my husband.
Oh, my. You'll want to talk to Enmon about that.
I thought Enmon and Mean had a little girl, but I haven't seen her in a while.
I think you might be looking for Enmon.
And what's that have to do with me?
Yeah, we're all looking for something.
Many thanks!
Oh, thank you!
Thanks.
Thank you!
Well. I do suppose thanks are in order. So... my thanks.
Thanks!
Azura bless you.
I suppose thanks are in order.
Thank you.
Many thanks!
Gods' blessings on you.
Thanks. I guess.
Thank you, dear.
Malacath preserve you.
Thanks!
Well now. I guess I owe you my thanks.
Thanks a lot!
My thanks to you.
Thanks!
Thanks a lot!
Thanks.
Thank you!
Yes, yes, thank you and all that.
Thanks!
Azura bless you.
Thanks!
I suppose thanks are in order.
Thank you.
Many thanks.
Ysmir's blessing on you.
Thanks. I guess.
Thank you, oh thank you.
Ah, my thanks. Malacath watch over ya.
Thank you.
Thanks!
Thanks a lot!
Gods be praised.
Thank you.
Thank you.
You know, I think you're right.
No, I'm afraid I don't know anything of the sort.
Ha! Humor is often in short supply here.
But I sense that perhaps you're after more than just that.
It would seem that the College has what you seek. The question now is what you can offer the College.
Not just anyone is allowed inside. Those wishing to enter must show some degree of skill with magic.
A small test, if you will.
Ah, the immortal plane. It is said to be the source of all magic. This is a noble goal, indeed.
No, not recently at least.
We don't mistake that for acceptance, though. Odds are they're simply too afraid to confront us directly.
And most of us are quite satisfied with that.
Well, yes. Probably.
I mean, yes. Certainly it is, but that doesn't matter now, because I know you're hoarding something even more important at your College.
The Council will be informed of this. They will find out what you're up to.
Is there some way I can assist you?
We are the only group left in Skyrim dedicated to the study of the arcane.
There are others who study, to be sure, but they do so in private, and often in secret.
Well, you clearly possess the minimum aptitude required. Congratulations. I'll escort you up to the gate.
You'll want to speak with Mirabelle Ervine right away. And welcome to the College of Winterhold.
Am I? Am I?
I think I've discovered more here than I could've hoped to.
Your trickery won't confuse me!
I think you'll be a superb addition to the College. Welcome, Apprentice.
I'll lead you across the bridge. Once you're inside you'll want to speak with Mirabelle Ervine, our Master Wizard. Please, follow me.
Excellent.
So, mage from Winterhold, despite your intentions I've beaten your little game.
Even if all you've said here is lies, I know you have something in Winterhold the Synod Council will be very interested in.
So fine, trudge off to Labyrinthian in search of your Staff. I shall return to Cyrodiil and deliver my full report to the Council.
This is not over, I assure you.
So the stories are true... you are Dragonborn!
Normally you'd need to show some aptitude with one of the schools of magic, but you...
I think there is much that we can learn from each other.
If it's true, and you do have the Voice, would you mind showing me? I've never seen it first-hand.
Dragonborn?
It's been so long since we've had any contact with the Greybeards. Do you really have the Voice?
I would be most impressed to see that.
Mind what - or who - you practice this on.
Your power over others shall be great, like mine.
I'm afraid your skills haven't improved enough to be considered.
If you can pass the test, then yes.
I told you, it's the wedding of Vittoria Vici, an extremely well connected merchant with the East Empire Company. The Emperor's cousin? Remember?
Why are we even going to a wedding at this far flung end of the Empire anyway?
Come along, no more stops, we need to find our way to Solitude.
Hopefully these gifts will put us in her good graces, secure that import deal, and lead the way to an audience with the Emperor.
Ha, what a coward. Didn't even put up a proper fight.
No, I guess not. Well, get out of here before I change my mind.
Yes, actually. You look like you're lousy with gold.
All right, hand over your valuables, or I'll gut you like a fish.
Hail, brother! I saw you walking there and thought you might need this.
Hail, sister! I saw you walking there and thought you could use this.
Hail, Nightingale! Luck has shined down on me and made me a rich man. Here is your cut, as promised.
A Nightingale? Oh, I had no idea. My apologies.
Good luck out there!
Well. I, uh, can see you're not one to be trifled with. Let this be a warning to you!
Nice try, but you don't scare me.
Of course! I've seen you at the Flagon. My mistake.
Then I guess that's your last request, fool!
I'm not going to ask again.
The self-loathing enhances Pelagius' anger! Ah, but his confidence will shrink with every hit. You must bring the two into balance.
With the threat gone Pelagius is under the delusion that he is safe, which means you've helped him... sort of. And we're that much closer to home.
The attacks he makes on himself can be seen here fully. They are always carried out on the weakest part of his fragile self.
But the deepest, keenest hatred was for himself.
Oho! I thought you'd never figure it out.
The butler did it! Or is it the advisor? Whoever that man behind the throne was.
Ah, now this is a sad path. Pelagius hated and feared many things. Assassins, wild dogs, the undead, pumpernickel...
All you need to do is find something to wake our poor Pelagius up. You'll find his terrors easy to repel... but persistent.
The objective here is simple, you simpleton!  Use your Wabbajack to defeat the enemy, while they do the same!
That woman wielded fear like a cleaver. Or did she wield a cleaver and make people afraid? I never get that part right...
You've headed down the path of dreams. Unfortunately for you, Pelagius suffered night terrors from a young age.
He would lie in bed, paralyzed with fear, imagining his own nightmares coming to life. Ooohhh... Scary.
You see, Pelagius' mother was... well... let us say "unique." Although, I suppose in the grand scheme of things, she was fairly average for a Septim.
Wonderfully done. Pelagius is finally ready to love himself... and continue hating everyone else.
Don't get too comfortable here, mortal. You may actually earn your way out.
Well now, that's something to crow about. With Pelagius up and about you're moving right along. We'll both be home in no time.
Oh, good choice. Well, good for me. I find everyone being out to get you so terribly entertaining. You might find it... less so.
Put that down.
Careful with that.
Thanks for clearing out that vampire nest. You saved Morthal.
Looking for the blade? You aren't the first!
You won't take my eye, man-thing!
You're after that damn hilt? Go ahead, try and take it!
Let's talk inside.
It's no secret that my family were once members of the Mythic Dawn. One of my forefathers was even chosen to assassinate Uriel Septim himself.
We hid from our past for years, became tradesman, people of coin and influence.
But I realized that the Mythic Dawn's importance -- our importance -- to history cannot be denied.
I'll see everyone in Tamriel remember that for a moment, we held the fate of the world in our hands, for good or ill.
And here you are. Good. Come in, browse the display, and let's talk. I have a job you look perfect for.
Never mind that. Madena is a good woman, but I have my own reasons for opening this museum, and I'm not changing my mind.
I have a collection of artifacts from the group that toppled an Empire. Their importance to history cannot be forgotten.
Why don't you come in? You can browse the displays, and we can talk. I have a job you look perfect for.
Ah, yes. That scabbard. Notice the insignia? An Oblivion Gate. A key symbol of Mehrunes Dagon, the patron Daedra of the Mythic Dawn.
Let's talk inside.
You dare to betray us, Illia?
Our sisterhood will be better off with you dead, brat.
You'll be able to run circles around the big, slow brutes in Heavy Armor.
Well, you've come to the right place.
More than you'll ever realize. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot.
My original intention was to use that arrow on Mercer, but I never had a clear shot.
I made a split second decision to get you out of the way and it prevented your death.
There were three of us. Myself, Gallus and Mercer. We were a anonymous splinter of the Thieves Guild in Riften.
Perhaps I'll tell you more about it later. Right now, you need to head for Winterhold with the journal and get the translation.
Here, take these as well, they may prove useful for your journey.
Farewell. I'll be certain to give Brynjolf your regards.
Enthir... Gallus's friend at the College of Winterhold. Of course...
It's the only outsider Gallus trusted with the knowledge of his Nightingale identity.
The infighting had taken months to subside which gave her time to go into hiding and carefully cover her tracks.
I spent thousands of septims and used every contact at my disposal, but it was as if she had simply vanished... like I said before, she was the best.
I wish I knew. The journal is written in some sort of language I've never seen before.
I saw what they did to Gallus. I wanted to use the Guild's resources to hunt down Karliah. The others didn't even care he was gone.
Fortunately, I persevered and the other groups were either killed or they left Skyrim.
My purpose in using Snow Veil Sanctum to ambush Mercer wasn't simply for irony's sake.
Before both of you arrived, I recovered a journal from Gallus's remains. I suspect the information we need is written inside.
The Guild was thrown into disarray. Several stepped up and tried to claim Gallus's former position as Guild Master.
Sides quickly formed behind these men and the Ratway became a bloodbath.
All I had hoped was to capture Mercer alive.
Mercer must be brought before the Guild to answer for what he's done. He needs to pay for Gallus's murder.
I promise you, the thought crossed my mind. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot.
Without the antidote I administered, you'd be as still as a statue. I treated your wounds and didn't leave you defenseless.
The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot and yet I used it on you.
Don't you walk away from me!
I don't know, Babette. Looks like he may have suffered a head wound. Best not let him out of our sight...
I don't know, Babette. Looks like she may have suffered a head wound. Best not let her out of our sight...
Whoa, whoa, whoa. Slow down. It's all right. You've been through a lot. Maybe you should just sit down for a bit...
I'm with you, Listener.
Sleep.
Damn it, there's got to be a way out of here!
Nazir told me what happened in Solitude. You poor dear. You've been through so much...
Oh! Well in that case, lead on. I'm right behind you.
She's here? By Sithis, I thought we'd lost her. Let's go!
My family thanks you. Take this as a token of our gratitude.
I expected too much. I'll seek someone else to salvage the family honor.
Good. He will have fled to gather others of his kind.
No. It's too late for the wardstone. Vighar must be destroyed. If this gets out, my family will suffer great shame.
You may have seen the open grave in our graveyard. Most of the townsfolk think it's the work of a corpse thief.
The truth is less pleasant. It is...was, the grave of Vighar, my own ancestor, dead for centuries now.
He is also a vampire. My family is entrusted with keeping him buried, but recently someone stole the wardstone, and Vighar escaped.
Destroy Vighar, my long dead vampire ancestor. Have you changed your mind?
You were helpful before. I have need of your help again, if you're willing.
I'm on a pilgrimage to the Shrine of Azura, the Goddess of Twilight. If you'll excuse me.
I can't believe I just met the Gourmet!
You know, I've heard the Gourmet is in Skyrim right now...
It is the Champion of Namira! I was just about to eat. Here, you should have the first bite.
In the mountains to the northeast. I'll mark it on your map.
I've heard of him. My father had suspicions about his connection to the Mythic Dawn. Guess they were true.
I don't need this. My family wasted 8 generations keeping that Razor safe from a dead cult. As far as I care, it can stay locked in my house.
All at once! You're efficient. I like that. Here's your reward.
Best leave the past buried, I say.
Easy. Here. These are the keys. The hilt is in a chest in my house, just don't hurt anyone.
You want a fight? You got it.
Divines know the mill could use some new blades. All right. It's yours. Here's the keys. It's locked in a chest inside my house.
Why don't you take your coin and keep walking?
We just need the shards of the blade and the hilt, now.
We just need the pommel and the shards of the blade, now.
We just need the hilt and the pommel, now.
There's only the shards of the blade left to collect. Exciting, isn't it?
There's only the pommel left to collect. Exciting, isn't it?
There's only the hilt left to collect. Exciting, isn't it?
You want the hilt that bad? Fine. Here's the keys. It's locked in a chest inside my house.
I don't care, but my ancestors do. You can't have it.
Damn it. Why'd you have to walk into Morthal?
Fine. The hilt is in a chest inside my house.  Here are the keys. Take that damn thing and go.
Guess I should have known. Maven and Sibbi have been at odds ever since she had him tossed into jail.
Look, if I were you, I'd proceed carefully. Crossin' Maven is one of the first things you learn not to do when you're down here.
As far as the Guild's concerned this is between Sibbi and Maven, so we're going to officially stay out of it.
Who in their right mind would be stupid enough to pay you to steal from Maven?
I believe this gem is worth more than you are at the moment.
It was Atmah's idea to come here, at first. She talked me into it, and I convinced the others.
Savos is right. We can make it if we just stay alert.
We all knew this was the end. Without even opening the door, we knew what was behind it would kill us.
You keep talking like you're sure we'll find something useful in here.
Elvali died here. I don't even remember what killed her. One of the countless faceless horrors. I think she was glad, in that final moment.
Now you've put it all to rest, but it can't undo my mistakes. They can never be undone...
There were six of us. Full of ambition, eager to conquer the world.
We can't go back. Might as well go forward. We can still do this.
What happened after was my fault. All mine.
Can you imagine the looks on their faces when we come back?
We could have turned back. It could have ended there. But we kept going.
That's how I consoled myself for years, after I'd sealed this place shut and vowed never to let anyone open it.
But now it all comes out again...
Gods, what have we done?
I was in charge now. I pushed them on, insisting it would be worse to try and go back.
I can't believe we're doing this.
When it was over, Atmah, Hafnar and I stared, pale-faced, at one another, unwilling to admit we'd made a terrible mistake.
If we'd all died there, if we'd loosed the thing on the world, who knows what might have happened?
I never meant for any of what happened here. Tried to seal it up, lock it away forever.
It's too late. There isn't enough of him left to go back in after.
Atmah cried to herself. Hafnar wouldn't look at either of us. And I kept telling them it would be all right.
Let's just get inside, see what's in there.
And then we were all fighting just to survive. None of us were prepared. It was amazing the rest of us survived.
It would seem I'm bound to this place. The bitter irony of it all -- my greatest failure, and even in death I can't escape it.
We barely made it out alive, and you want to go back in?
I've no idea what killed her, but I'm sure something did.
Don't forget, this whole idea was Atmah's to begin with.
I had to leave them behind, had to sacrifice them so I could make it out alive.
I knew you'd come eventually.
We... we have to go back. We can't leave Girduin...
There were only three of us left. Takes-In-Light just sat down and gave up, and we left her there to die.
You would care about that, since you're the Arch-Mage's favorite!
One moment we joked about what we'd find below, the next he'd been ripped in half.
I had no choice, don't you see?
Against six College-trained mages? I think we'll be fine.
"If we can just make it through, it'll all be worth it", I told them. And the fools believed the words I myself didn't trust.
We'll be back at the College before anyone even knows we're gone.
Girduin died first. It happened so fast, none of us had a chance to react.
"No matter what, we stay together", Hafnar said. I looked him in the eyes and lied to him.
And what if... What if there are things guarding this place?
Are we truly sure this is a good idea?
We thought it would be so simple.
Enchanted weapons, tomes of ancient knowledge, Shalidor's secrets themselves -- who knows what we could find!
I... I don't know why I pressed the others on, convinced them to keep going.
Come on, we're finally here! Let's not waste any more time!
We were sure we'd find it all here, hidden away from time. Power, knowledge... All the things we didn't want to wait for.
What was that thing, anyway?
None of our spells were potent enough, none of our wills were strong enough.
Given the history of this place, it's more than likely there's still some amount of power here.
Hafnar was covered in blood, but his stupid Nord pride wouldn't let him admit defeat.
Yes, well so do I. That's why I was hoping you could help.
Let him know that we've unearthed something... Well, I'm not sure. Something unique, let's say.
It's clearly magical in nature, but like nothing I've ever seen before. He should be most interested.
I'm not really sure. Made and used by Magnus himself, if you believe those sorts of things.
I believe I've heard it said that it's the only thing that could adequately contain his power.
The sort of embellishment wizards of ancient times loved to make.
I warned them it might be dangerous. It's not my fault they didn't listen.
As long as the Synod don't blame us for this mess, I have more pressing matters to deal with.
Not enough to bother with it. Arch-Mage Aren's approach to these things is to just let them sort themselves out.
Although now it looks like you'll be doing the sorting. Good luck with that.
Oh, I think I'll be fine. We seem to have eliminated the most pressing threat.
It certainly seems that whoever placed this here intended for it to be well guarded. I wonder why...
Your father's too easy on you, boy. I'd have you cutting wood at dawn if you were my son.
As do I. That man saved my life. I hope that someone helps him, as he once helped me.
And how is that old warrior doing these days?
Papa says I'm not old enough yet, so I help out by patrolling the town with Lucky here.
Oh dear. I wish there was something we could do to help him.
Father, how come Olda's so mean to everyone? She's only ever happy when she's with Lodvar, and she's not even married to him!
Yes, just this morning. It was from Logrolf. He wrote to thank us for the food we sent.
Fine.
Haven't you got some chores to do?
Not well. He's convinced that his house is haunted, so he's sleeping on the street.
Did you read that letter from Markarth yet?
That's no business of yours, son. Mind you keep your nose out of it.
Time to die, hero!
Go ahead, try and fight back!
Forsworn forever!
Bleed for me, mortal!
Behold my power!
The elements are mine to command!
Exactly! Now you're beginning to see how our little system works.
Maven sent word that you discovered something else while you were out there. Something important to the Guild?
Come on, everyone knows about you and Brynjolf.
If you like I'd be happy to take it over the operation for you. Take the source off your hands.
Well, everyone's wrong. Now why don't you just shut up about it.
And lose out just when the going is getting good? No thanks.
Hey Ton, you shouldn't cheat on Vekel. He's a good guy.
The only thing keeping me from having you killed is Brynjolf. He's stepped in and made amends.
At least you remembered to bring me the information I requested. Give me that.
This doesn't tell me much. The only thing that could identify Sabjorn's partner is this odd little symbol.
Yes, I'm going with you and together we're going to kill her.
Here's your payment for Solitude. Prepare yourself and meet me at the ruins as soon as you can. We can't let her slip through our fingers.
I don't know where you find your sources, but that last shipment of skooma was some of the best quality I've seen in years.
This doesn't tell me much. The only thing that could identify Sabjorn's partner is this odd little symbol.
What are you talking about?
Because, even after all their posturing and planning, they've made a mistake.
The parchment you recovered mentions a "Gajul-Lei." According to my sources, that's an old alias used by one of our contacts.
His real name is Gulum-Ei. Slimy bastard.
I just got lucky is all. Bumped into someone who knew someone else. The rest just writes itself.
I thought it was painfully obvious that Gulum-Ei was important to the Guild.
I'm sorry to say your payment for the job will have to go to elsewhere since you feel like taking things in your own direction.
Now, did Gulum-Ei give up any information on our buyer?
Welcome to the cozy little family. I'm one of the lookouts for the Guild. I watch Delvin's back.
If you're looking for extra work, talk to Vex or Delvin. They'll fix you up.
Stick to the rules and you'll end up rich. Break them and you're out on your arse. Simple, right?
If you're looking for conversation, The Flagon isn't the place.
Vekel and me, we have a thing going. So I recommend you keep your hands off the merchandise.
Vekel and me, we have a thing going. Try anything with him and it'd be bad for your health.
You don't impress me. So don't even try.
I warned you not to cross Maven. If I hadn't stepped in and paid amends, you'd be... well, I think you know.
At least Sabjorn has found himself in Whiterun's prison. How unfortunate for him.
Hear my Thu'um! Feel it in your bones. Match it, if you are Dovahkiin!
A gift, Dovahkiin. Yol. Understand Fire as the dov do.
A gift, Dovahkiin. Toor. Understand Inferno as the dov do.
A gift, Dovahkiin. Shul. Understand Sun as the dov do.
Speak. I will listen.
You'll die this day, dragon!
Come on, you monster!
You remind me of my cousin's cat. Killed that, too!
In their tongue, he is Dovahkiin.
But no one wanted to believe. Believe they even existed.
Their defeat was merely a delay.
Dragonborn.
And when the truth finally dawns, it dawns in fire.
To the time after Oblivion opened.
You should have acted. Theya??re already here.
But, therea??s one they fear.
When the sons of Skyrim would spill their own blood.
The Elder Scrolls told of their return.
Not a chance. Get'em, boys!
Hmm... looks like it's all here. Okay, go on through. But stick to the road, and don't make any sudden moves.
All right, that's far enough. You know how this works- toll here is 100 gold.
Don't play dumb with me. Pay up, and you walk right through. Refuse, and you'll never see the other side.
Not my problem. You want through here, you pay. Or else.
I may have lost a drop or two of blood. Truth is, I'm not going anywhere like this. Hurts to breathe.
In these parts? Good luck. But don't come this way again.
These two aren't a problem anymore.
Make 'em bleed.
The Silver Hand. They finally had the nerve to attack Jorrvaskr. We got most of them, but I think a few stragglers made it out.
Don't leave any of them alive.
The beasts return!
Your blood is ours, Companion!
You belong to Hircine now!
I am a loyal citizen of the Empire. My parents were Legionnaires, and I've followed in their footsteps.
But more importantly, I am a daughter of Skyrim and wish to see her made whole again.
I consider this conversation over.
I'm a soldier, not a politician. And my personal beliefs are not yours to question.
I oppose tyranny. I oppose those who tell me how to live, what to think, and what to believe.
I'm a man. Skyrim is man's homeland. That's a fact. A fact I'm proud of. There's no shame in that. Read your history.
If that's all, there's pressing concerns that need dealing with.
Not this Empire. The world's better without it. Certainly Skyrim is.
I fought in the Imperial army in the War against the Dominion. I bled and spilt blood for the Empire. And for what?
The Empire to bend its knee before those evil elf bastards? Signing a treaty meant to kill the heart of the Empire itself. To deny Talos?
No. I will have no part of any such Empire. And I will gladly gut anyone who says otherwise.
Enough talk. There's much to do.
Reasons? Since when does a man need a reason to protect his family, to defend his homeland?
It's the damn outlanders and Empire that need the reasons.
No, how did you find me? Just give me more time, I can pay Maven back!
What? No! Guard, help!
Maven sends her regards Letrush.
Indeed. If we ever get there.
Don't bother me, peasant. I've had a long journey and paid too much coin on these gifts to be late to Vittoria's wedding.
Hello there fellow, traveler. One itinerant minstrel and wandering wastrel, at your service.
Hello there, friend. How lucky for you to chance upon a bard on the road.
Ah, smell that fresh air? Truly, this is a good place to play a song.
The life of adventure and song is the life for us, eh friend?
For a generous fellow traveler? But of course. What would you like to hear?
Sorry friend, but it looks like you're a bit light in the purse, and the wayward minstrel can't afford to do free performances.
I've got a message for you from Louis Letrush. This is for double crossing him.
This has nothing to do with you, so back off.
Ugh. Speak to my husband if you must.
Get away from me, you Skyrim trash.
You! You must have told Maven about the horse. If you'd just stuck to the plan, I wouldn't be in this mess.
Listen, wench, we're on to you. We know who you are, so you'd better come with us right now!
We've made a mistake. Now move along before that mouth of yours gets you into trouble.
They thought I was some other Redguard woman! They just wouldn't leave me alone. I hope they get lost in the wilderness.
What? Oh... you're right.
Foreigners. What a nuisance!
She doesn't have the scar.
I'm not going anywhere.  Now back off before I do something drastic.
Brother, I think she's telling the truth...
I don't have time for this foolishness. Now come along quietly and we won't have to get rough.
Next time you're looking to pester someone, make sure it's the right person!
I don't know who you think I am, and I don't care. Leave me alone!
No Firebrand Wine yet? How disappointing.
Krosis. Sorrowfully, no. It cannot be known to me.
Your kind - joorre - mortals - created it as a weapon against the dov... the dragons.
Our hadrimme, our minds cannot even... comprehend its concepts.
Alduin komeyt tiid. What else would you seek? Alduin and Dovahkiin return together.
But, I do not know the Thu'um you seek. Krosis. It cannot be known to me.
Hmm. Yes. They are very protective of me. Bahlaan fahdonne.

Are you enjoying the party?
Is the food and drink to your liking?
Is my staff seeing to your needs?
This gathering is nothing more than a boast. The Thalmor are reminding us that we're at their beck and call.
We've got troubles enough without the Thalmor and their witch-hunts.
I'd rather be back at Whiterun, but while I'm here I might as well get some real news from the other parts of Skyrim.
These Thalmor might not like to admit it, but as long as the Empire has Skyrim, the Empire has strength.
Born and raised right here in Rorikstead.
My father was a soldier. He fought in the great war and when it was over, he retired here to raise a family.
My mother passed away when I was just a babe, so he did his best to raise me on his own.
It's not the most exciting place in the world, but the people here work hard and don't cause trouble for anyone.
Not every Nord is obsessed with strength, honor and skill at arms.
A rare few are born with a gift for making money. My investments are my strength and my wealth is my weapon.
As for honor, well, there are some luxuries even I can't afford.
Oh, yes! Quite! So many important people from all across Skyrim.
Splendid. Call if you have need of me.
It pleases me to hear you say so.
Excellent. I'll leave you to it, then.
Don't mention the Guild. In fact, it's better if you don't speak to me at all. This is not the place.
For now, perhaps. But I've heard disturbing rumors that they're marshalling their strength.
I suspect the treaty that ended the war was just a tactic to buy time so the Thalmor can rebuild their armies.
I hope I'm wrong. I'm not sure the Empire can afford another war.
Why, thank you. You are most courteous.
I admit, I'm surprised to see you here. Normally these parties are reserved for  the Jarls and business leaders of Skyrim.
Elenwen must be very fond of you.
Yarr!
Horkers' revenge!
This is for Haldyn!
Wait, where did you get this? This piece was thought to be lost forever, please take this with my utmost thanks!
Careful, huh? Thanks, Skulvar. I'll remember that. Ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
I see no harm in it, if you have the skill.
The grindstone will improve your weapons. Use the table to improve your armor.
If you've got the raw materials, you can use the forge to make something new.
I guess you don't have potatoes in your ears after all. Just make sure we get a fresh batch in soon.
That's a fair price. My blade is yours.
Looks like you've got some help already. I'll join you, but you'll have to let your comrade go. What do you say?
I'm what you'd call of soldier of fortune. Make me an offer, and I just might fight at your side.
If you've got the gold to pay my fee, then my sword-arm is yours.
Can't be too careful these days, with the war and other dangers. For a fair price, I'll watch your back.
Some call me a mercenary, but I like to think of myself as a freelance adventurer for hire.
My skill in battle is unmatched. Fortunately for you, that skill can be bought.
By blade or by spell, if you want it dead, I'll make it dead. All you need to do is pay my fee.
Blade and shadow, silence and death - these are my arts. For a modest fee, I'll make great art for you.
If it's a mercenary you're looking for, you've found him. There's no stronger sword-arm for hire in all Skyrim.
Truly? So maybe my mother isn't crazy after all.
You had best tell the Jarl in Whiterun. He needs to know about this.
Yep. Ale's going bad.
Did you hear me?
The ale is going bad. We need to get a new batch.
Hard not to.
Orgnar.
Orgnar!
Are you listening?
Lucan's got a little bit of everything at the Riverwood Trader.
If you need weapons or armor, see Alvor, the blacksmith.
Don't see that every day.
I've never seen anything quite like that.
Mighty impressive.
Wow! That's impressive.
By the Hist, what a sight....
Impressive. Damn impressive.
Wow. Would you look at that.
By Ysmir! That's impressive.
Gotta admit, that's impressive.
By the Nine!
He would dote on her so much. Let her work the forge and everything. I miss him so.
Riverwood's agreeable enough, I suppose. For a Nord village.
I understand. Speaking to a goddess is overwhelming for many. Return when you're prepared.
Oh, a customer. Sorry you had to hear that.
I'm with the Vigil of Stendarr. We believe this house might have been used for Daedra worship. Evil rites and so forth.
Coward. You're nothing. Nothing!
I can't talk about it. Rest assured, the Jarl hears everyone's concerns. You will be able to visit the dead again soon.
I don't need to know the details. Blessings of Arkay to you for your help.
Here. Take my amulet as a reward.
This shrine was built by the Dunmer. As our land was scorched by fire and brimstone, Azura's prophecies led us to safety.
She is a Daedra, a powerful being who watches from beyond our mortal plane. She has chosen you to be her champion.
Fine. I can't force you to help me, but the offer is open.
Is it dead?
Dovahkiin! No!!
You... must be Dragonborn!
Keep firing!
That's it! We'll bring that bastard down!
Is that all you've got?
For Whiterun and Skyrim!
Come down here and fight, you coward!
I can't get a bead on him!
Does anyone see him?
We don't stand a chance!
Stay under cover!
Now you're going to pay!
Hold it there! Keep your distance.
Take him down! It's our best chance!
I can't believe we actually killed a dragon!
Dragonborn... it's an honor.
You saved Whiterun today. We won't forget.
Is it really dead?
Come on, I want a better look at that thing.
I'm glad you're on our side.
Good luck. I hope you get there in time.
And what's wrong with him, huh?
I don't know where we're going, but Sovngarde awaits.
Ulfric? The Jarl of Windhelm? You're the leader of the rebellion.
But if they've captured you.... Oh gods, where are they taking us?
Watch your tongue. You're speaking to Ulfric Stormcloak, the true High King.
Fascinating! So the ancients were more daring... or foolhardy... similar to the Dragon Break itself, although less far-reaching...
This Elder Scroll might be able to show you the past in some way, then... yes, yes, it could work.
Return it here, to the Tiid-Ahraan. Then... Kelle vomindok. Nothing is certain with such things...
But I believe the Scroll's bond with the Tiid-Ahraan will allow you a... a seeing, a vision of the moment of its creation.
Then you will feel - know - Dragonrend, in the power of its first expression.
You will see them... wuth fadonne... my friends - Hakon, Gormlaith, Felldir.
Do you know the College of Winterhold? They have a deep interest in such arcana.
Hmm. Well, if that's what the Greybeards told you, I confess I don't have a better plan.
So little is known about the Elder Scrolls, almost anything is possible where they're concerned. Artifacts of truly inconceivable power.
Paarthurnax? That name is familiar... the legendary leader of the Greybeards?
I would not have expected a scholar of such an esoteric turn of mind among their ranks.
What are you waiting for?
Just keep swinging!
Pull him back!
Aye.
Hey, a fight!
Vilkas, take him out to the yard and see what he can do.
Vilkas, take her out to the yard and see what she can do.
Come on now, watch the footwork. Keep your balance.
Strike when the shoulder turns. He's giving you openings!
Watch the eyes!
Just keep moving, you'll wear him out!
Are those two at it again?
Nice!
Come on!
I've been working on a shield for Aela.
My wife is in mourning and I need to get back to her soon. I'd be much obliged if you could take this to Aela for me.
Wait, before you go...
Great, back to the fight.
Always good to see 'em go.
So who's winning now?
Keep a sharp eye about you and maybe I'll teach you to fight some day.
Watch the blades.
Vilkas will see if you're worth our trouble.
I hear you have spirit. This is good. You may yet join our numbers.
Keep up the effort, boy. Eyes on the horizon, always.
Keep up the effort, young one. Eyes on the horizon, always.
Get him!
Chop chop!
Get in there and push!
All right!
I won't be the one to go back there. I won't return to Avanchnzel. You can't make me do it.
Then you didn't know Roggvir, and you have no idea what it means to be a Nord of Skyrim!
He did the honorable thing, even when he knew it would cost him everything.
My brother was the best man in Solitude.
Depends on what you mean by "busy." I'm also the head jailor in the dungeon here. Quiet job, usually.
Roggvir. He opened a gate and they executed him for it.
He opened a gate and the wrong man rode out of it. If that man hadn't been Ulfric Stormcloak. If Ulfric hadn't killed High King Torygg...
But it was Ulfric. And he did kill the High King, in honorable combat. My brother refused to allow the the Imperials to take revenge for the deed.
So now he's dead.
You mean Roggvir. Yes. I made an appeal to Captain Aldis. He allowed Roggvir to have a proper Nord burial, as is the custom in Solitude.
Work in it? I own it.
You could say that. I own the place.
You must bring it to Avanchnzel, in the west. Return it to them. It must go back.
Then like Roggvir, you're a Nord at heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand.
I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had...
I... I think I need to stop talking now...
This city has a long history of madness and murder. The Wolf Queen... Pelagius... The death of High King Torygg... And now, public executions.
My books tell the stories. Have no doubt - as pretty as its streets are, as jovial as the bards may be... darkness is drawn to Solitude.
Listen, Roggvir and I grew up together. It was us, a few others and Vivienne. Vivienne Onis.
I have no idea why, but one day, I told Roggvir I liked her. It was a stupid thing to do... He mocked me incessantly after that.
From that point forward, every time Vivienne and I were anywhere close to each other, he made sure to humiliate me.
He'd beat me up, dump mead on my head, anything he could get away with. Once he even slipped a beehive into my bed.
We were children, sure. But Roggvir never outgrew that immaturity, or his cruelty. So any talk about him being an "honorable" Nord? Pure folly.
Sometimes you just do what needs to be done. You don't have to hate a man to kill him.
Though, admittedly... it helps.
If you're looking for a good attitude go see the Bards. They'll fake it for you if nothing else.
It is done. I won't go back to Avanchnzel. I won't.
I could have left it behind. I could have put it back where it came from.
Oh, wow. I knew it. I knew they were real! I have to remember to tell papa.
No. I think he was entirely wrong. Roggvir's execution was justified and necessary.
Being honorable might make you a good man, but it doesn't make you right. Be a better world if it did.
Let's just say the headsman's axe may not be the worst way for a Solitude Jail prisoner to die.
Look at that. I've told you to watch your tongue and mine is waggling. I've said enough.
I showed up to watch, yeah.
I don't know if they were right to execute him for what he did, but I'll tell you something...
It couldn't have happened to a nicer guy.
Really? Was it big? Did it breath fire?
Not my finest hour. Roggvir was a fine and honorable man.
No. That honor is Sybille Stentor's. A smart man steers clear of Stentor.
And keeps himself out of the dungeon when she's having a bad day...
Dragon?
You really don't know?
He let Ulfric Stormcloak escape Solitude. Ulfric rides in, murders the High King and Roggvir lets him ride right back out.
Then we should soon be selling arrows by the bushel. A sword is no good against a dragon.
Where the Dragon attacked?
We've heard rumors of death and... and fire from the skies. But no real news.
Can any man hope to be ready for a dragon?
I can but hone their skills and harden their hearts. The rest is up to the gods.
You're the Dragonborn aren't you?
I'm sort of hoping you're going to fight the dragons for us. A normal man is no match for a dragon.
Don't forget me next time you need herbs and potions.
Then like Roggvir, you have a Nord's heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand.
I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had...
I... I think I need to be done talking for the moment.
I'm not looking for conversation.
I'm in mourning. Can't you see that?
New in town, right? If you need a place to sleep, head over to the Winking Skeever. We keep a fine inn.
You know, the Winking Skeever's not just the best inn in Solitude. It's the also the only inn in Solitude.
Ain't got one. But I'd fancy a chance at that Jaree-Ra's scrawny neck.
Filthy Argonian never would have set foot in this city, if High King Torygg was still alive. I can tell you that.
Oh, I am well. I will be well. Well to be within the will inside the walls.
Excellent. You've done us a great service. Here is your reward.
The Jarl has put out a bounty to slay a giant. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
If you change your mind, seek me out here.
Oh, thank the gods. Here, take this with my thanks.
Here I'll show you on your map so you can stay away. Please, if you can help me let me know or get out of my way so I can try to find a priest.
You there, please you have to help me! I was attacked by a vampire! Do you have a potion or something that can cure this before I turn?
I'm on my way to Windhelm to join up with the Stormcloaks. Ulfric has the right of it.
The Empire is in shambles. They've banned the worship of Talos.
I may have roots in Cyrodiil, but Skyrim's as much my home as any Nord's.
I'm on my way to Solitude to join the Legion. A united Empire is better for everyone.
I was born and raised in Skyrim, but the Nords treat us like dirt.
I want to be on the right side.
Then get out of my way. I have to get to a town and find a priest.
The Jarl has put out a bounty on Forsworn. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Really? Power? Haven't you already killed enough of your foes using the more mundane means at your disposal? Swords, magic, etc.? How... uninspired.
Defense? No one would dare attack Riften. I'm not sure you realize just how well connected Jarl Maven is with the Empire.
With a single snap of her fingers, we could have a legion here in a fortnight.
As steward, I serve as an intermediary between the Jarl and her subjects.
I also handle the less important and day-to-day policies that effect daily life in Riften.
Lately I've had my hands full suppressing these ridiculous accusations of corruption in the keep.
I'm not certain what business it is of yours, but I'm moth... I mean Jarl Maven's steward.
I run the prisons in Riften, keep the riff-raff from robbing the good folk of this city and have drunks tossed into the lake.
As long as she continues to keep me at her side, she can count on my blade to protect her.
To be honest, I doubt someone would be stupid enough to make an attempt on Maven... sorry, Jarl Maven's life.
But just in case, I'll be ready for them.
Ulfric's a bloody hero. I don't think there's a worthy Nord alive who'd dare argue the point.
He's taking the Empire to task and making them answer for their crimes.
I don't think I'll be content until Ulfric sits upon the throne of Skyrim with the ashes of the Concordat at his feet.
Hey, if the Empire and the Stormcloaks want to kill each other over some foolish cause, I say let 'em.
All I care about is two things: watchin' Maven's back and countin' the gold she pays me to do it.
I don't care what you think. The Empire put Maven on the throne for a reason. That's all you need to know.
If you say so.
Indeed. You don't get to the position my mother's achieved by blind luck... her business sense and diplomatic skills are legendary.
The throne suits me, don't you think?
My title is just a formality. I've always been in charge around here.
Jarl Maven Black-Briar. Has a pleasant sound to it, wouldn't you say?
Speak, I wish to hear everything my subjects have to say.
Unmid and Anuriel shouldn't blame themselves for Riften's fall, I am the one who's ultimately responsible.
If Riften can be retaken, I will redouble my efforts to ensure the safety of my subjects and the defense of the Rift.
I presume you have something important to say?
If you're here to report any instances of corruption, be confident that we're handling these rumors accordingly.
The Imperials now control Riften and have placed Maven Black-Briar on the Jarl's throne.
Perhaps the Stormcloaks might rally to take back Riften one day. Only time will tell.
There's nothing more I can teach you.
You've already learned all I can teach you.
You already know all that I know.
It's as rewarding as it is dangerous. Let's see what I can show you.
With a master of magic at your side, you'll have nothing to fear.
The only thing better than a powerful mage fighting at your side is... well, nothing, really.
Why hire a common soldier to protect you when you can have a master of the arcane?
For a modest fee, I'll bring my formidable arcane powers to bear against your foes. What do you say?
Why settle for just stabbing your foes when you can roast them alive in a gout of arcane fire?
Watch your back, friend. Better yet, pay me to do it for you.
My blade is sharp and I thirst for battle. Onward, then!
Best not to walk the roads of Skyrim alone.
Sell-swords are common in Skyrim, but you'll find precious few as stout and as a strong as I am.
If you're looking to hire the strongest mercenary in Skyrim, I'd wager your search is over.
I've fought in more battles than I've got years of life in me. Pay my fee and I'll put that experience to work for you, friend.
Death is my art, and like all artists, I seek a patron. For a mere handful of gold, I will follow you into any danger.
I know nothing of fear, and nothing of remorse. Pay my fee, and together we will vanquish any foe.
I am a lethal instrument, yours to command for a modest sum. I suggest you hire me before one of your enemies does.
We welcome you to worship the Eight Divines, freely and openly.
I'm afraid I'm running errands at the moment. But please, visit me later at the Temple of the Divines.
Feel free to take a seat, or pray at the shrines. We appreciate worship in all its forms.
Come by the Winking Skeever any time you want to hear a tune.
You should think about staying the night. Corpulus keeps a clean inn.
Come back if you want to play tag.
Papa doesn't like when I talk to customers, anyway.
Remember the Winking Skeever next time you're footsore.
Need anything, you just say the word.
I was done talking anyway.
Yeah. Next time.
May song speed you along.
May the words of poets keep you warm.
May all your adventures be worthy of telling.
Now you can say you've met the most talented bard in all of Skyrim.
Don't forget my name. Someday, everyone will be saying it.
Glad that distraction's over...
We're done then? Good.
I suppose I should return to my papers...
Always good to talk to a fellow bard.
Farewell, stranger.
Stop by again, sometime. I'll sing for you.
Anytime, friend.
Always time for a fellow bard.
Oh, if you haven't heard Dean Pantea sing, you should.
We should talk again sometime.
Always glad to talk to a bard.
Thanks for taking the time. People never want to talk to me.
Time to finally see the sky, and make it rain red.
Okay, I'll go now.
I'm glad I could help.
And you've proven that was the best move I could have made. Don't give me that look. You're free to go.
Here, how about a little token for your efforts? My family's ring and all the things the guards confiscated from you when you were jailed.
Now if you'll excuse me, I have to figure out how we're going to fill our recently emptied mine.
Anything I can do.
Always glad to talk to the bard who returned part of the Poetic Edda.
It's been an honor.
I'll get back to court business, then.
Always have your shield at the ready.
Keep your sword arm strong.
Take care of your armor, and it'll take care of you.
I must return to the court. They simply can't make do without me.
Good, we've wasted enough time here.
We'll speak again later. If we must...
I'll be sure to check "Talk to random stranger" off my list of today's tasks...
You are free to leave.
You are excused.
I am done with you. You may go.
Be well. Pray for Solitude and my husband.
Keep the faith. General Tullius will win this war.
We have the utmost confidence in you and Ulfric's guiding hands.
May the blessings of the Eight be with you.
May the blessings of the Nine be with you.
Feel free to return to me with questions. The Blue Palace is an open forum.
Please feel welcome in the court of Solitude.
I won't forget what you've done for Solitude.
Anyone with a stout heart like yours is welcome here.
I'm proud to call a man like you friend.
I'm proud to call a woman like you friend.
Do not dishonor or attempt to harm Elisif in any way. Adhere to that rule, and you'll be fine.
I must return my attention to Elisif. If were were to lose another Jarl while I was... distracted...
Take care of yourself, and always remember: the world is ripe with people looking to spill your blood...
Stay guarded. You never know what could be out there.
All right. Back to slicing, dicing and baking.
Back to my errands.
Darkness guide you.
Yeah, yeah. Goodbye, kill lots of people, Hail Sithis, and all that.
Have a good contract.
Kill well, and often.
Just come back alive, and I'll be happy.
Go on now. Time to get your hands bloody.
Take care of yourself, beef roast.
Later, ham hock.
Yeah, good luck.
Represent us well, brother.
Represent us well, sister.
Remember, when you kill - we all wield the blade.
Watch yourself out there.
Cicero bids you... farewell.
Oh, yes. Goodbye, goodbye.
Farewell, farewell!
Skuli owns the inn with his mother gone, but I handle the rooms.
You should head on inside. Eydis or Skuli can take care of you.
Are you finished ogling the grotesque? I suppose I should be grateful you didn't simply attack me.
We are traveling to the Shrine of Kynareth in Whiterun. I hear the Gildergreen is more beautiful than ever since it's been restored.
They say that eating the fruit it bears will add years to your life.
And I have no more time for talking. Go speak with Kesh if you are so curious.
Returning to High Rock. Our shepherd lost his way, and I fear Peryite's wrath may consume those who remain with him.
Kesh could tell you more. I just want out of Skyrim as quickly as possible.
I am one of the Afflicted. I'd have been dead from this plague a year ago if not for Peryite's protection.
Don't mess anything up while you're here...
Good talking to you. Not many care to chat with the help.
Okay. I need to get back to the palace soon, anyway.
You can catch me at the Blue Palace later, if you want to talk again.
I guess I should be getting back to work. It was more fun talking, though.
May Arkay bless you with a long life.
May the light of truth keep the darkness at bay.
It's best you leave me be.
Sure, you make your retreat and I'll make mine.
Keep your head about you.
Just watch yourself. You don't want to meet me on the job.
Tell these Horkers to set me free.
Leave me be. I'm no harm here.
Keep my men in your prayers. They'll need them if they don't shape up.
Stay strong. The Eight willing, we'll win this war.
Sure, whatever you say. Bye.
Always good talking to you... friend.
Thanks for talking to me.
For a stranger, you're not so bad.
Blessed are those who worship, for they know true grace.
Keep the faith.
The strong and honorable always carry the blessing of the Eight.
The strong and honorable always carry the blessing of the Nine.
Your rewards in life will match the strength of your faith.
The Temple of Divines is always open for worshipers.
Take care, and go with the blessings of the Eight.
Take care, and go with the blessings of the Nine.
I really need to get back to planning my wedding..
Now if you'll excuse me. The East Empire Company has several accounts, and it's up to me to balance them.
Perhaps I'll be seeing more of you.
Please come back if you're ever in need of fletching supplies.
Good to talk to a fellow traveler.
Stop back anytime.
Thank you for helping my Aunt, earlier. I'll see you around.
People to see and places to go, huh? Later, then.
I understand, you're an important person. I'll catch up with you later.
I should make sure Svari is okay.
Addvar won't sell anything if I don't go and check up on him.
Come back to Bits and Pieces anytime.
Make sure to recommend us to your associates.
Return anytime you need arms or armor. My forge never rests.
Keep safe. And always wear your armor.
Yes, I agree with you. Have you heard any news from the other parts of Skyrim?
You speak truly. Have you heard any interesting rumors of late?
I'll not argue that. Heard any interesting gossip lately?
I couldn't agree more. By the way, have you heard any interesting rumors lately?
I couldn't have said it better myself. It's been some time since I've had news from elsewhere in Skyrim. What have you heard?
I know just what you mean. Heard anything interesting lately? Any news or gossip?
I heard Elenwen say she's going open a bottle of her best Firebrand Wine later. I can hardly wait.
These Stormcloaks are nothing more than an armed mob. This war won't last much longer.
I hope they'll serve the roast venison with bittergreen sauce that we had at the last party.
That was delicious.
This war has taken a toll on the merchants of Skyrim. Let's hope that it ends soon, for their sake.
I look forward to the day when this rebellion is put down, and we once again have peace.
The Gray Quarter in Windhelm is little better than a slum. It's shameful that the dark elves are made to live that way.
With the soldiers all fighting in the war, there's nobody to hunt down the bandits. Travel has become dangerous of late.
It's been a brutal winter. I can't remember the last time it was this cold, and for this long.
Do enjoy yourself.
I need another drink.
Goodbye, then.
Farewell.
Don't let me keep you.
Until later, then.
May wisdom light your path.
Good speaking with you.
Until next time.
Keep an eye on these Thalmor. You never know...
Come and visit me in Solitude sometime.
Very well then.
It was a pleasure, thank you.
Well, I did hear that the Argonians have been banned from living inside the walls of Windhelm.
They're only allowed on the city docks now. Most of them sleep together in one building, and they're as poor as wretches.
Have you visited Rorikstead lately? No matter how harsh the winter, the crops that grow there never falter.
Even when every other farm in the hold is barely scraping by, the harvest at Rorikstead is always bountiful.
Is it good fortune? The blessings of the Divines? Who knows?
Now that I think on it, I did hear something rather interesting the other day.
Apparently, the Red Wave has been docked in Solitude for well over a month, and with no sign of shipping out any time soon.
Some say the ship suffered damage to her hull when she struck an iceberg, and Captain Safia can't afford the repairs.
Of course, she would never admit such a thing. Captain Safia is far too proud a woman for that.
Well, there might be nothing to it, but I've heard that the feud between the Gray-Manes and the Battle-Borns in Whiterun has grown ugly.
Fralia Gray-Mane is convinced that the Battle-Borns have abducted one of her sons.
I'm not sure that it's news, but lately it seems like every blacksmith in Skyrim is out to prove that he's the equal of Eorlund Gray-Mane.
It's not really a fair comparison, if you ask me.
After all, Eorlund works the Skyforge. How is any smith going to compete with that?
Well, I did see something interesting not long ago.
There were several Khajiit camped outside the walls of Whiterun. Seems they've been traveling around the whole of Skyrim peddling their wares.
You've got to admire their courage, doing something like that with dragons on the loose and a war going on.
Make yourself useful and chop up some firewood. There's a few coins in it for you.
Honest gold for honest work.
I heard those mages up in Winterhold have been trying to figure out how many dragons there are in Skyrim.
They seem to think it's at least a dozen, but I'm sure they're exaggerating.
They're probably trying to scare us into giving them more money and influence, so they can save us from the dragons threat.
Well, I did hear that Ulfric Stormcloak was slain while defending Windhelm against a dragon, but it sounds too good to be true.
I'll wager it was Ulfric himself that started the rumor. Maybe he thought it would bolster recruitment.
How much worse is this going to get?
We need to get in there and stop this!
I'm as ready as I'm going to be.
Not until we're all ready. We have to go together.
What are we waiting for?
She... She didn't make it.
When it was clear we were going to have to fall back, she stayed behind and made sure the rest of us were all right.

Easy does it now.

Su'um ahrk morah.
Lok, Thu'um.
