I trust you don't presume on our friendship? I am a Jarl, after all. I wouldn't want to appear foolish in front of these people.
Hmm. I don't want to get involved in anything beneath my dignity as Jarl.
Oh... perhaps not, then. I don't think the Jarl of Solitude should be participating in a childish prank.
I don't know... I don't want to do anything that might offend Elenwen. There is a lot of money at stake, after all.
It's all for a good cause, old chap.
Ulfric Stormcloak? The rightful High King? That's bordering on treason, man! Stand up. Stand up, I say!
What...! Do you know who I am? I should have you taken up and flogged!
You've got a lot of nerve, suggesting something like that, to me of all people! If this was Riften, I'd have you thrown in the dungeon!
You asked for my help. Not for nothing, I hope?
I'm doing as you asked. Not for nothing, I hope?
Razelan. What is it this time?
I don't understand... did you say Ulfric Stormcloak? Fine fellow, although a bit too fond of... I mean, no, of course, I condemn him in the most...
Hmm? Did you say snakes? Where? I hate them, always slithering they way they do... what? Are they on me?
What? Of course I know who you are... I didn't mean to suggest, that is, I don't remember even saying that...
Let's hear it.
Well... let's hear it.
My ears are always open to a friend's request.
Say on, friend. What do you need?
I'm always glad to help a friend if I can. What do you need?
This is preposterous. Disrespect... insulting! I am tired of being hounded from all sides!
Madame Ambassador, you know that I would never have behaved... that is to say... well I swear I'm innocent this time!
I've got this. Do what you need to do.
I hope you're enjoying the music, sir.
I hope you're enjoying the music, ma'am.
These parties are a great way to earn a little extra money.
It's good practice. Most people barely notice what I'm playing.
I hope to get into the Bards College one day.
Well I really would like to make sure I've corrected what happened last time.
Please give me a chance to try again.
Well, it all worked out in the end, didn't it?
Good, good. It'll be worth it, I assure you.
This will be worth it.
Yes, but it needs to be kept quiet.
I may have entered into an... agreement with Enthir. He had something I needed, so I traded him something of mine.
It was a mistake, and now I want back what I gave him. It's an amulet that belonged to my family, and I never should've given it to Enthir.
But he won't deal with me. Talk to him; see what it'll take to get my amulet back.
Exactly as I said it would.
Now, are you ready to try again? I am confident it will work this time.
I need your help with something. Something private.
Oh, no it hasn't. Let's be honest.
I have a long way to go before I'm ready for the kind of advanced magic I've been attempting.
But at least I know where to start now. Thank you, you've been a great help to me.
Agreed.
And thank you for your help. You're a good friend.
Damn outsider. Why couldn't you just mind your own business?
You're no liar. Best fight I've had in years. There you are.
You ever need another blade at your side, just ask. Wouldn't mind seeing how you handle a few trolls.
Every day the threat of exile from Riften draws closer for myself and my family.
Who knows how many spies the Empire has sent into our midst already. We're at war. This isn't the time for hospitality.
Thank goodness mother is keeping a level head about everything. If my brother Saerlund had his way, we'd be flying Imperial colors by now.
Ulfric only cares about one thing... Ulfric. He's ordained himself the future High King of Skyrim and steps on anyone that gets in his way.
He's begun a rebellion against those that wish to eradicate the worship of Talos and uses it as his rallying cry.
His cause may be true, but the man is a lie... all he holds in his heart his lust for the throne.
Because I did something not a soul in this bloodstained house of war has the backbone to do... dared to speak my mind.
I dared speak of the Empire and the lies that have been spread by Ulfric, the leader of the Stormcloaks.
Now my mother's stripped me of my heritage and incarcerated me here like a common criminal and my brother has all but disowned me.
Be wary what you say around here, friend. You'll find not all take kindly to "insurrection."
Good. Wait until I start the distraction and then show me what you're made of.
Can you believe that fool had the audacity to speak of his love for the Empire in the plaza?
He expects us to drop our defenses and greet them with open arms... to dialogue with them rather than defend our homeland by spilling their blood.
He's a traitor, plain and simple. Had I been sitting on the throne, he'd be hanging from the gallows for his sympathies towards the Empire.
I know it looks bad, but we've got to figure out what happened. And if that dragon is still skulking around somewhere.
It's not an insult, just an observation.
If I were walking around with a wound, I would want you to tell me to see a doctor.
Your outfit is something of an open wound... where attire is concerned.
Not many people care, these days. The world is the worse for it.
We mostly carry herbs and alchemical ingredients, that sort of thing. Before the war, our stock was mostly used for aromatic use in homes, yes.
These days, we have more people like you coming in, looking for healing and the like.
I suppose I would, but you're the only one who gets that greeting.
It's never been an issue. People say you helped with the problems at Wolfskull Cave.
I applaud anyone who assists the court.
Oh, we do all right. Rumor is, you had a hand in the Blackblood Marauders' demise...
I'm impressed.
Radiant Raiment has the finest attire in all of Skyrim. That is how we...
Wait. You're that new bard, the one who saved the Festival. Aren't you?
That was a good bit of work.
You should look into joining. They could use every hand they can get.
But I'm not telling you anything you don't already know.
No, no. Mostly, I'm just a simple herbalist. Lami over in Morthal should be able to help you.
No, no. I'm just a simple herbalist. You'll want to see Arcadia, over in Whiterun.
It's almost all I have time for these days.
The men training here in Solitude and the troops out in the field, they all need weapons and armor.
Only when you're around. The rest of these fools I could do without.
You'd think so, wouldn't you? I'm fine. Thank you for asking.
It is when my customers waste time chatting, and not buying.
I think I would, if it weren't for my son. And my wife.
Oh, Sayma would have my hide if I even suggested I might step foot on a battlefield.
You think Stormcloaks and Legionnaires are dangerous? Ha!
Nothing at all. It's very... nice. If you want something with a little more style, however, you should come by our shop. Radiant Raiment.
We could fix you right up.
I do. So few people possess the taste and sophistication of a proper wardrobe these days.
Be sure and investigate Radiant Raiment's fine selection of clothing. We have many items you'll fancy, I'm sure.
As high priest of the Nine Divines, I bid you welcome.
We welcome you to worship the Nine Divines, freely and openly.
Blessings be with you.
Blessings of the Nine Divines upon you. How may I help?
May the Divines guide and keep you.
May you be saved by the Divines, as I once was.
I hid here in Solitude for a long time but war, war follows a man until it's done with him.
You shouldn't leave Solitude without trying our fish. Best in Skyrim.
Mother says I shouldn't talk to people...
If you want to talk about my brother, I'm not interested.
So good to see you again. How can I be of service?
No. That was an ugly affair. High King Torygg's death has turned the whole town on its head.
And between you and me? He wasn't even that good a king. All of those rambling speeches about the Empire this, and the Empire that...
We sure showed them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that.
Good job out there. Glad you were with us.
Thanks. Couldn't have done that without you.
Kind of fun to push people around sometimes, isn't it. Good work, sister.
You seem to have a knack for this kind of thing. I'll make sure people in charge know.
I figured you had it in you. Nicely done.
Try not to get carried away.
Get to it, then.
Not bad, not bad. The intensity of the hunt is special, isn't it?
Good to hear. Have your share of the pay.
But I don't want to be patient! I want my dress right now! Father promised!
It takes a while to make a dress, Dagny. You have to be patient.
I'm so angry, I can hardly stand it!
Father promised me a new dress days ago, now where is it?
Damn right it has. Demand's never been so high, at least not since I built the new mill.
Leave me out of your intrigues, Hrongar. If you have something to say to the Jarl, say it with you own tongue.
My husband owns it, barely. He'll lose it soon enough. He's behind on his loan payments, but he drinks all his money away just the same.
Now we're deep in debt, penniless and about to lose our sole source of income.
I'm not asking you to defy the Jarl, just to open his eyes.
Use subtlety and suggestion to turn his thoughts to the war. You're his steward, he'll listen to you.
No, I openly welcome the presence of armed strangers who have no reason to care about any of us.
Of course it bothers me! I'm sure it's only a matter of time until they seize our mill in the name of their war effort.
Next thing you know, there'll be more guards, and then we'll lose our homes as well. This is just the start of it, mark my words.
What would you have me do, Hrongar? Your brother is the Jarl. You know I can't question his judgement.
Proventus, you must help me. We need to convince my brother to do more for the war effort.
I'm going to tell father you said that. He'll tan your hide for sure!
All you ever do is complain about what you want. You're a spoiled baby.
This isn't an order of cowards. I'll find someone more agreeable, I'm sure.
Be cautious. Beasts are not nearly as predictable as men. Give the thing a quick death.
Good. I knew that we could count on you. It's simply a beast, but be cautious. The beasts of Skyrim are made of sterner stuff than most.
I knew we could count on you. Get it taken care of right away. Nobody wants an animal left roaming around in their place.
You're not afraid, are you? Of animals? Come back when you find your spine.
Unfortunate, but someone else will take care of it.
I see. Well, should you change your mind, the contract will be available for a while.
Orchendor? Kesh knew him. He's an Overseer. Shepherd. Gathers the Afflicted, contains the festering wound.
Orchendor and his Afflicted are meant to stand ready, awaiting Peryite's command to cover the world with his Blessing.
Not far, not far. This one looks upon it on clear days. Look to the west, at the foot of the mountain. The Dwarven ruins there. Bthardamz.
The pettiness of mortals. I had almost forgotten.
I can grant you a powerful token for this task. An escutcheon of Dwemer make, capable of shielding you from both the spell and sword.
Return when the elf lies dead and it will be yours.
An impertinent question. The elf must die. Either carry out my will or do not. You are not the only mortal agent I could choose.
So you have, mortal. Go now. Kill Orchendor.
I'm really getting tired of your excuses. When you borrowed the money, you said you'd pay it back on time and for double the usual fee.
Look, Shadr. Last warning. Pay up or else. All I care about is the gold. Everything else is your problem.
What? Are you telling me you robbed it? Why? Why are you doing this to me?
Next time, keep your plans quieter and nothing would have happened to it.
I know I did. But how was I to know the shipment would get robbed?
It is no matter, this fool will be the next act of the show.
And who is this?
I thought I was a goner just now. Thanks for the help, and if you need any supplies I have a few to sell.
Please, someone help me!
Help me! A vampire!
Morvath will taste your blood!
You will be my blood offering to Morvath!
Another victim fell for our ruse!
If you want something a bard can dig into look to the dragons.
A thousand years from now Skyrim will have changed rulers dozens of times but the return of the dragons, that story is once in an era.
I don't suppose it matters much anymore.
As long as Ulfric wishes Elisif to remain Jarl, remain Jarl she will. And the Thanes will continue to follow her orders.
As long as those orders came from Ulfric.
Well... okay. My father owns the Winking Skeever. But it's the family business, so it'll be mine when he kicks off.
My father owned it until he... passed on.
I shouldn't be. Everyone wants to ask me about mama and uncle Roggvir.
Maybe if I don't talk, they won't either.
Thank you. I'm still at the beginning of my lessons. Dean Six Fingers used to say I was quite good with the flute.
You really don't know?
He opened the gate for Ulfric Stormcloak. Ulfric rides in, defeats the High King and Roggvir was the one that let him ride right back out.
And now the Stormcloaks have claimed the entire city. I guess we have Roggvir to thank for that as well.
My husband. He was the blacksmith up at Castle Dour.
If you're looking for truth instead, see Noster. He's a good example of what life has to offer.
And if you're just looking to feel good, go get some Spiced Wine from Evette. But whatever you do, just leave me alone.
But you won't get any better than this from me.
Now that Sybille Stentor is out of the picture, I am indeed. And the court is, well... free of her... eccentricities.
Moving into her quarters was an interesting process.
Good man.
Stick around! Still plenty of songs to sing and mead to drink.
Yes. My older brother. He was a bit of a fop, really.
And truth be told, since Torygg's death, he had no real power in court. But still... He didn't deserve to die.
What are you looking at?
Leave me alone.
I've got to get out of here!
It's been easier lately.
The Blackblood Marauders haven't been seen since the Icerunner attack, so my shipments have been more regular.
I provide my wisdom and insight to the court.
It does indeed. You know, I hear you had something to do with Sybille Stentor's demise...
That must have been a hard bit of business. I hear Stentor had been alive for a long, long time.
No. Although it's true my husband Torygg was the High King and I am his widow, I am in no position to claim such a title.
Ulfric Stormcloak is victorious, and the Jarls have fallen in line.
It seems I will never take my rightful place as High Queen.
These days? Aye. Most of what I make is for folk like you.
But we must have the support of the other Jarls for that claim to have any meaning.
Should they accept her rule, we will have peace.
Because killing her would yield no political advantage.
By leaving Elisif alive, Ulfric completes the separation of the Empire and Skyrim.
With Ulfric's troops here, Elisif will be forced to support his bid for High King. He secures the position and appears above reproach.
What's more, Ulfric's refusal to execute his slain enemy's wife will be portrayed as the ultimate act of compassion. Hmph. It's genius, really.
That's the real question, isn't it? No one can deny Elisif has a legitimate claim to become High Queen of Skyrim...
Well... By rights, she had a legitimate claim as High Queen of Skyrim.
But now Skyrim is at Ulfric's mercy.
The moot will meet and choose Ulfric as High King. With his troops in most of the cities, they have little choice.
Perhaps, but necessary. As you know my cousin, Fura, died out there fighting for us. She wouldn't have had to, if it weren't for Roggvir.
Not much. But as a bard, I find the whole affair depressing.
There are no heroes in this war. Even with Ulfric here in Solitude, there are no winners to be had. No real conclusion.
You won't get anything out of me, you filthy sons o' horkers.
There's still Imperial camps to be cleaned up, as you know. And that takes arms and armor.
I swear, I left another bottle right here...
I got some fine armor, if you're lookin'.
Wanderer like you must have plenty of tales to tell. But I'm afraid I'm too busy to hear 'em.
Papa used to say I was greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him. I miss him...
So, what are your needs? Perhaps a pinch of this, and a bit of that? Hmm?
If you're looking for potions, I think I can be of service.
Beirand said you got here just before the execution. Must have been a lovely first sight.
Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand did. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything.
Welcome. Look around. We've got everything you could want in life. At least... that's what my Beirand always told me...
I thought adventurers were supposed to look tough?
I bet my papa could have beat you up. He was stronger than anyone.
I get to help mother with the shop. I used to help papa too... 'till I tried to use the forge...
I can help with purchases, but if you came to talk, see my sister. She's the one who gets on with people. It's her one virtue, really.
Have a drink, rest your feet and stay a little while.
I'm just running some errands for the old man... Err... For myself. I'm just running some errands for myself...
You don't want to talk to me. I'm just a stand in for a dead Thane.
You get around, right? If you come across any Stormcloaks... gut one for me. They did the same to my brother in Whiterun.
Think I'll forge me a new sword, just in case things go awry here in Solitude. I'm the blacksmith's apprentice, you see.
We've got strong swords and sturdy armor, if you're looking to buy.
Blacksmith, at your service.
Leave me be, Imperial. I haven't got anything to say to you.
It's only a matter of time before Ahtar's blade finds my neck...
You wouldn't know it, but before my family got killed? I was the luckiest man in Skyrim.
Is this about the stables? With my husband gone, my son Horm handles the business.
Need a horse? I guess I'm the one to talk to, since my da passed...
Any day is a good one, long as I don't have to go into town. But with Hjorunn gone...
They were a group of mages with a history that pre-dates the Empire. Very powerful, very secretive.
No one's seen them in well over a hundred years. They vanished, along with their sanctuary on the Isle of Artaeum.
I have no idea what connection they'd have to this place.
You will?! Oh, thank you!
I was able to work out a deal with the stables in Whiterun to sell me some of their tack and harnesses.
I borrowed some gold from Sapphire to pay for the shipment, but it got robbed before it even arrived.
Now Sapphire wants her money back, and if I don't pay her, I think she's going to kill me.
I owe a great deal of money to someone and I think they cheated me. I don't know what to do.
Yes. Hofgrir took me in when I arrived in Riften about a year ago. He's been teaching me everything he knows.
Horses are my whole life. Back in Hammerfell, I helped my family raise horses at our farm.
I hope to open my own stables one day, but I think it's going to take a lot longer than I expected.
I don't have any business with you, so get out of my face.
I can't talk right now.
Not half as sorry as I am.
If it's horses you want, you'll want to speak to Hofgrir. I'm afraid I'm in too much trouble to help you.
If it's horses you want, I'm not sure I can help you. I have too much on my mind as it is.
If it's horses you want, you're talking to the right person.
Follow me. I will show you how to open the path to Paarthurnax.
Paarthurnax is the only one who can answer your questions about Dragonrend.
The Words wait.
Use Clear Skies to open the way to Paarthurnax.
Ah, I see. Very well, my friend. I'll do it.
Oh... all right. If it's so important to you, I suppose I can help.
Very well. I suppose there's nothing wrong with a bit of harmless fun.
This is neither the time nor place for such things. I'm surprised you would ask it of me.
Finally, we have an agreement.
I look forward to having it back soon. The sooner, the better, in fact.
Do you see that?
It's an entirely different situation. I don't expect you to comprehend the nuances of these matters.
Regardless, I'm making you a very simple offer. You get me the staff, and I give you Onmund's precious amulet. Do we have a deal?
I told you, it's from my family.
I don't... I know they always disapproved of what I wanted in life, to learn magic instead becoming a farmer, or a hunter.
But no matter how poorly they may have treated me, they're still my family. I didn't consider that until after I'd made the trade.
Well, yes. I was completely unaware of the reputation of the other party in this trade.
I don't want that coming back to haunt me.
Ah, excellent. Well, here's the gold the Jarl offered in exchange for taking care of the problem.
Ah, excellent. Well, it's good to know that won't be a problem any longer.
It doesn't matter. It's also none of your concern.
Just talk to him; see if you can convince him to give my amulet back.
You're not very good at this, are you.
Look, I traded a staff to someone and found out later that was a bad idea. So, I'd like the staff back. Understand now?
Let's pretend for a moment that a certain individual was looking to acquire a particular staff.
Let's also pretend that he traded some valuable items for said staff.
And it was only later that it was realized that this individual might... misuse such a staff.
The resulting disaster would reflect quite poorly on everyone involved. Are you following me?
Look how persistent you are. It's charming, really.
Onmund gave up the first time I said no. But not you...
All right. I'll tell you a little story.
It would seem there's some sort of disturbance. From what few details we were given, it sounds quite familiar.
I suspect that the power released by the Eye of Magnus was not fully contained, and has coalesced in this part of Skyrim.
I do hope you still have the Staff of Magnus someplace. I suspect you're going to need it.
There's another report of a disturbance in this Hold. Best if it's taken care of before things get out of hand.
There's another report of a disturbance in that Hold. Best if it's taken care of before things get out of hand.
Actually, yes Arch-Mage. I believe there is.
No, no. You're not understanding this.
Onmund made a deal. He made that choice, and now he has to live with it.
Ha! I didn't think he'd really give it back.
Thank you, friend. It's good to know I can count on you.
I wonder... what effect might your spells have?
Pleasure doing business with you.
Oh, I suppose. But you keep this to yourself; I don't want it being known that I go back on my trades. Understand?
Yes, you see, the thing is... I'm not concerned with "everyone's best interest." I find I'm only concerned with my own.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Solitude.
We've become aware of a problem in Winterhold.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Riften.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Whiterun.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Morthal.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Markarth.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Windhelm.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Dawnstar.
We've been contacted by the Jarl of Falkreath.
Oh, good. I was really afraid I'd lose it forever.
Some kind of resonance... you and the wall. It must be connected to the amulet!
Oh my. How precious.
Onmund is too afraid to deal with me himself, and so he's sent you.
I'll make this very simple for you. All my trades are final. Onmund knew this ahead of time, and went through with it anyway.
So there's nothing more to be said.
I've already made it very clear that all of my trades are final.
Are you daft? This isn't the Dark Brotherhood! We don't go around murdering anyone, ever. It just isn't how things are done.
I guess I shouldn't be surprised. Nothing around here has been going well lately.
They live below the streets in the Ratway like dirty animals. And trust me, they're no better than the vermin that slink out of there for scraps.
They make it difficult for decent people to earn a living. Of course, not many decent people live here in the first place.
It's Riften's name for the sewers beneath our feet. Absolutely disgusting!
If you fancy keeping your purse filled with gold and your head on your shoulders, I'd stay out of those awful tunnels.
I may be dark elf by birth, but I was raised Argonian.
Through reasons I'm still trying to discover, I ended up orphaned then taken in by a kindly Argonian family in Black Marsh.
I hope one day to find out what happened to me... how I ended up like that.
It is? Well that's different.
If you're planning violence against me or mine, you'll regret it.
What do you want with us?
Please! Have mercy... I beg you...
Perhaps it's because there's no better way to face the cold Skyrim nights than with a belly full of ale?
Oh, there's a better way.
A buxom barmaid between the sheets will keeps me plenty warm.
Better yet, she won't make my head ring the morning after.
In the very oldest tales, back from when there still were dragons in Skyrim, the Dragonborn would slay dragons and steal their power.
That's what you did, isn't it? Absorbed that dragon's power?
Of a sort. I don't have formal training in the Restoration school, but... something just as good.
Within these walls, I have all that I need to brew a potion for nearly any ailment.
If I had a Septim for every case of Rockjoint or Witbane I've cured since I opened this shop, I'd be a rich woman indeed.
Adrianne. At her forge, just outside.
Place is named after her. "Warmaiden's." It's a childhood nickname, given by her father. What I hear, she was playing with swords even back then.
Anyway, our wares are strong and the prices fair. What else matters?
Oh, yes. Hunting is my great passion.
That's why my brother and I opened a shop to sell hunting supplies. It's the Drunken Huntsman, down by the main gate.
He tends the store, and I've got this stall.
Yes, I hear that question often.
My brother Anoriath and I came up with it one night after consuming... rather too much mead.
After our drinks, we went out for a moonlight hunt. We got separated, and in his drunken state my brother mistook me for a deer.
He shot an arrow that pierced my... well, my rump. After such a memorable adventure, we knew we had the name for our shop.
If my cleanin' gets in your way, just say the word an' I'll be off.
Ain't nobody high and mighty in these halls, exceptin' the Jarl. And don't you forget!
This here is Dragonsreach, an' it's my task to see it looks fit for a jarl, night an' day.
My poor Fralia thinks Thorald's still alive, but I know better.
We should keep moving. Still the draugr to worry about.
Now let's move. I think that was the last of the Silver Hand, but still plenty of draugr for the both of us.
This looks like the guardian chamber. The key to that door has to be around here somewhere.
"Every door has a key," says Vilkas. Sometimes he is wrong, but look around anyway.
Good that you're so eager.
Good that you're so eager.
I've been hearing rumors of Silver Hand activity.
I will take you down too. I am not splitting this horde with anyone.
You will pay for stealing from me you eyeless freak!
Let's go on ahead. See if the way is clear.
I better stay back and see to the old man. Good luck you two.
All right, let's see if we can find a way out.
I'll keep watch in case Ulfric comes through here. Talos guide the both of you.
What are we waiting for?
Still you persist? Very well.
Enough!
The staff! Use it on the Eye!
I am beyond your pathetic attempts at magic. You cannot touch me.
I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.
Spells have no effect!
I'll be right behind you!
Seems your purse is lighter than you thought.
Hmph.
Now, now! That won't be necessary.
I can't believe it! You're... Dragonborn...
Yes. I think I am. You can Shout now. That can only mean one thing. You must be Dragonborn.
There's one way to find out. Try to Shout...
According to the old legends, only the Dragonborn can Shout without training, the way the dragons do.
Well, you can Shout now. You couldn't before, right? That can only mean one thing. You must be Dragonborn.
There's one way to find out. Try to Shout... that would prove it.
According to the old legends, only the Dragonborn can Shout without training, the way the dragons do.
Now let us see how quickly you can master your new Thu'um.
Jump! Come on!
Get going!
Through the inn! Now!
You still here? Go!
Go! Now!
Get moving!
I do hope the court likes the verse. I think we've done an excellent job of recreating it.
Gods. A woman attacked right on the streets.
Are you all right? Did you see what happened?
Greetings, Shield-Brother.
Greetings, Shield-Sister.
Well done, mortal. All things are in their order, and Orchendor roams the Pits. His betrayal will be punished, and your obedience is rewarded.
This one would try a shop. You might prefer the direct route, hm?
Deathbell grow in the frozen tundra. You might nip into a mine for the ruby and silver. Vampire Dust is tricky. Vampires, you know.
And yet you have done my bidding. Why, I wonder?
The vapors are waning, but not yet gone. Take a breath. Peryite will speak if He sees fit.
Did you leave any alive? The Afflicted are mere vessels for my Blessing. It will spread to others through their touch and my own.
Another Overseer will replace Orchendor, when the time comes. For now all is cleansed and ordered.
Go, seek your fate. I will be watching, and perhaps we will meet again - afterwards.
Embrace order and hard truth, mortal. Good bye.
Ah, no. Don't think you've got the stuff. Come back when you've seen a bit more in life, perhaps.
Ro...
I think Giraud here would give you the best explanation of both it and the history of King Olaf's Verse. You should speak to him about it.
Get the coat. Then we'll talk.
Nice to meet the person who went to King Olaf's Tomb.
He's sending you after King Olaf's Verse then? That's good, we shouldn't leave it lying around now that I've figured out where it is.
The Verse was Svaknir's contribution to the Poetic Edda, the living history of Skyrim. Each bard adds to the Edda in his or her time.
And a very ancient one.
The verse criticized the reigning King Olaf. He was so incensed the bard was put to death and all the copies burned.
At least, that's what we thought until I translated some ancient texts a year or so ago. We now believe King Olaf buried the truth with the bard.
If I'm right Svaknir and King Olaf's Verse lie in Dead Men's Respite, along with the burial chamber of King Olaf himself.
Not at all. That was as much a test of your abilities as it was your resolve.
I wasn't sure your heart was in it. But you've proven me wrong. And I respect that.
Now, if were done being sentimental, it's time for you to take the Oath.
True, he's a dangerous and blood thirsty man, but he's also a Nord that honors our traditions.
Keep your wits about you and you won't be harmed.
And then get back here. Because if Ulfric isn't bluffing, I'll need every able body to defend Whiterun.
Only the ones I'm not sure about. This will prove your abilities, but more importantly, it will prove your commitment.
"...and unwavering obedience to the officers of his great Empire."
My father, the great Bear of Eastmarch, died during my imprisonment after the Markarth Incident.
I, his only son, forced to deliver his eulogy via a letter I had smuggled out of prison. Such is the love of Titus Mede for his subjects.
When finally set free, I returned to Windhelm, and was greeted by a city in mourning, at one with my own grief and anger.
Clamoring in angry voices, calling out for justice, for war, they sat me on the throne.
The Throne of Ysgramor! The throne of my father...  I only hope I can prove worthy of the honor.
Indeed, Elisif has become Jarl of Solitude, historically and conveniently home of the High King, backed by Imperial interests.
But the Moot has not yet met to name her High Queen. And they won't. Not as long as I have any say in it.
I'm originally from Cyrodiil, sent here at the request of the Emperor himself. I'm often ordered to places with problems that need fixing.
Now, while I'm certain this is all terribly interesting to you, I have important matters to attend.
I'm sorry to hear that. If you change your mind, speak with Galmar. What does bring you to me?
There hasn't been a true High King in Skyrim for generations.
For too long he's been hand-picked by the Emperor, and given emphatic nods by milk-drinking Jarls addicted to Imperial coin.
It's time we had a real king. One of our own making.
Now, that's it. One free outfit per soldier. You lose that, or get it banged up, you'll need to pay like everyone else.
The Legion's always been here. Without us to keep order, the provinces would fall into barbarism and lawlessness. Especially Skyrim.
Take for example, Ulfric Stormcloak and his little "rebellion." But rest assured, his days are numbered.
Good.
I challenged him in the traditional way, and he accepted. There were many witnesses. No "murder" was committed.
True, he didn't stand a chance against me.
But that was precisely the point! He was a puppet-king of the Empire , not a High King of Skyrim.
His father before him perhaps, but not Torygg.
He was too privileged and too foolish, more interested in entertaining his queen than ruling his country.
They don't make finer steel anywhere else. If this doesn't keep you from dying, nothing will.
I see. Then there's nothing further to discuss. If you change your mind, speak with the Legate.
I killed Torygg to prove our wretched condition. How is the High King supposed to be the defender of Skyrim, if he can't even defend himself?
Alright. This here's enough to keep your insides from spilling out, except from the fiercest of blows, and shouldn't weigh you down too much.
Good.
Don't wait for me. Get to Korvanjund as soon as you're ready. I'll meet you there.
Alright... here you go. Should keep you light on your feet, and hold up decent enough if you wind up trading blows with the enemy.
One moment. But don't go far. I expect I'll need you.
I've made up my mind, even if my advisors are still squabbling. I'm sending you to Windhelm.
Well, then. Repeat after me.
"Upon my honor I do swear undying loyalty to the Emperor..."
I remember you... You were at Helgen with us! Come to join the war? Speak with Galmar. He handles the new recruits.
Light armor for scouting detail, heavy armor for full on combat, or something in between?
I remember you, you were at Helgen. Speak to Legate Rikke. I suspect we could use someone like you.
Ulfric Stromcloak's head rolling away from his lifeless body.
I hope Ulfric will come to his senses. But I fear he's in too far to stop now.
Likely only Ulfric's death, or the overthrow of the Jarls supporting him will end this. Either way, much blood will be spilt.
First we'll kick the Thalmor and their bloody Imperial puppets out of the country. Then we'll rebuild Skyrim into the land she once was.
When we are done with that, we will take our army to the Dominion, and show those pointy-eared bastards not every man is fit to be their slave.
We're fighting because we're done bleeding for an Empire that won't bleed for us.
Untold numbers of Nords died defending the Empire against the Dominion. And for what?
Skyrim being sold to the Thalmor so the Emperor could keep his throne!
We're fighting because our own Jarls, once strong, wise men, have become fearful and blind to their people's suffering.
We're fighting because Skyrim needs heroes, and there's no one else but us.
Yep. I have just one question for you.
Light, medium, or heavy?
Not to mention our pay.
Of course.
Sure.
Properly controlled, it has knowledge enough to turn paupers into kings.
Besides, you're not scared of a few sleeping metal men. Are you, Drennen?
What are you waiting for? Get in there and find that mask.
Don't bother me if you haven't found anything about the mask.
It seems that after the strength of the Cult was broken in the Dragon War a last contingent came here to create a stronghold and regroup.
King Harald's forces crushed stragglers here the during the First Era and it has been haunted ever since, but the specifics of the siege are unclear.
They were among the first men to arrive from Atmora and they worshipped certain animals as avatars of the Gods; Dragons being foremost.
And for their bloody sacrifices, the Dragons gifted their priests with terrible power.
But their cruelty was too great for men to bear, and they rose up in what would be called the Dragon War and the Cult swept from the land.
You there, hold! If you are a true friend to Skyrim, you will help me in my mission.
Are you going to help me with my mission? I need someone very capable.
Isn't it obvious? Why, the wonderful weather and hospitable people, of course!
Not to mention my great fondness for dragons and petty political power struggles.
Ah, but without a doubt, the most compelling feature of this frozen wasteland is the volley of inane questions leveled at me on a regular basis.
Interesting, to say the least. What with all that's been going on.
On the one hand, supplies are harder to come by, since fewer traders are traveling the roads these days.
But on the other, the scarcity of supplies has led to some, well, "elevated" prices.
All that matters is that I have what you need. And it's worth every septim.
That'll be the Jarl, Balgruuf the Greater. He's an honorable enough man. Never done wrong by me or my kin.
Things are tough for him now, I'd wager. What with the fighting, and the dragons. But Balgruuf's a tough old Nord. He can handle it.
Of course. Every night I pray that we don't get a visit from a dragon, or that two armies don't show up to fight over the bridge.
You ask if I'm worried? I'm terrified, if you want to truth of it. But I have to stay strong for my wife and children.
Why, because I'm a proud citizen of the Empire? Because the Stormcloaks only want Nords in Skyrim?
The Stormcloaks can rot in Oblivion for all I care. I've lived in this city for twenty years. Twenty!
Both the Imperials and Stormcloaks have sent riders this way, scouting the bridge and the lands nearby.
They stayed at the inn, but didn't pay. They said soldiers don't need to pay because they're risking their lives to protect us.
And that's not all. They took a heap of our lumber, and one tried to have his way with my daughter. They think they can do whatever they want.
Try Hulda, the innkeeper at the Bannered Mare. It's just up the road, near the market.
You should check in at Dragonsreach, too. There's so much going on these days, the Jarl or his Steward might have need of help.
I grew up an orphan on the streets of Markarth. I'd have died there, too, but for the kindness of an old warrior named Logrolf the Bent.
Logrolf rescued me and gave me enough money to get out of the city. I followed the Karth river north, and came here.
When I met Michel, I knew this was where I belonged. I suppose that's it, really.
I can tell you're new around here, askin' a question like that.
My husband Eorlund tends the Skyforge up at Jorrvaskr, the Companions mead hall. There's no finer smith in the nine holds.
You should ask him about his work. It's the only subject that'll get him talking.
Well, that and maybe those stuck-up, dung-sniffing Battle-Borns.
You've got no idea. Half the men in Whiterun have proposed to me. Some were even single.
They'll never understand. No amount of flowers or honeyed words are going to change my mind.
Right now, all that matters is my daughter Mila. No man's going to get between me and my little girl.
Hard to say. It's ancient, that much we know for sure. It was here long before the town itself.
I just hope the bridge survives the war. It's the only place you can cross the Karth river for a long way in either direction.
Could be if one side wants to keep the other from coming across, they might try to knock it down.
Her name's Olda. If you've met her, then you probably already know what I'm talking about.
I swear that woman is the most spiteful shrew in all Skyrim. I think there's a chunk of ice where her heart ought to be.
Master Wulfgar will demonstrate Whirlwind Sprint. Then it will be your turn.
You must hear the Word within yourself before you can project it into a Thu'um.
Wuld...
Now it is your turn.
Use both words, if you can.
Use both Fus and Ro. Together, they will make the Shout more powerful.
Master Borri.
Master Borri will teach you the first Word of a new Shout. Attend to him.
Must have come from Mercer's place. He'd admired the Gray Fox for some time. Sure, I'll buy it from you... here you are.
Are you serious? The war is tearing the land apart, the dragons have returned seeking who knows what... Skyrim is going nowhere fast.
If I were you, I'd pack up and get out of here before you end up in one of those burial cairns.
It's worth quite a bit actually. Quite a catch! Here you go, this should make you smile.
Look, I've moved all over Skyrim selling these bits of junk and I'm barely scraping by.
I'm not out to win friends and I could care less if you're happy about your purchase or not. I just need the coin.
The sooner I get out of Skyrim the better.
By the Eight... you actually got your hands on it! This alone is worth more than some thieves earn in a lifetime.
What a delightful find! This should cover it.
This should compensate you for your find.
There are those that believe the Jarl does nothing for this city... that it's corrupt and run by the Black-Briar family.
I can assure you that this is entirely false. We are fully in control of everything.
You going to buy something or just here for training? Otherwise, move on.
You gonna stare all day or buy some armor?
Can't you bother me at my armor stand in the market? Or are you that desperate for training?
Come on, come on. The sooner you buy, the sooner I can get drunk.
Maven Black-Briar is the owner of the largest and most profitable business in all Skyrim, the Black-Briar Meadery.
We're pleased to have her in Riften. She brings stability, opportunities for employment and strategic value to our city.
I can't do this. We shouldn't be here. I'm sorry.
A few hours sleep and we should be ready to continue through.
Wait!
Best get rid of the ones we see. Just in case.
Ah, Karliah. I was wondering when I'd hear from you again. Lose something did we?
Before we depart, Brynjolf has some business to discuss. I suggest you listen to him.
If the Key isn't returned to its lock in the Twilight Sepulcher, things will never be the same for the Guild.
As time passed, our luck would diminish to the point of non-existence. And whether you know it or not, our uncanny luck defines our trade.
If you wish to talk, I'll be here for a bit.
Proceed down the hall to the gate when you're ready.
This way, please.
Please hurry, we need to catch Mercer before he leaves Skyrim.
Revenge? How interesting...
Very well, the conditions are acceptable. You may proceed.
My appetite for Mercer's demise exceeds my craving for wealth, Your Grace.
Very well. I name your initiates Nightingale and I restore your status to the same, Karliah.
And in the future, I'd suggest you refrain from disappointing me again.
You surprise me, Karliah. This offer is definitely weighted in my favor.
You're already mine, Karliah. Your terms were struck long ago. What could you possibly offer me now?
You appear ready for the Oath.
You appear hesitant to don your Nightingale Armor. What's troubling you?
It's just ahead. Please, keep following.
I'm preparing to leave for the standing stone. What is it?
You have returned with the gauntlets! Enter. Speak to our chief right away.
Return with the gauntlets, or not at all.
Most of my kin are in the longhouse. Come back during the day if you wish to trade.
No! You will not deny me this!
You cannot take this away from me!
No! The Eye is mine!
Well, I know the Companions make their home here. Their mead hall, Jorrvaskr, is the oldest building in the city.
Oh, and there's a nasty feud between two families called the Gray-Manes and Battle-Borns. You'll want to be careful there.
The rest you can find out by asking the townsfolk. I'd start with the barkeep at the Bannered Mare, or the castle steward.
Father says I should pick a name that describes what I really am,  but nobody's going to hire Erik Greenthumb or Erik Hoe-pusher.
No, I need a name that will put some fear in the hearts of my enemies. When I have some enemies, that is.
Another time, or not at all. It's really none of your business, after all.
None of 'em's older than nine, and the youngest is six.
Now, the horse I got for sale is seven years old, but in fine health. She's got a lot of spirit, that one.
We've taken to calling her Queen Alfsigr, or just Allie for short. I expect you can name her anything you like once she's yours.
Go ahead, hit me as often as you want. It's not going to change anything, other than to make me and the town guard angry.
Exploiting? What's that supposed to mean? Erik works the farm because he's a good lad!
I'm not forcing him to stay here. I know that he wants a life of adventure, but I've told him before that I can't afford to buy him armor.
Perhaps you should let a father decide what is best for his own son.
You can get a room at the Bee and Barb, but mind your coin purse -- it's no secret the Thieves' Guild makes its home here.
Oh, and if you meet Maven Black-Briar, stay on her good side. This is her city, and nobody else's.
My carriage is the safest way to travel.
Solitude's been the capital of Skyrim as long as I can remember. Most of the High Kings of Skyrim were Jarls of Solitude first.
Ah, let's see, what else... The Bards College is here, and the main office of the East Empire Company.
If you need it, you can get a room for a fair price at the Winking Skeever, just inside the main gate.
I appreciate the gesture, but I'd rather not be in debt to a stranger.
It's an old city, I can tell you that. The Palace of the Kings is ancient.
It's not the friendliest place for outsiders. The Dark Elves were all forced to live in a slum called the Gray Quarter.
The Argonians can't even live inside the walls. They're all stuck out on the docks.
Still, Candlehearth Hall's got decent food and cheap bunks. You could do worse.
Well, you were deep in your cups when you got here.
You were ranting but most of it was slurred. You said something about Rorikstead.
All right, all right. You're mean when you're sober.
You said you the ceremony was going to be in Morvunskar. You said your friend Sam was going to be your best man.
You think you can bully me. I grew up with five brothers. Go... get...my...ring.
Okay, okay. Most of what you said didn't make sense but you left a note. The only bit that I could read said "after repaying Ysolda in Whiterun."
That's all I know.
Do what you have to do, I'm not giving up on Gleda.
Well, we can always use contributions.
You were ranting when you got here  but most of it was slurred. You did say something about Rorikstead . Maybe you should take a look there.
Oh you must be nobility of some sort. Running around partying and paying others to clean it up.
I don't think so. Clean up your own mess.
You must have had a wild night if you can't remember that.
But I suppose everyone gets the jitters before their wedding. And I did say you could take a bit of time paying me back.
You said the ceremony was going to be at Morvunskar. Don't forget you still owe me!
I'm sure your bride-to-be will tell you when you where the ceremony will be.
If she still wants to marry you when you tell her you can't afford the ring.
Fine, but I'll still be missing my Gleda. Doubt I'll ever have a prize winning goat again.
Most of what you said was gibberish but you left a note. The only bit I could read was "after repaying Ysolda in Whiterun."
There's not enough money in the world to replace Gleda.
I suppose that makes sense. You didn't mention a Sam and nothing you said about the staff made sense.
You left a note but it was mostly gibberish, the only bit I could make out was "after repaying Ysolda in Whiterun."
You didn't seem to need either to sell Gleda to the Giant in the first place. I think you'll figure it out.
Decided to go through with it then? I knew you couldn't have forgotten about your fiance. You spoke of her so glowingly.
I don't know much about Morvunskar but it sounded like a lovely place for the ceremony. Congratulations.
I think I would prefer my ring back. I put a lot of time into making it and you just gave is away as a joke.
I'll do anything if it means saving my brother. You'll join us, won't you?
Thorald can't be left to those... monsters.
