There you are.
Are you ready to go?
Let me know. We shouldn't stay here all day. My men want their mead.
This thing from Saarthal. What does it do? J'zargo cannot say.
We shouldn't have been disturbing Saarthal, no matter what you found down there.
At least something good came out of that trip to Saarthal. I didn't find anything but dust.
I owe Thorald my life. Helping to save him is the least I can do.
No one should ever be left to the Thalmor.
I pledged to aid Avulstein and Thorald a long time ago. There's nothing that could keep me from helping them now.
Not well I'm afraid. That Brynjolf... he keeps draining the people's pockets with his ridiculous miracle cures.
A few months ago it was troll fat salve and now he's got something new.
Not that there's anything that can be done about it. He's in good with the Thieves Guild.
Maven Black-Briar wants me to keep the mead flowing and that's what I aim to do despite all these lazy good-for-nothings that work for us.
Ever since I took over as the foreman the production of our mead has never been more profitable.
And if the meadery is profitable, I get rich. Get the picture?
Indeed. There have been multiple attempts on the Jarl's life. We're not certain if it's the Dark Brotherhood or simply Imperial sympathizers.
We've also had run-ins with spies attempting to probe our security for weaknesses. I work with the city guard to make certain they fail.
At the end of the day, I'm the last line of defense for the Jarl. I will not allow her to fall.
While I feel Ulfric's cause is just, my concern is for the people of The Rift.
How can they continue to lead their already meager lives with dark clouds looming overhead?
My heart goes out to them. If only our coffers were deeper, I could protect them as they were my own family.
I've been adventuring across Tamriel since I was a fresh-faced young woman barely able to swing a blade.
My travels have taken me from High Rock to Valenwood, Elsweyr to Morrowind and all points in between.
The moisture in the air is better for the ingredients. It isn't every day that you get your hands on fresh nirnroot or painted troll fat.
It also makes it easier to gather water when the canal is right out the front door.
Besides, I also prefer the quiet. I didn't open this business for browsing. Come in, buy what you need and get out.
Talos is part of our culture. He's part of our heritage and our tradition. He's Skyrim personified.
To suddenly have all that wrenched away is like losing someone you hold dearly in your heart.
The Empire already took my daughter from us, but by Talos they will not take our homeland!
People say I'm making gold off of other people's misery, but what else would I do? Smithing is all I know.
Next time one of my shields deflects a killing blow, they'll change their tune.
Lady Maven could buy or sell anyone in Skyrim. I wouldn't be surprised if she's the one pulling the strings around this city.
She's always welcome in Mistveil Keep and between you and me, I'm pretty sure she even has some pretty influential friends within the Empire.
I'd also warn you not to cross her. She employs quite a few... troubleshooters  to take care of messy problems.
Been stuck with the name for years.
I've been ridin' horses since I was a lad... nothin' I liked more. But as I got bigger, so did my mounts.
Then one day, I was drunker than I've ever been and mounted a poor colt. Broke his back in two. Haven't ridden since.
I'd hardly call them a Guild. More like unorganized rabble.
In fact, I wouldn't say they were a threat at all. No, our resources should be spent on preparations for invasion.
We're all eager to see them brought to justice. Maven Black-Briar has assured me that they're being dealt with appropriately.
As one of our city's most influential citizens, she's has taken it upon herself to oversee their incarceration.
Sadly, they're proving elusive but I have confidence that Maven won't give up until this city has been rid of them all.
Liars and bastards. Every one of them. I'd have their heads on a pike if it wasn't for the war effort.
All I need is a dozen men and we could march into the Ratway and burn them out like rodents.
Stormcloaks won't have it though. Too busy keeping the Imperial forces at bay.
They're vermin... garbage. They're exactly what makes this city such a horrible place to live.
How appropriate they should live in the Ratway with the rest of the trash.
Of course! I'd be more than happy to add a donation to our charity box.
Right now, the best you could provide is coin. We need all the help we can get.
Yes, I suppose someone has to be around here. Have you seen the condition of this sorry excuse for a city?
Beggars roam the streets, crime runs rampant and the threat of war is driving away business.
The paltry amount of coin that I place in the charity box at the temple is the least I can do to help.
He's the protector of Man, the Dragon of the North, the patron of all Nords.
And despite the Imperials and their treaty, he will always be one of the Nine Divines.
A devout Nord would rather fall in battle and have his blood spill upon the earth than allow the Empire to strike his name from the divinity.
Surely you're aware of the Great War between the Imperials and the Elves who called themselves the Aldmeri Dominion?
When the smoke cleared, the White-Gold Concordat was signed... it was supposedly a treaty aimed at establishing peace within the Empire.
One of the terms of the treaty was the outlawing of worship to Talos.
To the Stormcloaks, this was viewed as the moment when the Empire became unworthy of the allegiance of any true Nord.
As you wish. Whatever you can afford will certainly be put to good use.
The Black-Briars have been instrumental in keeping Riften on the map.
Before the meadery was opened almost fifty years ago, this town was nothing... a simple waypoint for those journeying on to Morrowind.
Now, thanks to my mother's generosity, this city is making its mark.
When speaking of the Black-Briars, only one thought should spring to mind. We are not to be trifled with.
Help us, you end up rich. Cross us and you'll end up a memory. Is that enough information for you?
Hmph. My family. All that wealth and they squander it on foolish ventures and political schemes.
I was meant for so much more than all of this... I wish to pledge my life and ply my talents in darker circles.
If only my mother would let me, I would make her proud.
I haven't seen so much celebrating in years. Mercer's dead, we have a new Guild Master and things are finally looking up.
Business is booming, coin is flowing and everyone seems content as things keep going our way.
A new chapter in the Thieves Guild is being written this day and we all owe it to you. Many thanks.
I haven't seen so much celebrating in years. Mercer's dead, we have a new Guild Master and things are finally looking up.
I only hope we can restore the Guild to its former glory after all the damage that's been done.
So, word has it you and Brynjolf know where Mercer's hiding. Good.
Make sure you make him pay for what he did to Gallus... heck, for what he did to all of us.
We're all behind you, friend. Do your best.
The word is that Mercer Frey is a dead man. The Guild wants his blood.
I can't say I blame them. Murder is not our way. Gallus was before my time, but I still can't believe it.
You need to teach him a lesson... show him the penalty for betrayal around here.
Look, I like you, so I'll tell it to you straight. Brynjolf and the rest are in the Guild, and they're ready to cut you down.
I know you have a damn good reason for siding with Karliah, so I hope you can give it to them.
Everyone's been worried since you and Mercer haven't shown up in a while. Brynjolf even went out to find you.
Delvin and Vex have been running things for now.
I hope this all gets straightened out soon or I'm headed on the first caravan out of Riften.
Glad to hear Gulum-Ei got what he deserved. That lizard owes me big for a bad shipment of Shadowbanish Wine he tried to pass off on me.
Mercer's been storming around waiting for you to come back. I think he's finally figured out who's been messing with the Guild.
Never seen the guy so angry. Better help him out any way you can.
Maven was down here right before you got back and she was all smiles. Haven't seen her like that in a long time.
Whatever you did at Honningbrew, you're now on her good side. Best of all, if she's happy, Mercer's happy.
Keep it up and you'll go places.
Well, you did something Vex couldn't do at Goldenglow, so you're looking pretty damn good.
Word is that you found something while you were out there though... something that could be a problem for the Guild.
Just watch your back and do what Mercer says... he'll sort it out.
I don't know about the others, but I sure am glad to see a fresh face down here.
The Flagon used to be packed every night with the boys from the Guild but now look at it.
Last few years have been pretty bad. I've almost closed this place up.
I don't know you so we've got nothing to talk about. How about you finish the job Brynjolf gave you, then we can talk.
Sure. How about you mind your own business about it, and I won't have to get Dirge to toss you into the cistern?
Disgusting. Ruined sewers filled with goodness knows what.
There's an entrance down by the canal, but I'd highly advise you stay out of there... its the Thieves Guild's territory.
Everyone knows the Thieves Guild uses the old sewer system beneath Riften as their hideout.
I'd go down there myself, but that would leave Jarl Laila unprotected.
Thank you! I can promise you that this will be put to good use.
With the Stormcloaks at his back, Ulfric's poised to rid Skyrim of the Empire's forces and invalidate our involvement with the White-Gold Concordat.
Between you and I, I think his motivations are a bit more self-serving. He uses this holy war as leverage in order to pursue the throne.
If he were to be crowned High King, I'm not so certain it would be the golden age his followers expect.
With the Stormcloaks at his back, Ulfric's poised to rid Skyrim of the Empire's forces and invalidate our involvement with the White-Gold Concordat.
Many have died taking up arms for this cause. I fear that the land will be stained with blood for years to come.
Wealth is my business. Help me out and I can add to yours. Would you like a taste?
There are ruined sewer tunnels beneath the city. The only way I know how to get in there is through a door off of the canal.
If I were you, I'd keep my feet dry and stay up here.
I'm going to make you sorry for this!
Come on, give it your best shot!
How could you possibly know about...
Please. My family means too much to me. Don't hurt them.
Imperial business. Be on your way.
Citizen. I'm warning you. Your presence is interfering with Imperial business. Be gone!
Citizen. You have been warned. You leave me no choice but to resort to violence.
This doesn't concern you, friend.
Go on, get out of here!
I warned you. You leave me no choice but violence.
I can't thank you enough for what you did.
The East Empire Company is in your debt.
Glad Orthus was able to find someone competent to help out.
You handled yourself pretty well back at Japhet's Folly. We'll remember that.
Foul blood drinker!
We have guests as often as we can but there aren't a lot of travellers these days. The war keeps people off the roads.
We supply Falkreath to the south. Until recently we supplied Helgen.
Falkreath trades with the surrounding holds and without our wood they would have trouble keeping their prices low.
We would but there's a big wedding taking place at Castle Dour. We'll have to wait until after the wedding.
Come back in a few nights when the wedding reception is over and we'll start the ceremony when you do.
Other people just make things complex. Out here, Hern and I can get everything we need from the occasional traveler.
Hey! It's the bard-to-be.
I am Burguk's mother and wise woman to our tribe, outlander.
I'm Burguk's forge-wife. I shape all the steel for the stronghold.
We will see about this...
I'm sorry, were you about to say something?
Ayleid, Dwemer, Daedric... Not even Falmer. None of them are a match. Quite curious indeed.
I hear you found some... thing in Saarthal. You think it could help make me a powerful mage?
Is it true? One of the Psijics was here in the College?
No one's heard from the Psijic Order in so many years. Hard to believe one would just suddenly show up here.
J'zargo regrets that there was no chance to speak with the Psijic Monk.
There is indeed much that we know that you do not. That does not mean that you are ready to understand it.
Do not let your easy mastery of the Voice tempt you into the arrogance of power that has been the downfall of many Dragonborn before you.
Good. Then you will be ready for whatever lies ahead.
That is commendable. But remember, the Dragon Blood is itself a gift of Akatosh. Do not try to deny that gift.
Your destiny requires you to use your Voice - why else would Akatosh have bestowed this power upon you?
If you remember to use your Voice in service to the purpose of Akatosh, you will remain true to the Way.
The Dragonborn is an exception to all the rules - the Dragon Blood itself is a gift of the gods.
If we accept one gift, how can we deny the other? As Dragonborn, you have received the ability to Shout directly from Akatosh.
We therefore seek to guide you on the proper use of your gift, which transcends the restrictions which bind other mortals.
He was a great war leader of the ancient Nords, a master of the Voice, or Tongue.
After the disaster at Red Mountain, where the Nord army was annihilated, he spent many years pondering the meaning of that terrible defeat.
He finally came to realize that the gods had punished the Nords for their arrogant and blasphemous misuse of the Voice.
He was the first to understand that the Voice should be used solely for the glory and worship of the gods, not the glory of men.
Jurgen Windcaller's mastery of the Voice eventually overcame all opposition, and the Way of the Voice was born.
Ah. I sometimes forget you are not versed in the dragon tongue as we are. This is a rough translation:
"Long has the Stormcrown languished, with no worthy brow to sit upon."
"By our breath we bestow it now to you in the name of Kyne, in the name of Shor, and in the name of Atmora of Old."
"You are Ysmir now, the Dragon of the North, hearken to it."
We spoke the traditional words of greeting to a Dragonborn who has accepted our guidance.
The same words were used to greet the young Talos, when he came to High Hrothgar, before he became the Emperor Tiber Septim.
No doubt. The appearance of a Dragonborn at this time is not an accident. Your destiny is surely bound up with the return of the dragons.
You should focus on honing your Voice, and soon your path will be made clear.
The Voice was a gift of the goddess Kynareth, at the dawn of time. She gave mortals the ability to speak as dragons do.
Although this gift has often been misused, the only true use of the Voice is for the worship and glory of the gods.
True mastery of the Voice can only be achieved when your inner spirit is in harmony with your outward actions.
In the contemplation of the sky, Kynareth's domain, and the practice of the Voice, we strive to achieve this balance.
We study the Way of the Voice, according to the teachings of our founder, Jurgen Windcaller.
Very few are permitted to study with us here at High Hrothgar.
But in your case, Dragonborn, it is a privilege to guide you towards mastery of your Voice.
Stand in the center of the room and prepare yourself to taste the Voice of the Greybeards.
Come inside. The other masters will meet us there.
First, Master Wulfgar will teach you the final word of Unrelenting Force. You are ready.
Please, stop! I'll pay! Take it!
Please, no more. I'll pay, I'll pay. Here!
Here... take your damn coin and get out of here!
Oh? Where?
I'll get everything ready and meet you there.
Our new home. I'll see you there!
Goodbye, my love.
Some layabout named Sabjorn.
Been a thorn in my side for the last few years now.
Well, whoever this mysterious marking represents, they'll regret starting a war with me.
You should bring this information to the Thieves Guild immediately.
About time you showed up.
You might want to rethink that. You're missing out on some wonderful opportunities, working in that bar.
I'm not interested, Jaree. It doesn't matter how many times you ask.
Degaine the beggar got himself kicked out the Temple of Dibella. Caused quite the ruckus.
Yes, I have something special for you.
But it's not for everyone to hear. Meet me in the Underforge tonight.
We will speak more.
Still eager to please. This is good. Skjor may be right about you.
He has something special planned for you. Better talk to him.
There you are.
Are you?
Well. I'm afraid I can't help you with that. I need the crystal to do anything useful, and I don't have it.
Hello, my love. Back from some adventure I bet.
*Sigh*... and that's the last one. All right, enough of this.
C'mon, let's get something to eat.
Here, this is fresh. I'll have another meal ready tomorrow.
Yes, my love? What do you need?
You have it. The Kel - the Elder Scroll. Tiid kreh... qalos. Time shudders at its touch.
There is no question. You are doom-driven. Kogaan Akatosh. The very bones of the earth are at your disposal.
Go then. Fulfill your destiny. Take the Scroll to the Time-Wound.
Do not delay. Alduin will be coming. He cannot miss the signs.
Use Dragonrend! It is the only chance to defeat him!
He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Use Dragonrend, Dovahkiin!
Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Ruth wah nivahriin joor!
Aav uv dir. Join me or perish with your mortal friends.
Use Dragonrend! It is the only chance to defeat him!
He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Use Dragonrend, Dovahkiin!
Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Now, Dovahkiin! Now he is vulnerable!
This is your chance, Dovahkiin! Strike with all your force!
Hurt him while he is grounded!
Kynesgrove is this way. We can travel together or split up and meet there, your choice.
That's better. Let's get on the road to Kynesgrove.
The Greybeards seem to think you're the Dragonborn. I hope they're right.
I think you're looking for this.
We need to talk.
When he shows up here and vouches for you, then I'll see what I can do for you.
If you really are Ralof's friend, I'll be glad to help you. But you didn't answer my question - how do you know him?
Here it is. Magnificent, isn't it?
Place the crystal in the central apparatus, and we can start the process for focusing it.
Took an incredible amount of work to get it running again. Now I'm hoping it'll all be worth it.
No one seems to know. Perhaps the return of the Dwemer. Perhaps the end of the world.
Waiting for what?
We're at war, old fool, or have you forgotten?
Whiterun is the crown jewel of Skyrim, and you would leave us defenseless?
As you command, my lord. But, the issue could be solved another way.
If we spend less on new arms and armor for the guards, for example...
Raise the taxes, then! Put anyone that refuses to pay in the stocks and make an example of them.
My lord Jarl, if we do not take steps soon, our coffers will be empty.
I'll be glad to help you. There are many ways to improve your home, provided you have the coin.
And of course, don't forget to consult your Home Decorating Guide for descriptions of the decorations you can buy.
Now, what would you like to purchase?
You've already purchased every available improvement. I'm afraid I have nothing else to offer you.
Splendid! There's a house available right now.
The house I mentioned earlier is still available, if you want to buy it.
Wonderful! Here is the key to your new home.
Very well, just let me know when you're ready to purchase the house.
Very well, just let me know when you have the necessary funds.
Is there anything else you'd like to purchase?
As you wish. Speak to me again if you decide to purchase some decorations for your home.
I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough gold to pay for that.
Very well, I'll make the arrangements. The next time you visit your house, your new furnishings will be in place.
Hrongar became the Jarl when his brother Balgruuf, who had the job before him, was murdered.
Balgruuf was an even-tempered man, but his brother's a hot-blooded war-monger who wants to settle everything with a blade.
If you've got some business with Hrongar, mind you stay on his good side. The palace dungeon's filled with folk who roused his temper.
Vignar the Revered is the Jarl in Whiterun. I guess he was a great hero once, because the Companions all call him "Vignar the Revered."
Is Vignar too old to be Jarl? It's a fair question. If he's grown infirm and is losing his wits, where does that leave the people of Whiterun?
Lead us to your Sybil.
Dibella, guide us.
Come on. Hurry. We might be too late.
Wait. Something's wrong.
No, you don't want to go up there! A dragon... it's attacking!
The memories. I cannot stand them. You must take them away, return them to Avanchnzel.
You must take the Lexicon from me. Please... take it now.
I'd rather have two bottles of mead then two wenches, friend.
You've never had a taste of sweet Honningbrew? Why, it's only the finest mead outside of Sovngarde!
Drink Honningbrew Mead with a wench on each arm? Ha ha!
Black Briar? How dare you shove that swill in my face. I'd rather drink mammoth drool!
Ah, but you look tired. Come, share a bottle of Honningbrew Mead with me!
Ah, is there any way you'd rather spend the day than drinking Honningbrew Mead?
Cheers, my friend! May your adventures find you fame and fortune!
Hail, friend! It's good to see another merry soul enjoying this fine day.
I don't talk to people who drink that Black Briar drench.
This good deed should not go unrewarded. Here, take this!
Ha! Too true, my friend. Too true...
Ysmir's beard! You're one after my own heart. I would love another bottle.
Ha, nothing like fine spirits to help raise your... well, your spirits!
The meads won't argue your ear off about which one should go first!
Black Briar Mead? What are you trying to do? Poison me?
But enough talk. Come! Won't you share a drink with me and my companions?
Why's that?
Its sweetness is like a fair maiden's kiss on a starry night. And it's got enough kick to put a frost troll on his hind quarters! Ha!
Poisons can kill your enemies faster than any blade.
I am wise woman to this stronghold.
We can trade in the morning, outlander.
One day, I must fight my brother Nagrub for chiefdom. It will not be easy.
It's late outlander. Come back in the morning if you need to spend coin.
We only trade during the day, outlander. At night, most rest in the longhouse.
I'm here to mine, outlander. So unless you want to dig, get out.
I have metal to dig.
Malacath has given me the task to mine. Good ore brings strength to our people.
Every Orc learns the heavy labor of mining. All spend years in the caves.
The mines are hard, but Malacath rewards those who rise to the challenge.
Come on in. Just stoked the fire. Take a seat and get the cold out.
Can I help you?
Oh, do come in! I hope I have what you need.
Not in our nature.
You don't need my fruit, I've heard when you sing people throw tomatoes at you anyway.
I think I may wait until the Burning of King Olaf to have and Spiced Wine.
You Bards don't cut each other any slack do you?
How about I trade you a song for some fruit?
Positions.
It is a shame though.
Speaking of harsh.
Perhaps if I were a little more blessed I could afford to give away my fruit. As is you'll have to pay, Rorlund.
Perhaps you could spare a tomato for a priest of the Divines?
It's too bad the Burning of King Olaf was called off this year. I think some of the younger bards would really have gotten a kick out of it.
I think our flutist here has gotten better since the Burning of King Olaf.
Lock the city gate.
That's wonderful. You've done us a great service. As promised the outfit is yours and here's something for your trouble.
He's okay. A little breathy and a little harsh on the notes.
Take a good look around. I'm sure you'll find what you're looking for. If not, let me know, I might have it stored away.
Got something for just about everybody in here. Give a holler if you have any questions.
Aren't you a sight for sore eyes.
Hello friend. Make yourself at home.
You lost?
I trust you're not planning any trouble. What can I do for you, friend?
If you've got the coin, you've come to the right place. Pull up a seat.
Welcome. Let me know if you want anything... think I got a clean mug around here somewhere.
Come on in. We got warm food, warm drinks, and warm beds.
She's okay. A little breathy and a little harsh on the notes.
My mother always used to say that too.
It is. We train the bards you hear throughout the land. Viarmo is the head of the college and in charge of admissions.
Only in that I'm a graduate. I work at here at the Winking Skeever. I would highly recommend it though. Talk to Viarmo if you're interested.
Only in that I'm a graduate. I work over at the Winking Skeever. I would highly recommend it though. Talk to Viarmo if you're interested.
Oh really? Well you can tell them that I will be putting in a request for a few dresses quite soon.
Lurbuk?
Fancies himself a bard. He pays, so I let him stay.
If I had any customers, I'd be worried about him annoying them. But, well... Look around.
No, no. Well, sometimes, but that's not the problem.
I just often don't have much to do, so I tend to... I try things out. They don't usually work, but I try.
Next thing I know, I've used up most of my stock.
The marsh is treacherous. You would do well to not wander at night.
As anyone will tell you, Morthal can be a dangerous place.
My... talents... are useful here. I help maintain order, even if it goes unnoticed.
Slow? No. It just ain't there at all.
Few enough reasons to pass through Morthal before the war started.
Now... Well, let's just say the front door doesn't get much use.
Fine, Ataf. You should eat some more tomatoes, though.
It's quite fetching actually. The craftsmanship is excellent.
I could listen to him play all day.
I could listen to her play all day.
Um, excuse me. Could I buy two leeks.
Well, you can understand Elisif not wanting it.
I'm so glad we got to have it. I thought for sure Elisif would never consent.
Look silly! As if you could look any sillier than you do now.
I'm afraid that kind of thing doesn't work on Captains in the Imperial Army.
Generally, if you want rumors, you should talk to Corpulus. That said... I did see a visitor pass through on his way to the Blue Palace.
Absolutely not. I prefer to be near enough to come visit but free to come and go as I please.
Good. Here's the outfit. Try to get Elisif's opinion on it and if she likes it mention it's from Radiant Raiment.
It's those damned Blackblood Marauders. They're scaring off shipping.
One apple. Since the Blackbloods were dispatched I've had good luck getting shipments.
Oh, no. But being surrounded by good music, good drink and good friends is all I've ever asked for.
Do you miss your days training at the College?
If you're heading to the Blue Palace, you might want to rethink that outfit.
If you ever want to replace those old rags, stop by Radiant Raiment.
Youngest bard at the Bards College, at your service.
Anything I can do for you, I will.
The pleasure's all mine.
So exciting when new folk come into town. I bet you've had plenty of adventures.
Someday, I'll be a gallant heroine. You make it look so.. appealing.
Maybe you need a partner? Hmm? Just picture it. Two women, the dusty road, strapping men at every tavern, a fortune in gold to be claimed...
You're really going to the Blue Palace? That presents an opportunity.
If you were willing to wear one of Radiant Raiment's outfits and speak to the Jarl I would not only pay you but let you keep the outfit.
Your fruit are looking a little less ripe today.
Your selection looks very delicious. I'll take an apple.
You and everyone else. Word is it may not happen this year though.
Damn shame, Roggvir. You were a good man.
That makes Twenty-Three.
Talos protect you.
Roggvir. You damn fool.
She did, did she? She's going to get a sit down later.
Yeah, that's what papa says too.
You would do that? That's great. You should go do it right now.
Thanks. Papa talks about it some. Mum doesn't say much since Uncle Roggvir died. She doesn't even go to Temple anymore.
I wish she would. At least that way she'd have people to talk to.
Thanks so much. I'll return to temple. Here's something for your time and effort.
Why did you... nevermind, it's a sign. I'll return to temple. Here's something for your help.
Thanks.
Well. I wish it were that simple.
Maybe it is. Look, I want to go back but I won't feel right in there without a tie to Talos.
My brother Roggvir wore a symbol of Talos. He kept it hidden on his person. If I had that...
I can't go near his body. Would you get it back for me?
Free of charge for you.
Hello, Silana. Would you like an apple?
I'm pleased when the palace is clean, I'm pleased when the beds are made on time, I'm pleased when I Erdi isn't around.
Your a hard woman to please Una.
You don't put up with a man like that. He puts up with you.
Vegetables are the food of the Divines.
There's nothing quite like a good vegetable.
I once saw Fihada put an arrow in between the eyes of a Blackblood Marauder at five hundred paces.
I keep coming out here thinking I'll see what others see in this... frivolity.
How do you put up with that fop Fihada?
I've heard stanger things, are eaten over at the Temple of the Divines.
Most of those don't happen very often.
I've tasted your food, Odar. It's better than most songs I've heard.
You never can tell.
Sometimes I like to think my cooking is like playing the flute or singing a song.
The guy in from Dragon Bridge? He didn't seem the type.
I don't think that was a compliment.
I'll need a whole crate of fruit. That Varnius Junius went through quite a few of our supplies.
You like everyone Jorn. You'll make a good court bard some day.
That's generous, Jala, but I am happy to pay my way.
I like you Una. You've got presence.
By the gods! What manner of power is that?
That is one... big hammer.
Your shield... Dwarf-make, is it not? But yet it seems so much... more.
What a foul and unnatural weapon you wield. Never have I seen its like.
What manner of staff is that? Such a horror...
You wield... Azura's Star? How... how can such a thing be possible?
Fine armor you've got there. Dwarven make, am I right?
What do you aim to do with that hammer, friend? Knock down a house?
No.. get that accursed mace away from me...
Still can't wake him up? Perhaps you're barking up the wrong tree?
You seem to be having a small problem... or perhaps it's a big problem? Maybe if you shrunk the whole thing down a little first?
Hmm... Your creature doesn't appear to be fairing any better than before. Methinks your aim is off.
Dammit...
I think this might be the end...
Been about eight years now. I used to be a mercenary, but one trip to Ravenscar Hollow cured me of that.
There's hagravens in that cave with talons like daggers. They'll open your bowels quick as a wink if you let 'em get close.
Creature like that'll make a man think long and hard about what he wants out of life.
My pa used to say there ain't no use worryin' about the things you can't change.
If it's my fate to be ended by a dragon, so be it. Meantime, I'll live my life the way I want to.
Enchanting services are one of the few things that keep us in touch with the rest of Skyrim.
Perhaps we can go another few decades without a disaster. That would be nice.
I'm working on some very important research, I assure you.
The Eye of Magnus. Here, but lost. Slipped through our grasp. Oh, what we might have learned.
I wonder what we could've done with the Eye, had it been put to better use?
Have you seen Mirabelle around? I know she's dead, but I thought... Well, I thought she might stop in and say hello anyway.
You know, they say knowledge is its own reward.
Arch-Mage or not, my rules about books still stand.
All this knowledge might have been lost, were it not for you.
I don't want to see you treating any of these books poorly. Are we clear?
Tolfdir as Master Wizard. Certainly didn't anticipate that.
And to think, Mirabelle questioned my allowing you into the College. I can just imagine the look on her face now.
Mirabelle does a fine job as Master Wizard, I suppose. Still, there's always room for improvement.
I care little for idle conversation, but perhaps you need help with Conjuration magic?
Ancano is exactly why we're so restrictive about access to the College. We'd all have perished if it weren't for you.
I'm sure you've made note of who's been the most helpful during your time here, Arch-Mage.
May I just say, it's a pleasure to have you leading the College. Really.
If there's ever any way I can assist you, please let me know.
I'm quite pleased that we're back to business as usual.
Certainly nothing illicit going on here, Arch-Mage. You can be quite sure of that.
Even with the College's laid-back attitude, there are still some things that are off-limits.
Like it or not, you are a shining example to future generations of College Mages.
After all that it's been through, the College still stands. In no small part thanks to you!
I don't know what it's doing up there, but I'm not waiting around to find out!
It flew over the town and landed on the old dragon burial mound.
Well, I don't know. Not yet... it flew over the town and landed on the old dragon mound!
It's always encouraging to see the younger generations embracing education.
I still can't believe it. You, the Arch-Mage? Never would have guessed it.
I never would have suspected that the Eye could be so dangerous. Tolfdir is right, isn't he? Magic really can overwhelm you.
I've certainly learned more here than I ever expected to.
I wonder if the College's reptuation will ever recover from our adventures.
The College is a bit of an oddity in Skyrim. Magic isn't something that's looked upon fondly here.
Back in 42 I was stationed in Hammerfell, on leave in Sentinel, trying to track down some refugee relatives who had fled persecution in Alinor.
Suddenly an explosion of magic in the refugee quarter. Thalmor mages were attacking the Altmer dissidents who were resisting with magic of their own.
I ran to the scene with other Legionaries who where stationed there, but the entire quarter was a smoking ruin by the time we arrived.
Everyone was dead. Wholesale slaughter. The Dominion, not content with killing dissidents at home, came to Hammerfell to finish the job.
We're supposedly at peace now. But I put in to be stationed here to keep an eye on the Thalmor. I've a feeling they're behind this unrest in Skyrim.
My parents were traveling merchants eventually settling in Cyrodiil. I must have inherited their wanderlust...
I joined the Legion to see the world. I know, that's one of those things people say, but it's completely true in my case.
Wasn't long before I saw more of the world than I bargained for.
For the glory of the Empire.
The life of a soldier is full of hardship. That's nothing.
But they send the Legion to places that've gotten too bad to be settled without violence. What's hard is seeing good people warped by evil.
And I've seen the face of evil. It was in the air above Sentinel on the Night of Green Fire.
Home? Home to me is a hot cup of ale at the end of the day and five minutes without someone needing something from me...
Cyrodiil is a beautiful place, full of diverse peoples and histories. But so too, Hammerfell and Skyrim, and every other place I've been.
Don't get me wrong, every country has its dark corners. But that's why we're here. To bring order and civilization, and to protect the people.
Ulfric and his thugs are stirring up trouble for their own agenda. The Empire is the only thing keeping the Dominion from walking all over Skyrim.
It matters little if some of the people here are ignorant to that truth. They are still citizens of the Empire. It's our solemn duty to protect them.
Aye. I've worked on a few moorings here and there for it. Most of the ship captains are superstitious about who works on their vessels, though.
And Dawnstar is known for its bad luck. We're the last port of call before Windhelm, and the ore we trade makes rich targets for pirates.
Pfft. I'm the oldest woman in Dawnstar. Was here when the Skald the Elder was Skald the Younger.
He's a fool, if you haven't met him already. Thinks Ulfric Stormcloak is invincible and spits dragon fire.
The people here look to Brina Merilis when they need things settled. Real firebrand going up. Wasn't surprised when she joined the Legion.
That's right. In the Second Era, Tiber Septim himself led the army that conquered Old Hroldan from the barbarians of the Reach.
Septim would later found the Empire that united Tamriel, but his first known battle and victory was right here.
And this inn has the very bed the great general slept in on his first night as Old Hroldan's liberator. As good as it was hundreds of years ago.
Every Nord should admire Ulfric. He fights for all of us.
In fact, he's the reason I chose to learn the smithing trade. I want to make weapons and armor for the great Stormcloak army.
Grew up here. Colder as Atmora, but that just grows the beards thicker!
Lot of history in these walls. We're trying to make some more. It's a lucky time to be alive.
Wherever I can find them. If you're the type who worries over where something came from, you may want to move on.
Are you kidding? I came all the way from Cyrodiil just to work with him.
He's... he's not the best teacher. But just watching him, I've learned so much.
I don't see these troubles as business. This is a labor of honor.
To bring Ulfric his rightful glory, I would hammer a thousand shields.
Shor's bones, I might just have to!
No, but then I don't have much need for Ulfric.
We both leave well enough alone.
Smithing isn't the most exciting thing for me, but we all have to lend a hand as best we can.
If a Stormcloak uses one of my swords to strike down an Imperial soldier, then I've done my part to free our land.

Ah, so it's gold you're after, then?

Well met, kinsman. What brings you to Whiterun?
Well met, traveler. What brings you to Whiterun?
Ah, hello my Argonian friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Ah, hello my Khajiit friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Ah, kinsman. How my heart soars to look upon a brother Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs?
Ah... How my heart soars to look upon a sister Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs?

Well met, traveler.
...You! Why?

Good.

For Whiterun! For the Empire!
Now, you. Get down there.

It's off to Cidhna Mine for you.
All right. Open her up.
Carry on men, my gratitude and blessings go with you!
Anything for my favorite lass.

A warrior marches on the gut. Here. This should give you strength. I'll cook another tomorrow.
Here. This is from a recipe my people have passed on to me. I can cook another tomorrow.
Here you are. I added a little mead into the recipe. I think it tastes better. Let me know what you think tomorrow, I'll make another one.
I should be asking you the same question, but all right. I'll cook. Here, try this, and don't you dare ask for another one before tomorrow.
I can. Here. This is fresh. I'll have another one made tomorrow.
A home for the two of us. I'll meet you there.
It will be our little nest. I'll be waiting for you there.
The two of us, living together. I'll see you at home!
I'll see you at home, then. Don't keep me waiting!
I'll be waiting for you at home, then.
Where are you thinking?
Where?
You want to move? Where?

And where would that be?

We're united, but where should we live? My home is yours, of course.

This is the happiest day of my life. Where should we live? My home is your home now, of course.
Married. It doesn't feel like I thought it would, but I'm happy. Where should we live? You could stay with me....

To the block, prisoner. Nice and easy.
Next, the Nord in the rags!

Who are you?

It's kind of dark in here.
How deep is this cave?

Hard to believe people actually have to work in places like this.
Wonder what they mine from these walls.
I'll take a sword or mace over a pick axe any day.
Do you think we'll find gold nuggets in here?
Never knew a place could be so ancient. I bet it's over a thousand years old.
Wonder if any of my ancestors ever walked these halls?
Everything in here seems to be about death. Burial urns, crypts, funeral shrouds. It's a bit depressing.
I wonder what the ancient nords used this place for?
This place is amazing! It's hard to believe that mortal hands built them.
What do you think these devices were for?

It's amazing this place is so well preserved. There isn't even a hint of rust.
The Jarls never want to pay to keep the forts. They just keep slipping further into ruin.
I wonder what great battles were fought here?
Do you think Tiber Septim ever fought here?
I can see why they built the fort here. A small force could control most of the nearby land.
Wizards. Why is it always wizards.
Most bandits are cowards. These seem to be braver than most.
I hate bandits and thieves. Men like these raid our town every year.

The Forsworn are strange men. They fight to reclaim Markarth, but they live like savages.
I don't like wizards and their spells. Give me an axe any day.

These Forsworn are just savages. They live in caves and ruins when civilized folk build towns and cities.

These Forsworn don't even have the decency to dress right.
Draugr are slow and clumsy. Hardly a challenge for a man like me.
Don't get too far ahead.
Ah yes, the extract, good. Poisonous, of course, but it has its uses.
I suppose you want something for your trouble? Here.
Very well.
We have nothing to talk about. I can't believe what you did to me.

Thanks.
Get out of here. Get out of here and never come back!

Very well.

Grrah!

Maar saraan ko Sovngarde.

Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.

The dark elves are too proud and naive to understand the way things truly are, and so they continue to dwell in that slum.
I'm a Dark Elf and I live in Windhelm, so yes, I live in the Gray Quarter.

Sit back and enjoy the ride.

Where do you want to go?

Ready to go?
Woah!

You're needed in Winterhold. Report to our hidden camp there.
You're needed in Hjaalmarch. There's much to be done.
I'm promoting you to Quaestor.
Take this blade, a symbol of your new station and responsibility. I'll keep an eye on your progress. The Empire rewards excellence, and so do I.
Battles are won by trained and disciplined men. Wars are won by talented and exceptional individuals.
I'm raising you to Praefect. Take this blade, as a gift and symbol of your new rank. You've earned it.
You've come a long way. I don't think we could do without you. I'm hereby raising you to Tribune. Congratulations.
Here, take this gift as a sign of the Empire's appreciation, and my own.

On behalf of the Empire, and myself, please accept this gift along with your new rank.
Congratulations.

Please take this on behalf of all the brothers and sisters.

Multitudes of our enemies are buried in an avalanche of pain and retribution delivered by your hands. I shall call you Snow-Hammer now.
You show great passion for battle, and compassion for those we fight for. You are becoming indispensable to our cause.

You've become a true hero of Skyrim. I number you among my kin. You shall now be known as Stormblade.
The love of the land and her people flows from your heart, even as death to her enemies flows from your hands.
On behalf of the sons and daughters of Skyrim, on behalf of all that is righteous and true, take this token of our appreciation for your service.

Sorry, a girl can't be too careful out here.

Name's Angi and if you try anything stupid I won't hesitate to put in arrow in your head.

Hey! Watch what you're doing.

I'm staying out of this.

This should be good.

Ah!

You're short on coin.
I don't think so.

Fortunately, these doors have a weakness if you know how to exploit it. Quite simple, really.

That should do it. After you.

This one doesn't look too difficult.

Only inside the gates. Much of Falkreath hold is wilderness and there's plenty of trouble to be had.

You have the look of a killer.

Indara and I like to keep to ourselves and run our farm.

My husband Mathias and I tend Corpselight Farm.

Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.

Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.

Falkreath's cemetery reminds of us that war is not new here.
Being Housecarl of Falkreath is an honor.
Dengeir was a good Jarl in his day.  Even if his day has passed.
I'm the Steward here.  I serve under Jarl Siddgeir and Jarl Dengeir before him.
Siddgeir can sometimes act too quickly but he does listen to Helvard and I.
For Falkreath and I this is just one more war in a long line.
Stand aside.  I don't need to speak to you.
Boland speaks much about the war and the Stormcloaks but he knows who provides.
The Stormcloaks want freedom but don't understand its price.
There are opportunities everywhere.  Why don't you go find them.
No time, I have business to conduct.
Unless you intend to bury someone this isn't the place you're looking for, traveler.
Falkreath was once full of wanderers and warriors like yourself. Those were better days.
Skyrim's history of war is well documented here in Falkreath's graveyard.
My brother Dengeir can be... difficult. Take his words with a grain of salt.
While I appreciate the sentiment, I still have to decline.
Being an admirer, I'm sure you can appreciate the need to keep my research a secret.
Do you realize that at a snap of my fingers I can bring the entire Markarth city guard to my defense?
You best rethink this course of action or you may find yourself on the executioner's block.
If you need a bow, there's one on the rack to the left of the door.
Already did. Part of the reason I'm living out here now. Thanks for offering though.
I'd give you a bow, but it looks like you've already taken it from the rack.
An outsider, eh? Never heard of the Companions?
An order of warriors. We are brothers and sisters in honor.
And we show up to solve problems if the coin is good enough.
Certainly not. But a true warrior would have relished the opportunity to take on a giant.
That's why I'm here with my Shield-Brothers.
Well nobody asked you. If you think you're better than we are, go talk to Kodlak Whitemane.
See what a warrior of true mettle is like.
Ha. Another milk drinker crying about his effort.
Glory only awaits those who triumph.
And as Companions, our glory must always be hard-fought.
Not for me to say. You'll have to talk to Kodlak Whitemane up in Jorrvaskr.
The old man's got a good sense for people. He can look in your eyes and tell your worth.
If you go to him, good luck.
You handle yourself well. You could make for a decent Shield-Brother.
You handle yourself well. You could make for a decent Shield-Sister.
Well, that's taken care of. No thanks to you.
I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes!
But you disappeared! You were there... then you weren't! Did you mean to help poor Cicero or not? I'm left to wonder...
Ah, it doesn't matter. What's done is done, right? Cicero is here! As is the Night Mother! Joy of joys!
Here to shame an old woman? Leave me be.
But.. stealing the boss's sword? Did he have a death wish?
Damn it, not now!
Ra'jirr was always dragging her into things.
But... Eisa? She's smarter than that.
Who knows. The cat was crazy. She was a fool to trust him.
We've talked about composure, speed, and precision.
Now it's time to use all three at once.
However, with such a long shot, it's more than likely that's the only shot you'll be able to take before your prey runs off.
I'll count to three and then say "go." You have ten seconds to hit each of the four targets. Draw string and get ready.
OK, your turn. Try and hit that target way in the back, far behind the other three. Remember to take your time and make your shot count.
You did it! All four targets in ten seconds. Nice job.
Often times you won't be able to get as close to your prey as you'd like and you'll have to settle with a long shot.
When I tell you to, try and hit all four targets in ten seconds.
I'm warning you now, this challenge will be quite difficult.
Try and hit the far target.
Nice job hitting those targets.
Excellent. Follow me to the targets and we'll begin.
One, two, three...
Go!
Time's up! Better luck next time.
Here, take these. Looks like you're running low.
Listen to me and you might learn something.
We've talked about speed and composure. Now we'll go over precision.
If you find yourself in such a situation, then you need to make your shot count.
Don't rush your shot. Crouch down, get comfortable, and take aim.
Shoot the far target.
Shoot the far target.
I seem to have more chores for the Winking Skeever each day. I still have a delivery to do too.
Liar!
As long as they're playing instruments they're passable. You don't want to hear them sing.
Greta is taking it hardest. She won't go to the Temple of Divines anymore.
A little late in the day for your rounds Sorex.
Traitor!
Speaking of sounding good, I've heard a couple of your classmates on the street recently.
I'm sorry about Roggvir. It's a shame, I liked him.
Thank you, Addvar, I am picky about what I eat these days.
Shhhhhh...
Salmon sounds good today.
Sorry, I need to pay attention so I can remember this.
A war veteran should be respected.
Thanks, Aia. That means a lot.
Glad to hear it, Aia.
Stand back. Interrupting an execution is a serious crime.
Fresh fish sounds like just the thing today, Addvar.
Cut em down!
I appreciate the thought though.
Roggvir always talked about his honor. Seems to me honorable men don't get executed.
A sale's a sale but I have to know... why are you buying something for Noster?
None of what he collected tasted as good as your Spiced Wine, Evette.
I'm still finding your Spiced Wine to be the best drink I've ever tasted.
Booooo.
Excuse me, I'm trying to watch a traitor to the Empire lose his head.
I am buying a fish for Noster. He likes fish.
Rum from Hammerfell, mulled wine from Daggerfall and the like.
Booooo.
I don't think he would have wanted your absolution, Rorlund. He worshiped Talos.
A traitor right up to the end.
That's my brother up there.
Okay. Well. That's all I've got.
Really?
Booooo.
I do not like fish.
You know, my father collected exotic alcohols.
He doesn't deserve to speak!
I wish we could have absolved your brother-in-law of his sins before his execution.
Jawanan, I don't see you here often.
Stop moping about, girl. Tears won't change what you've done.
Real men prove their love. Words are as empty as the air they run through.
He said he loved me....
You should be more careful with the men you pick, dear.
Oh right, the rum. Thank you. I developed a taste for it after a fair weather trip to Hammerfell.
Here, this is for you.
Yeah, thanks.
Save me a lot of trouble. Here. Take it to the Blue Palace.
Jarl Elisif the Fair. Poor woman. Losing her husband and king like that. It was a dark day for us all.
Damn, that reminds me, I have a delivery to make to her steward, Falk Firebeard. He ordered a special bottle of Stros M'Kai Rum.
We all get used, dear. It's living with it that makes you a woman.
I was such a fool. How could I let him use me like that?
We need to move. Now!
Up the stairs! Quickly!
Move! Up the tower!
Come on! Before the dragon brings down the whole tower!
What are you waiting for? Up the stairs! Now!
I do hope mummy and father come to get me soon. Ever since that Aventus boy left, Grelod has been so angry.
Things are much better now that Grelod is dead, but still. I'm sick of this place.
I can't believe Grelod is dead. I had no love for the woman, but to be murdered, in cold blood...
The boss went down there? Today?
Now you're sounding as crazy as the cat. Be going on about the Pale Lady next.
What? There's a whole shift down there!
Get her!
Kyr's orders. Nobody goes in or out until he gets back. Or the boss will skin you soon as he's done with Ra'jirr.
I've got a bad feeling about this... something's just felt wrong down there lately. Eerie.
There she is!
Yeah, he's after Ra'jirr- never seen him so angry.
We have a cozy little profit. Here. This is your share, love.
We've made a bit of gold. Here, love, this is your share.
Fortune smiles on us. Here's your share, love.
The gold is flowing nicely. Here's your half of the profits, my dear.
You want your share, don't you? Here you are, love.
Of course it has. Would you expect any less of me? Here, this is your half, my love.
It has. Here, this is your half, love.
You should probably just take this directly to Faleen. Wouldn't want the old icebrain stumbling over the words.
You should probably just let Faleen read it herself. Don't want you stumbling over the words and ruining my flow.
As for what she might like...
I didn't tell you this. Faleen likes to act tough, but she really has a soft spot for, of all things, poetry.
You know, I took some classes at the Bards College as a youth. Poems come in handy when wooing.
There's a poem I once used on an older lady of Rorikstead. I can change it to be about Faleen, if you've got some gold.
Calcelmo? Is he interested in Faleen? That sly old codger. I should have guessed.
Good for him. For the both of them.
Between you and me, she could use a bit of warmth.
Ah, a secret admirer, eh? Don't worry, I won't tell a soul.
He must be here somewhere...
I don't have time for your foolishness. Either help me or be gone.
If it's a fight you want, it's a fight you'll get.
Back off!
You'll have to take it from me.
Your blood won't bother me!
You'll have to fight me for it.
I'll hurt you if I have to.
You're too slow and stupid to take it from me.
Who left that here?
Well! Finders, keepers as they say.
If nobody wants that, I'll take it.
Damn shame to leave that lying around.
It's a bit worn, but I'll take it.
Looks like it's my lucky day.
Hmm. What's this?
I saw it first! I'm taking it.
Not if I get it first.
No. It's mine!
We'll see about that.
I'm taking it anyway.
No!
I think not.
Although... it might be difficult to persuade the Jarl to allow you to use his palace as a dragon trap...
I'm sure you'll manage, though. If you can defeat the World-Eater, surely sweet-talking the Jarl of Whiterun isn't beyond you.
Esbern should be able to help. He's spent his whole life poring over every old book he could find about dragons and that prophecy.
He's been digging around the records the Blades left here, too. But we can talk about that later.
Did you find the Elder Scroll? What happened when you took it back to the Throat of the World?
What's happened? Did you find a way to learn the Dragonrend Shout?
The Jarl's palace in Whiterun? Ah, yes... the old story about King Olaf's pet dragon. Ingenious!
Arrogance will serve you poorly. Your worth must be determined before knowledge can be bestowed upon you.
And so you shall be tested.
I need you to collect some dragon scales. And not just any dragon scales.
Colette has already told you. You must be tested before knowledge can be imparted to you.
I need dragon scales. And not just any dragon scales.
Once again you have come seeking something.
The circumstances differ. This time I have what you seek.
Are you prepared?
The trial awaits. Are you now prepared?
At this very moment? No. However...
Well, I've been working on something. An improvement on the Ebonyflesh spell.
I've made progress, but then hit a snag. I think perhaps you could help.
You are not worthy!
Knowledge is power. Knowing spells can be powerful, but applying that knowledge is key.
You will be called upon to rely solely on your Restoration spells.
Step into the light, and your test begins.
You will rely on your skill as a mage. Not on your belongings, not on your scrolls and potions. Only what lies within.
Survive, and you are worthy. This is the test put before you. Will you accept?
As I've said, I made some progress using the essence of dragon scales. But they're just not quite potent enough.
My knowledge of Skyrim's history is far from complete, but I did some research and discovered two things.
First, there are ancient references to dragon "heartscales". What little I could learn suggests they may be what I need.
Second, there are stories of a dagger called Kahvozein's Fang, said to be sharp enough to carve individual scales off a dragon.
First you'll need to retrieve the dagger, then use it to carve a few scales off a dragon. Presumably a deceased one.
No, no. You just stay here and rest.
You want to stay here, fine. But the rest of us aren't giving up now.
We'll come back for you as soon as we've reached our goal. You have my word.
I don't care... We're going all the way in. I want to know what Shalidor hid in here.
Don't just stand there! Kill that thing!
Guards, get the townspeople to safety!
Someone get the battlemages out here. Now!
Sentries! What do you see?
What in Oblivion is that?
So. You were at Helgen? You saw this dragon with your own eyes?
Ahhh, a fine but bloody tale. I can do that one.
You must be a Stormcloak through and through to request that one.
We drink to our youth, and to days come and gone. For the age of oppression is now nearly done.
We'll drive out the Empire from this land that we own. With our blood and our steel we will take back our home.
All hail to Ulfric! You are the High King! In your great honor we drink and we sing.
We're the children of Skyrim, and we fight all our lives. And when Sovngarde beckons, every one of us dies!
But this land is ours and we'll see it wiped clean. Of the scourge that has sullied our hopes and our dreams.
There once was a hero named Ragnar the Red, who came riding to Whiterun from ole Rorikstead!
And the braggart did swagger and brandish his blade, as he told of bold battles and gold he had made!
But then he went quiet, did Ragnar the Red, when he met the shieldmaiden Matilda who said...
"Oh, you talk and you lie and you drink all our mead! Now I think it's high time that you lie down and bleed!"
And so then came the clashing and slashing of steel, as the brave lass Matilda charged in full of zeal!
And the braggart named Ragnar was boastful no moooooree... when his ugly red head rolled around on the floor!
Forgive me, my lord. It was foolish of me to make such a suggestion.
The finest cuts, fresh from the wilds!
Chops and steaks fit for a Jarl! Come and see my selection of finest-quality meats!
Shiny trinkets for your good lady! All crafted by the great Eorlund Gray-Mane himself!
Bits and baubles for sale, all crafted by the best blacksmith in Skyrim.
Ripe fruit and fresh vegetables for sale!
Fresh-baked loaves, still warm from the oven!
He is set to leave the Penitus Oculatus outpost at Dragon Bridge, and inspect the security of each city in Skyrim.
Go there now. Observe Gaius Maro's departure, and follow him. Waylay him in one of the cities, and send his soul to Sithis.
Once he's dead, plant the incriminating letter on his body, and let fate take care of the rest.
Oh, and one final thing...
Killing Commander Maro would do no good. Someone would merely replace him. No, we need him to remain in charge. But... distracted. Weakened.
Implicate and kill his son Gaius, and the commander will be grief-stricken and humiliated, his family name ruined. The security plan will then fail.
Best yet, the Emperor will be lulled into a false sense of security, thinking an assassination plot had already been discovered and foiled.
Walk in Gaius Maro's shadow, and deliver to him the judgment of Sithis.
Citizen?
You must be lost. The tavern is down the road a piece.
I'm fairly certain you wandered into the wrong building, friend...
That's not the Skyrim I want to live in.
Ulfric. Stop.
Stop what? Taking Skyrim back from those who'd leave her to rot?
Stand aside woman. We've come for the General.
Rikke. Go.
You're free to leave.
You were there with us. You saw it. The day the Empire signed that damn treaty was the day the Empire died.
Rikke. You don't have to do this.
The Empire is weak, obsolete. Look at how far we've come and with so little.
When we're done rooting out Imperial influence here at home, then we will take our war to the Aldmeri Dominion.
Already done.
This is what you wanted? Shield brothers and sisters killing each other?
Families torn apart?
This is the Skyrim you want?!
I'm also free to stay and fight for what I believe in.
Damnit woman, stand aside.
You're a damn fool.
He has given up. But I have not.
Always was a stubborn woman. She's not going to stand down.
War always comes down to this, doesn't it? A single truth-laden moment.
Dragonborn... Perhaps this is the fate of Skyrim after all. To be destroyed from the inside.
Aye, I'll hate it, but I'll do it if I have to.
It's up to her, isn't it?
If that must be, then it will. And why should I be any different? How many the lives already lost. What's one more?
Secure the door.
You're a cold hearted bastard, aren't you?
I fear that will be the way of it.
A true Nord never fears death. It's the how and why of it that one needs consider.
You're wrong. Ulfric. We need the Empire. Without it Skyrim will assuredly fall to the Dominion.
You've left me no choice...
Talos preserve us.
You're also free to die for it.
We Gray-Manes got just as much to be proud of as them Battle-Borns, but you won't see us with our noses in the air.
I think my voice is ready. I hope we've done this well.
Does the journal say where this wealth came from?
Hmm. This is intriguing, but highly disturbing.
I'm not familiar with the Twilight Sepulcher. What is it? What's Mercer Frey done?
Anything else, Enthir? Anything about... the Nightingales?
It's alright Karliah. You don't have to say a word.
The last few pages seem to describe "the failure of the Nightingales" although it doesn't go into great detail.
Gallus also repeatedly mentions his strong belief that Mercer desecrated something known as the Twilight Sepulcher.
Yes. Gallus seems certain that Mercer had been removing funds from the Guild's treasury without anyone's knowledge.
It appears that Gallus had suspicions about Mercer Frey's allegiance to the Guild for months.
Gallus had begun to uncover what he calls an "...unduly lavish lifestyle replete with spending vast amounts of gold on personal pleasures."
Shadows preserve us. So it's true...
Hmm. Yes, here it is.
As a Nightingale, I've been sworn to secrecy regarding the Sepulcher.
I know the Guild doesn't do much to foster faith, but I'm going to have to ask that you continue to trust me.
I'm sorry Enthir, I can't say. All that matters is we deliver your translation to the Guild immediately.
Farewell, Enthir... words can't express...
Shhh. Listen to Enthir... we can't afford to miss a single detail.
We haven't much time for discussion, but what did you need?
Don't you go trackin' your dirty boots on my clean floor!
Soldiers! They're everywhere these days. And not a one of them knows how to pick up after themselves.
We're not done here.
No. I was just getting some fresh air. Had one too many pints of mead at the Silver-Blood Inn.
Oh, I think you dropped this. Some kind of note. Looks important.
You don't have to say sorry to me. I just hope the Eight bring us more peace in the future.
Huh. Not sure. I don't worship Talos, myself.
I think I heard someone mention it was underneath the Temple of Dibella, in the big crag in the center of the city.
My note? No, that's yours. Must have fallen out of your pocket.
Sure, I understand. It's not easy to go from being executed by the Legion one day to joining up the next.
But I think you'll see that the Legion is Skyrim's only hope for real peace right now. I know you'll make the right choice in the end.
I hope so. The Legion is Skyrim's only hope right now. Come on, we'd better get moving.
Welcome to Whiterun, friend. Go right on in.
Halt! City's closed with the dragons about. Official business only.
Oh yes, many times. But mighty Talos was my keeper, my shield against the heresy of the south!
If not for Talos, and a few modest bribes paid to the right jailors, why, I would not be standing before you today, resplendent in my faith!
Or else what? You think you can stand against the entire Whiterun city guard? The gate's closed.
Riverwood's in danger, too? You'd better go on in. You'll find the Jarl in Dragonsreach, at the top of the hill.
Fine, but we'll be keeping an eye on you.
Safe travels.
Of course! I know, today wasn't the best introduction to the Legion, but I hope you'll give us another chance.
The Legion could really use someone like you, especially now.
And if the rebels have themselves a dragon, General Tullius is the only one who can stop them.
General Tullius has his headquarters in Castle Dour, in the city of Solitude, northwest of here.
You'll want to talk to Legate Rikke. She deals with the new recruits.
I'll be sure to put in a good word for you if I get back to Solitude first.
Oh, of course you want me to leave. Just waiting for me to turn my back. So you can have all the glory for yourself!
Sulla look, the passage is open, wait... there's someone down there!
Sulla, let's just get out of here. Hasn't there been enough death?
No! Blackreach was to be my discovery!
Ha! I thought as much.
So? So?
No, I'm a Redguard. My family hails from the Imperial Province. Ah, but I left home when I was just a lad. Been wandering ever since.
No, I'm not. Looks like we've got something in common, then.
Hmph. No, friend, that I'm not. But I get along with your kind well enough.
Ha! And neither are you, eh? Good to see another Redguard. I like to think we bring a little heat to this frozen wasteland.
And if I surrender?
What who wanted?
That Empire is dead. And so are you.
It's a little more than a rebellion, don't you think?
We aren't the bad guys you know.
Well, Dragonborn. What do you say? Do you want the honor?
It makes us right.
Perhaps you're right. But then what does that make you?
Just kill him and let's be done with it already.
This is it for you. Any last words before I send you to Oblivion?
By the gods! If it's a good ending to some damn story you're after - perhaps the Dragonborn should be the one to do it.
As you wish.
Heh.
The Empire I remember never surrendered.
Enough... enough...
You just said it yourself.
Good point.
Maybe not, but you certainly aren't the good guys.
You realize this is exactly what they wanted.
So be it.
The Thalmor. They stirred up trouble here. Forced us to divert needed resources and throw away good soldiers quelling this rebellion.
Dragonborn. Here, use my sword to do it. This moment will be immortalized in song. Make it a good kill.
Come, Galmar. Where's your sense of the dramatic moment?
As you wish. This moment, we three, will be immortalized in song.
You want answers? Well so do I. So does everyone in this city.
A man goes crazy in the market. Everyone knows he's a Forsworn agent. Guards do nothing. Nothing but clean up the mess.
You're right, I saw it. Didn't want to believe my own eyes, is all.
A dragon... here in Skyrim. What's this world coming to? First the war, now dragons... trouble loves company, they say...
Suit yourself. I hate to turn away any friend of Hadvar, but in times like these... you understand if I can't just take your word for it.
What? A dragon? In Helgen?
That explains what I saw earlier... flying down the valley from the south... I was hoping I was wrong about what I thought it was...
Of course. Take what you need.
There's something you could do for me. For all of us here.
The Jarl needs to know if there's a dragon on the loose. Riverwood is defenseless...
We need to get word to Jarl Balgruuf in Whiterun to send whatever soldiers he can. If you'll do that for me, I'll be in your debt.
You lead, I'll follow.
Let's get going, then.
Still here.
I've got your back.
Damn. That's quite a sight.
That's...that's...wow.
By the Twin Moons...
Yuck. Something smells.
Trouble is in the air here.
I've got a bad feeling about this.
Keep your blade ready.
I don't like where this is going.
I have a feeling there's going to be trouble.
Get ready for a fight.
Keep your eyes open.
Don't like the looks of this.
Something smells foul.
Got a feeling there's trouble ahead.
Smell that? Blood.
I don't think I like where this is going.
Another filthy cave. Always so dark.
Hmm. Caves. Wonder what's below.
Hey look, a cave. Wonder what's inside?
Look, a cave. An exciting place to venture, if you know what you're doing.
Look at that, a cave. Who knows what could be down there?
Look, a cave. A fine place to find trouble.
A cave. Dark. Dangerous.
There's a cave. Tread carefully.
There's a cave here. Think we should check it out?
We've found a cave. I don't think there are warm fires and friendly faces inside.
There's a cave. Animals or bandits inside, no doubt.
A cave. Up for some hunting?
Cave up ahead. Trouble or maybe treasure?
Look. Nordic ruins. I hope there's gold inside.
Nordic ruins. Old. Secretive.
Is that one of the old ruins?
Old ruins. Nordic, I think.
My word, look at those ruins. Remarkable.
Look at those ruins. How long have they stood here, I wonder?
Old stonework. Gold, traps, and bones.
Nord stonework. Thousands of years old.
Look at those ruins. Think there's gold inside?
The old Nords sure did love their stonework.
Look at these stones. The ancestors knew how to build, no doubt.
Ruins ahead. Means old traps and bonewalkers.
Look. One of the ancient Nord ruins. Who knows what's down there?
The entrance to a dwarven city. All manner of strange traps and machines will be inside.
Is this that dwarven city? I hear the treasure from their ruins is worth a fortune.
Dwemer ruins. The people under the mountains. All gone now.
Look over there. That's a dwarven ruin.
Dwarven metalwork. So much wasted effort.
Oh, look. It's the entrance to a dwarven city. I've heard stories....
I dislike lurking near dwarven cities. It's like their ghosts are watching us.
Huh. Dwarven ruins. Strange places.
A Dwemer city. The lost people. The heretics.
Is that a dwarven city? I've never seen a dwarf, ever. Don't think anyone has.
Seems like a ruin. Dwarven, I think?
By the Mane, look. A dwarven city. Still sitting here after thousands of years.
Get nervous around dwarven stonework. Legends say these cities are haunted.
Old dwarven ruin. Look at that metalwork. Such a waste.
That's a dwarven city. I hear they built armor that walks like people.
Hey, there's a cave. Bad place to stumble drunk.
Look at those old stones. Ancient Nords sure could build.
Get a bad feeling.
I want to help you, Dragonborn. And I will. But I need your help first.
Now, what's this nonsense about trapping a dragon in my palace?
Ulfric and General Tullius are both just waiting for me to make a wrong move.
Do you think they will sit idle while a dragon is slaughtering my men and burning my city?
Spoken like a true Nord. I'll stand beside you, Dragonborn.
No Nord could have said it better! I'll stand beside you, Dragonborn.
I don't know about such things, but I heard the Greybeards summon you. That's good enough for me.
I didn't say anything about giving up.
We'll be ready, don't worry. You do have a plan for actually luring a dragon into this trap, don't you?
My men will be ready when you are, Dragonborn. You have a truce to arrange first, eh?
Then... Whiterun will stand with you, Dragonborn. We will take the risk, and gain the glory, whether it be in victory or defeat!
So what's the plan, then? How do you intend to lure a dragon into the trap?
Alduin? The World-Eater himself? But... how can we fight him? Doesn't his return mean it's the end times?
How can we fight Alduin? Doesn't his return mean it's the end times?
What you're asking for is insane. Impossible!
Why would I agree to let a dragon into the heart of my city, after working so hard to keep them out?
Aye, Dragonborn. Maybe you can stop the dragons - and this war into the bargain.
Need you to deliver some false orders to the Imperial Legate in Morthal.
But first we need to get our hands on some Imperial orders to make the forgeries.
Need you to deliver some false orders to the Stormcloak commander in Dawnstar.
Once you find the courier's route, kill him and take his place.
Need you to deliver some false orders to the Imperial Legate in Morthal.
Once you find the courier's route, kill him and take his place.
We understand the Nightgate and Roughwater inns are frequent stops for Stormcloak runners.
Set an ambush along the road between, or camp by one of the inns.
Bring whatever he's carrying to me, and I'll send you with convincing misinformation back to Dawnstar.
My scouts tell me the inns at Dragonbridge and Rorikstead are frequent stops for Imperial runners.
Set an ambush on the road between, or camp one of the inns.
Bring me whatever he's carrying, and I'll send you to Morthal with something convincing but full of lies.
Two things I have for you. Two shapes. One edged, one round.

Takes coin to pay a fine. And you don't have enough.
Ah, yes! There it is! Thank you so much.
Shor's blood, what have they done?
I think the Bards College might be looking for recruits. You should speak to the head of the college, Viarmo, if you're interested.

People say there's a man wandering the streets near the Blue Palace. He seems distressed but he's not making any sense.
When the Stormcloaks took Solitude, I thought for sure things would slow down. But they keep me just as busy as the Imperials.
Need a weapon? Hmmm... By the looks of ya, yeah... Think I've got somethin' you can use.
It's good to be away from the forge for a bit.
I think Bryling's the one you want to talk to. I'm mostly just here to scare away the riff raff.
It shouldn't have been like this.
I think I expected more.
Good riddance to bad rubbish I say.
He died well.
It's a smart man that demands something up front for his work. Some gold now and some when I have my dog. Here's the meat.
Ha! You've done it! Got to admit, I wasn't sure you could pull it off. But you've definitely earned your pay.
Did you see a hound on the road? A fine, strong creature that's been wandering near town.
There's one out on the road. I can't afford to chase him down but I could use a fierce, loyal beast to keep me company.
Yes, yes. Here it is. I think I'd best be off now.
Tell me, do you believe in mighty Arkay, god of life and death?
This is your last warning, outsider. We keep the peace, here. Stay out of our business.
Looks like you weren't followed. Good.
Once you've found out anything, come straight to me.
City's got eyes. Be careful when you're out there.
"Night of Tears", by Dranor Seleth. Old book, one I'd forgotten about. This thing here... it might be what the book was about.
Here's something to consider, Mirabelle.
He proposed that the real reason Saarthal was fought over by the Elves and the Nords was because something was unearthed there.
Let's not jump to conclusions. We'll do all the research we need to figure out what we're dealing with.
I don't see the relevance.
Something arcane. Very old, very powerful. Do you think...?
I feel better already.
Gods bless you, healer.
Thank you. The pain is already easing.
I'm afraid that's impossible.
Good, then you understand why this is about more than just Mercer's lust for power.

Karliah and I will meet you there. Prepare yourself, lass. This will be a fight to remember!
Although it would benefit the Guild more than you can imagine, in the end, it would do more harm than good.
Gunnar, take care of the boy. I have to find General Tullius and join the defense.
I wish Elgrim had spent more time tutoring me then that Ingun girl.
If there's anything you need, I'd be glad to help you.
My husband Bersi will be the death of me. He spends too much time worrying about Riften and not enough about himself.
Can you believe my former husband used to give most of our gold to the poor?
If you're looking for a handout, you'll want to speak with my husband.
Horses are all I know. I've been riding since I could walk.
If the forest wasn't so dangerous, I'd spend every spare moment riding.
Your skin's as pale as the snow. You scared of sunlight, or something?
Don't like those eyes you got. There's a bad hunger to them.
Vampire! Burn it!
Vampire! To arms!
Burn it! Burn the vampire!
Vampire!
