What do you want? Haven't you done enough damage to Markarth?
I don't think we have anything to discuss, Stormcloak.
You lay one finger on Igmund, and I'll end you Stormcloak.
Not long, in truth. I came to Skyrim after I found myself unwelcome in both Elsweyr and Cyrodiil.
I seem to have an unfortunate talent for getting myself involved in misunderstandings with the law.
Ri'saad was able to look past that, and it was he who helped to set me up with a trade caravan. Now I work for him.
Mostly it is the Nords. They do not like outsiders in their land, and so we are forbidden to enter the cities.
When they look upon us, they see only pickpockets and skooma dealers. It is most unfair, but we do our best to ignore them.
An astute question, for we are far from home and this is a cold, hard land.
The wise trader finds the best opportunities, even if he must travel far to find them. Skyrim is a ripe opportunity indeed.
The dragons and the war have scared many other traders away, but for those with courage, there is much profit to be made.
The Khajiit hail from a distant land called Elsweyr, bordered on the north by Cyrodiil and the south by the glistening blue waters of the sea.
Elsweyr is an arid land of deserts and rocky canyons, where the sun shines warmly, always.
There are cities so ancient, the sands have swallowed them whole. But now I will say no more, for I miss my home greatly.
Quite the opposite, in fact.
The soldiers are some of our best customers, for we offer supplies and wares they cannot obtain by other means.
It was Ri'saad's idea to come here now, while the land is in turmoil. Where others saw only danger, he saw opportunity.
It is Ri'saad who owns the three trading caravans that travel the roads of Skyrim.
Long ago, I was an orphan on the streets of Corinthe. I stowed away aboard a trade ship that brought me here. It was Ri'saad who found me.
He provided the funds to start this caravan, and put me in charge of it. I owe him more than I could ever hope to repay.
Tools, wares and weapons, all for sale at fair prices.
Goods and wares of all kinds, and all can be yours!
Come! Come and see what goods I offer.
Far have I traveled to bring you these fine goods. Come and see what I offer.
He hired the others and paid for my goods. I am allowed to run the caravan, but I pay Ri'saad a percentage of my profits.
I say this without bitterness, for it was Ri'saad who saved me from a life of servitude aboard a trading ship.
I am grateful to have the life that I do now. It is a far cry from my days as an orphan on the streets of Corinthe.
Don't let the shop's name frighten you away. I have plenty of reliable tonics and healing potions to sell.
A strong drink will chase off that chill in the air.
I've half a mind to ask if you'll take me on one of your adventures, but I can't just leave Valga on her own.
Not much exciting happens around here. You're as like as not to be bored to death if you stay too long.
May your road lead you to warm sands.
I hope that you will come to do business with us again.
May the sun keep you warm even in this land of bitter cold.
Until our next meeting, if such is fated.
I hope you will find a warmer welcome in your travels than we have found in ours.
The roads of Skyrim can be treacherous. If you must walk them, be wary.
So many refuse to talk to us. They call us thieves and smugglers. I am glad to see that you are not such a one.
Welcome. If I cannot serve you, I am sure that one of my other traders can do so.
I have traveled far across Tamriel to serve you.
Perhaps you have encountered Ma'dran or Akhari? Both are sworn to me, and both have worthy goods to offer.
The road makes this one weary.
Wind guide you.
Sky guard you.
When I was young, our leaders became obsessed with the Thalmor threat.
They believed that if and when we found a Dragonborn, we would need to protect him against the Thalmor.
You were given this gift by the gods for a reason. It is up to you to figure out how best to use it.
Turns out, we fatally underestimated the Thalmor. They smashed us with ease during the Great War.
I was one of the few who escaped. For a long time, all I cared about was staying alive, and taking revenge on the Thalmor when I could.
But then the dragons returned. And I remembered that the Blades used to be dragonslayers.
And that we were sworn to protect the Dragonborn, the greatest dragonslayer of all.
I expected better from you Dragonborn. I came here in good faith, and now it seems you help the Empire at every turn.
You disappoint me, Dragonborn. I accepted your invitation on trust in your good name. But it seems you intend to favor Ulfric.
You did the best you could, Dragonborn. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
Are you one of them? Please don't hurt me.
Skjor was looking for you, earlier.
I'm sorry, just... Just don't hurt me. I know you're not one of them, but you just can't help them. You can't let them know I'm here.
Please, will you help me? There's no one here I can trust.
I mean it! I'll... I'll cut you in half!
So, are you working with them? You think you can take me? You so much as touch me, and you're going to lose fingers.
Let's see. Ah, so that's what happened to Staubin. Tragic, but I warned him he was leading those people on a fool's errand.
Unfortunately, sometimes the stone of scholarship is built on the foundation of death. I'll inform the kin.
Here, I usually pay researchers for their work when they emerge from the ruins, but they won't be needing it anymore.
You know, I was there for that whole sordid affair. Marvelous time! Butterflies, blood, a Fox, a severed head... Oh, and the cheese! To die for.
Some of these Nords will come up with any excuse to despise us.
And it isn't just the dark elves they hate -- they make a target of the Argonians as well.
In fact, just about anyone who isn't a Nord is fair game for their bullying.
Curse you!
You just sit tight. I'll get around to it eventually.
Right, right... Oh, look at that. I seem to have lost my keys. Might take me a while to find them.
I said my fine has been paid! Now let me out!
Sorry, my ears aren't so good. You say somethin'?
Guard! My fine's been paid, now set me free!
Please, we need to speak in private.
Quickly, come with me.
Find me the moment the Alik'r are taken care of.
I have faith in you.
But, if you find more trouble than you can handle, run out to the courtyard. We'll come as soon as we hear fighting.
Talos preserve you.
We found a cave under the lake. With luck, it'll lead into the prison.
I need you to sneak in there, and free our men, killing anyone inside as you go. Once you've rescued the prisoners, head out to the courtyard.
We'll stand guard out here and rush in as soon as we hear fighting.
Should be fun.
There's a grate outside the wall. Looks like it used to be buried in snow. I don't think they know it's there.
From the look of it, I'd bet it leads straight into the prison.
I need you to sneak in there, and free our men, killing anyone inside as you go. Once you've rescued the prisoners, head out to the courtyard.
We'll stand guard out here and rush in as soon as we hear fighting.
Break our men out and head to the courtyard. We'll meet you there.
You go report our success. I'll set up a garrison here. A shame none of the prisoners made it, but their names will be remembered.
Glad you were here. Couldn't have done it without you!
Great work! Get back and report our success. I'll stay here and clean up the mess.
Glad you were here. Couldn't have done it without you!
Calcelmo is stubborn as a mule. Be careful when you speak to him.
Working with the Guild again is the best decision I've ever made. Protection, respect and a constant flow of coin... it couldn't get any better.
Best of all, there's no need to smuggle things quietly anymore. With the influence we have in Solitude, I can march it right out the front door.
How could I complain when I went from Sabjorn's lackey to one of the wealthiest merchants in Whiterun in no time at all?
Plus, the meadery's doing so well, I'm almost having trouble keeping up with the demand.
I'd say things couldn't be any better.
Business? Business is actually inconsequential to me. I share little of your lust for coin.
However, as promised, I'll still be more than happy to take any stolen merchandise off your hands.
I've been setting up some open trading with Tonilia back at the Guild in Riften, and it's proven to be quite profitable.
There's been no word from the remnants of the Summerset Shadows, so I'm assuming you either eliminated all of them or they've fled Skyrim.
Everything seems to be working out nicely, wouldn't you agree?
Business is absolutely booming, my friend.
I've taken to hiring a few extra men of my own just to keep up with all the shipments.
Old Delvin certainly takes care of his own, doesn't he.
If trying to rid yourself of stolen goods becomes a burden, and you find yourself in Winterhold, visit me at the College.
I've been known to handle items of questionable interest from time to time and I'll see what I can do.
If you ever manage to gain entry to the College, and you find trying to rid yourself of stolen goods becoming a burden, come visit me.
I've been known to handle items of questionable interest from time to time and I'll see what I can do.
Just remember me if you get your hands on anything else.
Do me a favor. If you speak to Maven, tell her everything's well at hand down here, okay?
Give my regards to Karliah if you see her.
Hurry back... and bring more merchandise.
When you get back to Riften, tell Delvin this new arrangement's working out quite well.

Come to sell me something special, or did you just want to look through my merchandise?

Brought me more than some Firebrand Wine I trust?

I'm assuming you're here for more than a social call. Down to business, eh?
I'm always on the lookout for all sorts of goods. Bring me whatever you... well, "find" and I'll see what I can do.
So, did Delvin send you again or you here on your own this time?
In trouble with the Guild, eh? Well, looks like we won't be trading merchandise until you smooth it over.
I've got all sorts of interesting things for you to buy, and I assure you they're of the finest quality.
If you've got the time to sell, I've got the time to buy.
Let's see what you have for me today. Something simple to turn around for a profit I trust.
I've been forbidden to deal with you until amends are made with the Guild. Sorry.
Now I know you've come by for more than just a visit. What did you bring?

Thanks!
Let's teach these bastards a lesson.
All right!
Last but not least. Let's go!
Hey!
Get me out of here!
Hurry!
The guard has the key.
Perhaps dating to just after the Dragon War. If so, I could use this to cross-reference the names with other later texts.
I should come along. I would very much like to see this dragon.

Don't worry, my lord. I'm the very soul of caution.
Tell him what you told me. About the dragon.
So, Irileth tells me you came from the western watchtower?

Yes, yes. Don't worry. Although the chance to see a living dragon up close would be tremendously valuable...
You should come, too.

Farengar, you need to come at once. A dragon's been sighted nearby.
As you command.
Farengar!
Good. I'm glad you're making progress. My employers are anxious to have some tangible answers.

What did it do? Is it attacking the watchtower?
Uh... that's right. We saw it coming from the south. It was fast... faster than anything I've ever seen.
Let's go.
I feel better already.
Gods bless you, healer.
Thank you. The pain is already easing.
The pain's a little less than before, but it still hurts bad enough.
It's not as bad as before, but I still can't get a good night's sleep.
I ain't going to lie, it's still pretty bad.
The fever's down a bit, an' I can almost see straight.
I still can't hold nothin' down, but it's better than yesterday.
My joints ache, I can't eat nothin' and my head feels like a furnace. I've seen better days.
I have things I should see to, but go ahead.
I'll tell you more about it when we are nearby.

Thanks again for your help.
We should get after Vals Veran before he does more damage here.

Quickly, follow me!
Sure.
Khajiit walks in your footsteps.
I will walk alongside you.
To the next fight.
Lead the way.
(woof!)
It will be done.
As you say, it will happen.
Then that is my task.
Will do.
(panting)
Is that all?
Do you need anything else?
We done?
That it?
I'll shoulder some of your burden.
Need me to carry something?
Fine. Let's trade, then.
What do you need to take?
J'zargo is listening.
What do you need?
Go ahead. What is it?
What is it?
Fine. What?
Not for you. Not for anyone.
Even Khajiit have their limits. No.
Maybe you want to rethink what you're asking?
I will not drag my honor through the mud for you, or anyone.
Forget it. I don't do that kind of work.
Let's see it.
Point the way.
Let us see it, then.
As you will.
Right, then.
Then back home is where you'll find me.
I'll be heading home, then, if you need me.
You need my strength again, I'll be home.
I'm heading home if you need me.
(bark)
What is it?
What do you want?
Show me.
What?
Did you require something of J'zargo?
Do you still need me to do something?
You wanted me to do something, yes?
You still wish something of me?
Still have something you need?
Then lead on, friend.
We are in this together, yes?
Lead, then. You have my steel.
Let's not waste any time.
You have someone with you, already.
You have someone already, friend.
You already have someone to do your fighting for you. Don't need me.
Looks like you got someone already.
Lead on. I'm behind you.
To Oblivion and back, as they say.
Ours is to follow, friend.
Khajiit guards your back.
If anyone sneaks up on us, I'll smell them coming. Or I might not. We'll see.
You're taking us somewhere warm, I trust?
An Orc follows to the death.
My blood's calm. I prefer it boiling.
You have a grim look.
We've stopped. What is it?
I'm still here.
Let's get going.
Ready to get moving?
Whenever you're ready to leave.
You're ready to get going?
Shall we get moving?
We going?
You are ready to move, yes?
We are leaving?
Waiting.
Still standing here.
We'll be waiting, then.
I will wait.
Back to the great task of waiting.
I'll be right here, then.
I'm ready, let's continue.
We follow, then.
Khajiit will follow.
Lead on, friend.
May the Hist guide us.
Let's tread softly.
Lead. I'll follow.
Malacath witness our deeds.
Lead us to our next task.
Go where the challenge is greatest.
Let's go.
Lead on, then.
Following.
Oh Listener. Can't you see that's impossible?
Not possible.
That can't be done.
Can't do it.
No. Not possible.
All right.
As you wish.
Very well.
Fine.
Your call.
Head on, then. I'll keep an eye out.
I'll keep watch from here.
You sure? Fine. I'll stand watch.
Go on ahead. I'll stand guard.
Actually, I want you to bring him something. You see, the caravans are notorious for transporting illegal substances.
Present Ri'saad with this satchel of Moon Sugar, and I bet my last septim he'll make a deal.
Good, I'll contact some of my people and have them prepare some shipments right away.
Good job. Here, let me give you something for all the leg work.
I'm not sure if you've noticed, but there are several Khajiit Caravans that travel across the realm.
They're shrewd traders and don't mind getting their hands dirty. I've bartered with their leader, Ri'saad, on more than one occasion.
Ah, Moon Sugar. I could smell it on you. I'm pleased with this offer, and accept.
Take my word to the leaders of your guild and tell them we look forward to a prosperous and profitable future.
If you happen upon any of our caravans in your travels, we'd also be more than willing to pay you a fair sum for any of your stolen goods.
Well, as you know, the Guild's growing and things are looking up around here.
The only thing we're lacking is a reliable way to transport our merchandise across Skyrim.
Before we get down to business, I've got something I need you to do.
Yes, I've heard the Guild is rising back to power. An alliance would be most beneficial to both parties.
I'll consider the offer, but I'll have to discuss it with the other caravans.
You approach as if you know us, stranger. Who are you and what do you want?
Ri'saad is the head of a small syndicate of independent merchant families.
Each caravan is obliged to pay dues, but in return a small portion of the profits are shared between them and the leader parcels out the routes.
If we could make contact with them, they could provide an excellent way to inconspicuously transport goods for us.
Give my regards to Tonilia. Tell her I'll contact her soon.
You'll find Ri'saad with his caravan group. Just don't do anything stupid when you get there.
Was there anything else, or is our transaction complete?
Did Ri'saad accept our offer?
It's an illegal substance highly favored by the Khajiit. Very difficult to come by.
If Moon Sugar is refined properly, it can be used to make skooma, another substance that will knock you over after a single draught.
Look at that. Am I drunk? I must be drunk.
The cemetery is ancient. It's been here longer than the town.
I'm no scholar, but I know that a lot of battles have been fought here over the course of history.
After each battle, more dead were buried, and the graveyard got bigger. I've heard it's now the biggest graveyard in Skyrim.
Yes, and I couldn't tell you why.
My wife and I think there's some dark magic at work. Or perhaps Arkay's influence is strong here, and he likes to keep it this way.
Oh, merciful Arkay, no! I am too old and frail, and this graveyard is too large.
Kust is my assistant. He helps me tend the headstones and keep our cemetery clean, as befits a place of rest.
Kust is responsible for the cemetery here in Falkreath, I take care of the Shrine.
Call upon me if I can be of any service.
Browse as much as you like, please.
Look around all you want, but don't try anything funny.
Hey! You planning to steal something? Don't even think about it.
Steal anything from my shop, and you'll regret it.
Be seein' you, stranger.
You hear any juicy gossip in town, be sure to share it with me!
I'll be right here if you need anything else.
See me at my forge if you need arms or armor.
Good steel's worth every septim. Remember that.
Life is far too short, my friend. Don't waste it!
We'll all meet Arkay sooner or later. Best to enjoy the time we've got.
Good to talk to you.
You'll meet a lot of tongue-wagging gossips in this town. Try not to take them too seriously.
Mind what you say! The Empire has ears all over this town.
Mark my words, I'll be Jarl again someday!
May wisdom forever light your path.
Be safe, traveler.
Don't judge Dengeir too harshly. He is old, but he means well.
May your time in Falkreath be pleasant.
Come and see me again sometime. I might have some use for you.
We're done here, I think.
Keep in song in your heart to ward off the gloom.

Court is in session.

Did you find anything at Wolfskull Cave?

I suppose if you're truly interested you could talk to Falk. He's the better-safe-than-sorry type.
Been up to the Blue Palace? It's a sight to see. Even if it is filled with fops...
We couldn't have taken this city without you, sir.
We couldn't have taken this city without you, ma'am.
Everything's in order.
Solitude is as glorious as they said it was. Nothing like Falkreath where I grew up.
You'll pay off your debt to the Reach. Just work and you'll earn your freedom.
There's yet hope that our relationship with the College can be repaired.
Keep going! We need to get farther away from here!
Let's get out of here, fast as we can!
Now you die!
You should never have come here!
It's so nice to have a visitor.
I get so lonely out here.
It's tough, just scraping by out here. But I make do.
We are used to the cycle of gain and loss. It is no trouble.

Bandits harass the caravans at every step, but Khajiit usually scare them off.
Milk drinker.

Good work. Take this.

Are you loyal to Skyrim? Break into Lod's house and get that letter.

Good.

If you find Staubin, it would be good to see any notes he may have collected. I doubt you'll find him alive, though.

I don't have any money to give you. but I can show you a few tricks I learned playing for the Winterhold wizards.
Larina. That fool student of mine stole my flute and sold it to some necromancer.
I just got a ransom note for it. That flute has been handed down for seventeen generations!
Well don't just stand there. Go get my flute!
That damn fool! I should ring her neck!
At last! I have searched for this drum for 20 years.
The college treasury is a bit thin right now. But I can show you some tricks I learned from my days with the army.
What? You did? Oh sweet Divines! You did!
There is no way I can pay you what this Lute is worth. But I can teach you a few tricks I've learned over the years.
It makes me sad whenever I think about it.
You're joking, right? Rjorn? Only the most famous battle drummer of the second age. Well, famous among bards at least.
That drum has been in half a dozen famous battles. It's a priceless artifact.
Rjorn was always looking for new stories to tell. It seems he was writing a lay about Halldir when he vanished. That was the missing clue.
Rjorn's drum! Nobody knows where he died, and therefore where his drum might be.
Halldir was the missing link. Rjorn entered Halldir's Cairn in secret, and presumably died there.
Now all I need to do is find someone to get it. Wait. You. You could do it. I need you to get me that drum.
Finn was the bard who invented the eight course lute that is commonplace today.
Until he found a way to string the extra courses, bards only had six course lutes.
The lute they stole is Finn's original eight course lute. I doubt they even realize its true value.
They found it! They actually found it!
Apparently Larina told him some story about the flute being able to make the dead dance.
It's just a flute. A really old and valuable flute, but just a flute.
Hmm? Oh, sorry. My mind wanders sometimes. I was just thinking about Finn's Lute.
Over a year ago, thieves broke into the college and made off with a lot of valuable things. Mostly gold and silver.
But they also stole Finn's Lute. We just learned where the bandits fled to, and I very much want that lute back.
Please. If we have to negotiate the terms of the negotiation, we will never get anywhere.
This is how the Empire repays us for our loyalty?
Now that everyone is here, please take your seats so we can begin.
She's part of the Imperial delegation. You can't dictate who I bring to this council.
Well said.
I have every right to be at this negotiation. I need to ensure that nothing is agreed to here that violates the terms of the White-Gold Concordat.
Diplomatic as usual.
Well said.
Yes!
Hear, hear!
Yes, my lord.
Maybe so, but bringing her here is a deliberate provocation. Tullius needs to know I won't be pushed around.
That didn't take long.
Peace, Galmar.
Besides, Jarl Laila will never agree to...
Your chief Talos-hunter?
You are weak. Sooner you realize it, the sooner we can see the back of you.
Ulfric, you can't be taking this demand seriously? We can hold Riften against anything the Empire can throw at it.
No. You insult us by bringing her to this negotiation?
Vignar. We will do whatever I decide is in the best interests of Skyrim. Are we clear?
What do you think? I can't let Ulfric dictate terms to me before the negotiations even start.
By Ysmir's beard, the nerve of those Imperial bastards, eh?
To think that I would sit down at the same table with that... Thalmor bitch. Either she walks or I do.
This council is unprecedented. We are gathered here at the Dragonborn's request.
I ask that you all respect the spirit of High Hrothgar, and do your best to begin the process of achieving a lasting peace in Skyrim.
Jarl Ulfric.
General Tullius.
Take your seat and we can begin.
We all wait upon you, Dragonborn.
Why delay? Please, take your seat so we can begin.
Perhaps this would be a good time to get the Dragonborn's input on this matter.
By Talos, the stones on this one! You're in no position to dictate terms to us, Tullius!
Maybe so, but I can't be making concessions before negotiations even start. It makes the Empire look weak.
I hope that we have all come here in the spirit of...
And so we meet again. But this time I know who and what you really are.
I'll give you credit for boldness. But associating yourself with the Blades is a choice you will come to regret.
I'm here to ensure that General Tullius does not exceed his authority.
You gave Ulfric more than he deserved. This will certainly prolong the war.
These terms are satisfactory to my government. The truce is unlikely to last long in any case.
I'm here because it's required of me. But there's nothing to be gained by talking to that murderer.
I don't know why you gave Ulfric so much. That murderer will never rule Skyrim.
I'm glad you were able to negotiate a truce, but I don't trust Ulfric. He'll break his word as soon as it suits him.
We should get started.
There ain't much more to tell... they're haunted and you should stay away.
Look, I've seen one of the spirits with my very own eyes. When it glared at me, I swear it burned right through my soul.
Don't blame you one bit.
I just said that to make the poor guy feel better. I'm pretty sure she's dead.
Reyda would gather ingredients from the small island in the river east of here. Then one day, she just vanished.
I tried to look for her, but she never turned up.
Are you kidding? Those damn things will drive me right out of business!
Tell you what. Bring me ten of their pelts from anywhere in Skyrim I'll gladly pay you for thinning out the herd.
Well, least I can do is give you something for taking care of him. If you won't accept it as a payment, consider it a gift.
Reyda! You saw Reyda? Did you tell her Narfi cries? Did you tell her Narfi never said goodbye like mother and father?
Reyda! You saw Reyda? Did you tell her Narfi cries? Did you tell her Narfi never said goodbye like mother and father?
If you think there's anything you can do, be my guest.
He's harmless. He's been in a state ever since his sister Reyda disappeared over a year ago.
He just keeps to himself in what's left of his folk's farmhouse across the river.
With father I said goodbye... with mother I said goodbye. Reyda leaves and Narfi can't say goodbye.
Makes Narfi very, very sad. Narfi needs Reyda to say goodbye.
Let me see that!
...I can't believe this...
It was all just a fabrication of this Wyndelius character? I can't believe we were so stupid.
Fortunately, they seem to be sticking to the barrow. I think they're guarding it.
Certainly isn't helping my business any; who'd want to rent a room anywhere near a haunted barrow?
Have you ever seen what a bear does to the trees? They jump up on their hind legs and scratch them to bits... marking their territory or something.
It's getting to the point where I have to scour Skyrim for untouched trees at the right size. Costs me too much time and money.
You've made Narfi so happy! Narfi now waits for his sister until she comes home.
At least Narfi has Reyda's Necklace... reminds Narfi of his sister. Thank you for giving this to Narfi.
I've seen some sort of a cave entrance over there. Folks call it "Geirmund's Hall," but I don't know why.
Probably best if you avoid it for now... it didn't seem to do Reyda any good.
Let me see that!
...I can't believe this...
It was all just a fabrication of this Wyndelius character? I can't believe we were so stupid.
Nice to finally meet someone who can follow simple instructions.
Here's your reward, as promised. Now if you'll excuse me, I have to get back to work.
Reyda was here, then gone. Went to gather plants and never came home... nope, nope.
Everyone looked and no one could find her. Wilhelm said she'll be back... told Narfi not to worry... Reyda will come back.
Oh no! No, no, no. Narfi never got to say goodbye! Now Narfi's all alone.
At least Narfi has Reyda's Necklace... reminds Narfi of his sister. Thank you for giving this to Narfi.
About a year or two ago, some fella named Wyndelius came through; said he was some kind of a treasure hunter.
I warned him not to go in there, just like I warned you. The very next night we heard screams from the barrow, and that was it.
We never saw him again.
You again. Guess you need another lesson, huh?
He is quick to anger, and slow to forgive. One who wrongs Malacath is one who will endure great torment in return.
He demands loyalty and strength from his orcs. We have tried to please him, but he is now angry with us, and so we suffer.
You do not know of Malacath? He is the keeper of oaths, the master of curses.
You are an Orc, but do not know of Malacath, our master and yours?
We have been on our own for some time now. I fear we will not survive.
Please hurry. We are counting on you.
No, don't go! You may not be one of our tribe, but you are an Orc nonetheless!
Malacath has heard my pleas! He speaks to us!
You have returned. Will you now aid me in contacting Malacath?
Weeks? Longer? It feels like an age has gone by. I haven't slept in so long.
Oh, of course! Because Malacath would take pity on us if only a useless outsider would lend a hand.
We have sworn loyalty to Yamarz. He will see us through these dark times.
By the Ashpit, we can't go on like this.
I would be honored. Beyond honored to have such a thing here at the College.
This opens up entire new realms of research for us. Oh, if only Septimus were here to see this!
Thank you. Please, take this. It can't quite compare to an Elder Scroll, but still. You will be remembered for generations for this.
Wait. Are you... Are you serious?
You have an Elder Scroll? You?
You... Really? An Elder Scroll?
Very funny. Did Enthir put you up to this?
Fair enough. You claim to be neutral, but it seems that you actually favor the Empire. Still...
Oh? True, I may meet my end in a dark alley with a dagger in my back. But the people have seen the truth, and they will not back down.
It seems cowardly.
Oh, he's serious. But so am I.
I have shown the people that when our Jarls drink the Empire's milk, it makes us weak.
I must now show them the path that will lead us back to our strength.
There is no progress without sacrifice. No wheat without threshing the chaff.
The Empire and the Jarls who back them must be swept away. The people demand it. I demand it.
You plan to march on Windhelm?
As in all things, Lord, caution... I urge us to wait and see.
Then might I urge you to consider General Tullius's request? I mean, if you are bent on offending Jarl Ulfric...
Torygg? He simply walked up to the boy and murdered him!
You there.
I have a message for you to deliver to our friend, the esteemed Jarl of Windhelm.
Proventus. Bring me my pen. And the good parchment.
The chests of gold didn't hurt.
It's time to act.
You've returned with my axe. I knew that would be his response.
As soon as you left I sent word to General Tullius, who's been kind enough to lend us some of his troops and Legate Cipius here.
Let Ulfric try to make it past our combined forces.
I'll turn you back over to your Legion. Legate Cipius will have use for you. Gods be with us all.
Blocking Pale Pass would cut one of the main Imperial supply routes... and would leave Whiterun dangerously exposed...
Is that so? I've been wondering when he'd come around.
Hrongar carries on the policies of his brother and refuses to acknowledge the Legions right to garrison troops in his city.
On the plus side, he also refuses to acknowledge Ulfric's claim.
I'm not the High King, but neither am I a boy.
If Ulfric wants to challenge my rule in the old way, let him.
Though I suspect he'll prefer to send his "Stormcloaks" to do it for him.
How long have you been in Skyrim? Yes. Give the man my axe.
If he returns it to you it means we have business to settle. If he keeps it, then we are at peace.
And what harm is there in letting a few legionnaires die in place of your own men?
Yes, to General Tullius.
I need to make a few things clear before I accept these Legionnaires of his.
Lord, wait. Let us see if Ulfric is serious.
I'm of a mind with Irileth.
Sooner than you think.
Proventus, what do you make of all this? If Ulfric were to attack Whiterun...
Deliver this axe to Ulfric Stormcloak.
Don't be daft. Proventus is my eyes.
Just give me the letter. I presume once I have it, I can do as I please with it? Good.
I can see now that this is not a negotiation at all.
He was rather straight forward with Torygg.
Was it cowardly then to accept the White-Gold Concordat?
True. He's already proven his personal strength. Now he seeks to prove his army's.
Finally.
Here we are. Solitude. Safe and sound, as always.
Men who understand one another need not waste words.
There are but a few simple truths behind one warrior giving another his axe.
Ulfric will know my meaning.
Good.
Good...
Oh. What's this?
Ahhh... You're quite brave to carry such a message. It's a pity you've chosen the wrong side...
You can return this axe to the man who sent it. And tell him he should prepare to entertain... visitors.
I expect a great deal of excitement in the city of Whiterun in the near future...
I'm not a fool, Proventus. I mean it's time to challenge Ulfric to face me as a man, or to declare his intentions.
This again?! That was different. Was I given a chance to object to the terms of the treaty? No.
The Jarls weren't asked. We were told. And we had to like it.
This again?! That was different. Was Balgruuf given a chance to object? No. The Jarls weren't asked. He was told. And he had to like it.
Prey waits.
That "boy" was High King of Skyrim.
Are we writing a letter, Lord?
Jarl Hrongar...
Damnit! This isn't about gold!
Now, I believe you have some business to conclude with my court wizard?
Now get out of here and to the outpost! I need to know what's happening out there.
Hmm... these are interesting reports...
Ulfric is the one who has offended.
It's time to decide.
No doubt requesting to garrison his men in my castle. How many times must I deny him?
Well? Out with it.
We can talk about that once this dragon is dealt with.
Ah yes. I've studied those documents... Very interesting...
He'll do no such thing!
Until that dragon's dealt with, I don't care what Tullius wants.
For now, give the papers to my Steward.
I see... Give the papers to my Steward.
But, Proventus has a point. Ulfric has made it clear. In his mind, to refuse his claim is to side with the Empire.
End of the road. Markarth, right on schedule.
Here we are. Whiterun. Everybody out.
This is it. Riften, jewel of Skyrim. End of the road.
We made it. Windhelm. Hope you enjoyed the ride.
Not enough to outweigh the loss of Riften. The whole south of Eastmarch would be open to Imperial attack.
Not enough to outweigh the loss of Markarth. With the Reach in enemy hands, our whole position in Solitude would be threatened.
The circle of stones to the southwest? That's Secunda's Kiss. The giants hold it sacred, so tread carefully.
You know, I saw something near Shimmermist Cave, to the northeast. Some kind of creature... small and wicked. Never seen its like before.
If you're heading east, steer clear of Hillgrund's Tomb. Thing about those old crypts - the dead having trouble staying that way.
Looking to do some hunting? Avoid Brittleshin Pass. It's southeast of here.
Yesterday, I saw a dragon fly right over the city. Headed northeast. Toward Shearpoint, by the looks of it.
By Shor, you're hauling around a lot of junk. Best stop in at the Arnleif and Sons Trading Company, sell it off.
Let me guess - you need a drink. It's the Silver-Blood Inn you're looking for.
Break the law in Markarth, and it's off to Cidhna Mine with you.
Lots of nasty holes in the Reach, and Blind Cliff Cave is one of them. A filthy Hagraven nest, northeast of here.
There's an evil to the north, friend, and its name is Ragnvald. A tomb like so many in Skyrim, where the dead have found no peace.
Travel southeast and you'll find an Orc stronghold, up around the old Dushnikh orichalcum mine.
Beware the region northeast, across the river. Forsworn have taken refuge in Broken Tower Redoubt and Red Eagle Redoubt.
There's a small skeever den just east of the city, at Reachwater Rock.
The Forsworn have been spotted to the north, near Bruca's Leap Redoubt.
If it's arms or armor you need, see Beirand in Castle Dour. Bloody good blacksmith, that one.
If you need potions, head over to Angeline's Aromatics.
You can sell off that junk at Bits and Pieces.
The Winking Skeever's got warm beds and cold mead. You look like you could use both.
I don't abide fancy clothes, but you look like you might. I hear Radiant Raiments has a large selection.
Off the coast a ways to the east, you'll find the wreck of the Brinehammer. Never know what treasures may still be aboard.
You heard of Ravenscar Hollow? Cave on the island to the west. Place is a roost for those foul Hagravens.
If you must venture southwest, stay clear of Volskygge. It is a cursed place, a tomb where the dead still live...
The road to Whiterun's not safe these days. Lots of travelers just... disappear, south of Dragon Bridge. Can't find a trace of them.
Pinemoon Cave is down to the southwest, but it ain't fit for exploring. There are stories... Vampires, some say...
You could say the Ratway is the city under the city. Dark, dangerous, and no place for decent folk.
There's an apothecary in town, yeah. But it's down on the lower walkway, so stay sharp if you go down there.
There's fine steel at the Scorched Hammer, if you fancy a new blade.
Need a drink, you go to the Bee and Barb. Stay out of the Ragged Flagon, stay out of the Ratway.
A pleasure, thane.
If it's a bed you need, talk to Keerava in the Bee and Barb. She'll set you right.
City got attacked once by those damn bandits, come east across the lake, from Faldar's Tooth. They won't be trying that again.
Best not go wandering east of the city. Bandits been seen around Broken Helm Hollow.
Talk of strange happenings at Autumnshade Clearing, just north of the lake. My guess? Spriggans.
There's a foul presence coming from Darklight Tower, southwest of the city. Some say the place is home to necromancers.
If you journey north, keep well away from Snapleg Cave. Damned Hagravens have infested the place.
By the gods, a dragon's been sighted just south of the city, flying around Lost Tongue Overlook.
You should unload some of that unwanted gear. Head over to Sadri's Used Wares, talk to the Elf.
Stay away from the Aretino Residence. The place is cursed.
If you need an apothecary, the White Phial has respectable stock.
By the gods, you're diseased. Get yourself to the White Phial and purchase a remedy, before you infect us all.
The Dunmer get their drinks at the New Gnisis Cornerclub. Why they can't just call it a tavern is beyond me...
If it's bed or board you're after, Candlehearth Hall's got both.
Across the river to the northeast lies Yngol Barrow. Steer clear of that foul place, lest the walking dead claim you as their own.
The Eye of Talos was a fine ship, once. Now it lies wrecked, off an island just north of here.
Maybe you've seen masts sticking out of the water to the northeast? That would be the wreck of the Winter War. Fine vessel, back in its day.
South of here, and west of the road, lies Cronvangr Cave. Stay away from that cursed place, and the vampires that call it home.
Tell me you've seen them. Those... things. Small and evil, like something out of a nightmare. They come from Stillborn Cave, to the north. I know it.
I've no problem with your kind, Orc. But that stronghold you've got in the southeast? Narzulbur? It's an affront to my people.
I've no problem with Orcs. But that stronghold they've got in the southeast? Narzulbur? It's an affront to my people.
You hear the news? There's a dragon roosting up on Mount Anthor, to the northwest.
Him? Oh... Ah, name's Balablob or Malaclob, one of them funny Orc names. Talks real good, though. Not a savage at all.
Said he's a writer. Don't know what kind of job that is, but it must earn him some pretty coin. He's paid up for the next few months.
He mostly just hangs about. Goes down to the lake, sometimes samples the stores of wine in the cellar. Man can do whatever he pleases, far as I care.
You see any other inns around here? Where else would I go to drink?
Nah, not so much. The odd traveler on the road. But mostly just old Fultheim, come to drink away a lifetime of bad memories, I'd wager.
Course there's the Orc. Long-term tenant, that one. For what he pays, I could afford to shut this place down.
This old place? Been here forever. Built by my great grandda. Run by him, then all the way up the line to me.
Ah, hello there, traveler. Come to the Nightgate for food or lodging?
Need a room? Or maybe just some mead?
Something I can get for ya?
I can't help you, friend. Talk to Hadring, the Innkeeper.
I value my privacy. So if you don't mind?
I really don't mean to be rude, friend, but I'm not feeling particularly sociable...
Hadring got himself another customer, eh?
The beds and beer are both lousy, you ask me.
What do you want?
Well he don't like company, I can tell you that much. Just wants to be left alone. But no... that's not really it.
It's like... he wants to talk. Likes people and all. But he stays separate, because he's supposed to.
Kind of sad, really.
Anything you need, just holler.
Sure, walk away. I wasn't talking or anything...
Really? That would be kind of you.
Here, take this bag of supplies. At the top of the steps you'll see the offering chest. Just leave the bag inside and you're done.
Well, it's kind of an understanding between us. I mean, it just wouldn't feel right to charge them for a bit of preserved food.
Trouble is, my legs aren't what they used to be and climbing the 7,000 Steps takes its toll.
Mostly food supplies like dried fish and salted meats; you know, things that keep fresh for a long time.
The Greybeards tend not to get out much, if you catch my meaning.
Well, there's the occasional wolf pack or stray, but that's all I've ever had to deal with. Shouldn't be a problem for the likes of you.
Other than that, watch your footing. In these wintry conditions, the stairs can be treacherous.
Quite a climb, wasn't it?
Anyway, much appreciated. Here, take this for your troubles.
Maybe I'll hold off and go another day. Get some rest first.
Pardon me, milady. Would you care to hear me play my lute?
You a pilgrim or just passing through?
Watch yourself on High Hrothgar, I hear wolves prowl the steps.
You better not be here to stir up any trouble.
Narfi's sad now. Still wait for Reyda. No more sleep. No, no... no.
Ah, good to see you again! How did that delivery go?
Passing through on your way to High Hrothgar? About to make a delivery up there myself.
This has to be the worst place in Skyrim to run a mill. How am I supposed to do anything with all those bears running around?
Keep your eyes open in the barrow... that's how they get you!
If you're headed up to the monastery, watch your step... it's a long way down.
If you pass through again, stop by for a drink.
A pleasant journey, milord.
A pleasant journey, milady.
Just get out of town as fast as you can. Nothing for anyone here.
Sure, leave. Everyone else does.
I wish I could go with you...
Narfi so happy! So happy! So happy!
The mountain will eat you! Watch the mountain!
If you see Reyda, tell her that Narfi misses her and to come home soon... very soon. Soon... soon, like the moon!
Reyda! Reyyyyda!
Hmph. What a boring conversation.
Thanks again for the legwork.
Be careful up there.
Welp, time to gather more supplies I guess.
If you get a chance, kill a few more bears for me, will you?
Now go give those stupid bears what's coming to them!
Just get out of my way, I've got work to do.
Have yourself a great day!
This is life, brother. The struggle is what reminds us to draw breath.
This is life, sister. The struggle is what reminds us to draw breath.
Family and honor. That's what it means to be one of us, boy.
Family and honor. That's what it means to be one of us, girl.
How did you... you reach beyond your station, new blood. Let's wait and see how we judge your Trial before speaking further.
You shouldn't even know about that. Remember you're not a full Companion yet, whelp. Know your place.
What did I tell you? Keep quiet about that.
I think there are some things that... oh, wait. Are you trying to trick me? You are not a full-blood yet. We can't talk about that.
We should not be talking about that.
I don't know what you're talking about. But if I were you, I'd hold my tongue.
I don't know who you've been talking to, but they were clearly mistaken.
Especially for a new whelp, you should choose your words more carefully.
For me, a heavy burden.
The beastblood has given me prowess, but my soul is now forfeit to Hircine upon my death.
I keep searching for some hope of Sovngarde, but to no avail.
A sensitive question. You may want to be careful broaching that subject with some.
The old man and I have our differences when it comes to this. It's a blessing, given by Hircine.
To worry about the hereafter means losing sight of the now.
What do I care about Sovngarde? Give me the power to tear my foes as a beast of the wild, and I will savor it.
I was raised in the woods. Hunting every animal I could see, with my father. I know them all as well as I know my own mind.
And when I feel myself filling with the blood of the wolf... it's like I'm bringing all the strength of all the beasts inside me.
But my mind and soul remain my own. I become more than either human or beast.
It's like being born. Every time.
I've enjoyed the boons that come with beastblood. Just like every member of the Circle.
Kodlak is right, though. We've given a piece of our souls for this power. I know my mythic histories. Bargains like that lead to ruin.
This is a curse that was laid upon us. That much is clear.
I like being stronger when I become the wolf. But Kodlak says it is bad for the soul, and I trust him.
Politics are something best avoided. I prefer more personal confrontations, myself.
None of our business. There's no honor to be had in the squabbling of jarls.
Kodlak says not to worry about it. I worry that a bunch of snowberries are out there earning glory while we stay out of it.
But I'll follow the old man's lead. As ever.
Too confusing for me. Empire, Nords, Talos. Who cares?
Just tell me who needs bludgeoning.
There are always good reasons to fight. I just wish this war had them.
Who cares who worships what dead god? Give me something to make me draw my sword.
I learned the ways of the blade in the Great War. Nearly lost my life outside the Imperial City.
I came home to Skyrim when it was all over. But I wasn't much good at anything other than fighting.
Wandered around as a blade for hire. Was a damned good one, too.
The money was good, and the women were good, and the drinks were good. But I was losing myself. My heart.
I was lucky the Companions found me. Now there's a reason to be fighting. The honor of my brothers and sisters is worth more than coin.
Of course, the money is still good. And the drinks!
My mother was a Companion. And her mother. And all the women in my family, back to Hrotti Blackblade.
I stayed with my father in the woods until I was old enough for my Trial. We hunted everything there was to hunt.
Good training.
Ma didn't live long enough to see me join, but I fight to honor her and all my Shield-Sisters through time.
Vilkas and I have been here since we were little whelps.
Our father, Jergen, raised us here.
Even Vignar couldn't remember Companions younger than us!
To hear Farkas tell it, our father raised us here as happy pups, running around biting knees.
I love my brother, but his brains are not his strong suit.
We were brought here by Jergen. Whether he was our father or not, I don't care.
He left to fight in the Great War and never came back. So he's not my problem anymore.
We've been here as long as either of us can remember, though. So try to show some respect.
Like most of our band, I found this family after losing my own.
I traveled the length and breadth of this land, learning all I could of the sword and the axe.
I was just a boy, but I had the fire of a man in my heart. Eventually, my body caught up to my spirit.
My predecessor, Askar, found me in Hammerfell. I was serving as bodyguard for some weak-necked lord out there.
He brought me back here, and I realized... that I was actually coming home.
I work to bring honor to this family, and to the family that I lost. For my mother, my father, and my grandfather. For all my Shield-Siblings.
Fortune and glory, friend. Fortune and glory.
So I wouldn't have to answer stupid questions from idiots.
Are you kidding? I've wanted to be with them since I was a little girl.
Haven't you heard the stories of Kodlak and Skjor fighting off the hundred-and-one Orc berserkers?
Skjor says it was more like forty, but he's just being modest.
Where else would I want to be but here? Learning from them, fighting at their sides.
A man of my station has many debts. I'm not much use at labor or learning, but I can throw a punch with the best of 'em.
The Companions let me fight for gold, and so I fight.
When you get to be my age, you don't remember why you did much of anything.
Only that it happened.
Oh, I'm not a Companion, myself. I just help Vignar. He took me in when I was having a tough time, and... well, I owe him.
Ask any fool around here, and get a different answer.
Mercenaries. Warriors of honor. Brothers and sisters of the blade. Drunken rabble. Take your pick.
I've been here longer than most of them. Even I don't know sometimes. I just hope they don't kill each other.
The Companions are my family. We fight so that other people don't have to.
We bring honor and glory to ourselves and each other.
You'll hear some of the brighter faces around here talk about honor and glory.
I've got nothing against it, but for me, the promise of coin is what feeds my blade.
Wherever someone in Skyrim doesn't feel up to defending their own honor, we'll take up their burden.
Your question carries more weight than you may know, newcomer. The sort of thing some of us spend our lives pondering.
The difference between a noble band of warriors and a ragged bunch of assassins is as thin as a blade's edge.
I try to hold us to the right path.
Why would you even come to Jorrvaskr without knowing what we stand for?
This is not play-acting, here. We are the true spirit of Skyrim. Honor is in our blood, death in our hearts.
If you seek either, you've found the right place.
Are you looking to join? I wouldn't get your hopes up.
The short answer is: we fight. The long answer is... eh, forget it. You're not worth my time.
Only the most famous warrior band in all of Skyrim. Have you been living with the horkers?
When we arrive, blood is spilled and our blades sing to the glories of Ysgramor.
We're the best damned fighters in Skyrim. At least, some of us are.
They used to be something. Nowadays they just squabble amongst themselves.
Take my advice and keep away. You look too smart to get caught up with this bunch.
To hear Vignar tell it, they're a bunch of layabouts and useless whiners who used to know how to fight.
But he thinks everything used to be better. I, for one, would trust any Companion with my life.
A good bunch of men and women, who make too much a mess, if you ask me.
It means living such that your Shield-Siblings would proudly say they fought at your side.
Glory in battle, honor in life.
Deal with problems head on. Leave whispers and sneaking to the gutter rats who can't fight for themselves.
I wish more of our ranks asked such questions.
I've started seeing the Companions as a kind of family.
I've battled next to every man and woman here, and the bonds of sweat are stronger than those of blood.
I would trust anyone here with my life, which is more than I'd say for the milk drinkers who raised me.
It means resting your haunches in Whiterun more than I'd care to, for one.
But when it's time to draw blood, there's no one in Skyrim I'd rather have at my back.
It means waking up every day knowing that you could die, and having to earn your life by clawing for every breath.
I don't know how those cozy lords manage to drag themselves out of bed every day. Why bother if you're not living?
I've never been a smart one. But the Companions welcome anyone with the heart of a warrior.
When we step into battle, we fight for our own name, and the name of the Companions.
There's been a group called the Companions for over 4000 years. It's been many different things in that time.
A conquering army. Ruthless mercenaries. A band of drunken louts. And the esteemed company you see before you.
But there's always been a kind of honor to it. We don't deal in politics or underhanded sneaking.
We try to uphold the legacy of Ysgramor. To bear his good name such that it never be forgotten, and always be spoken with reverence.
It means signing on for the Nord ideas of how to live your life. Honor, battle, glory, all that.
"Even an elf can be born with the heart of a Nord," Skjor said when I joined. I think he meant it as a compliment.
Just because you've been accepted here so quickly, don't think I'm going to help you rise any faster.
Fight well, don't be a lout, and you'll be fine. You don't need my help.
So much.
This group... this family... this band... this is the best thing I've ever been a part of.
The oldest fighting group in Skyrim, and nothing but glory from Ysgramor's day to our own.
To be counted in that line is a bit of immortality. Even if I never see Sovngarde, I'll have that much.
It means when I have troubles, I always have someone on my side.
We have Shield-Siblings to keep our backs as dangerous as our fronts.
I think.
My advice? Don't overthink it. Fight well and the rest takes care of itself.
Soon enough, it'll be me. In the meanwhile, the Circle handles our daily routine, and Kodlak advises them as Harbinger.
You don't just waddle in here and get to speak to the boss. Who do you think you are?
If you want to embarrass yourself, I won't stop you. Kodlak is the Harbinger. He's the closest thing you'll find to a leader around here.
The Circle are the ones who parcel out the jobs.
That's Aela, Skjor, and the brothers, Farkas and Vilkas.
Kodlak advises them, but he doesn't really give orders.
In charge of what? I'm in charge of me, and you're in charge of you.
If you're looking to join up, Kodlak's the one to be talking to.
I can't wait to learn from the priestesses.
Please, let's hurry to the Temple before the Forsworn find me again.
I hope Fjotra is happy in the temple. We're so honored to have given her.
Thank you for getting her back from the Forsworn. Dibella was smart to have sent you.
I swear, if those things have laid a hand on her...
How could they live like this? It's disgusting.
Please step back, there's an execution in process.
Last warning. Stand back from the execution.
Do it, Listener. Kill me.
You have no choice. You must complete your contract. You must kill me.
Please, Listener. Release my soul, and let Sithis be the judge.
Of course you do.
All right. What's your fancy?
My family were in the Mythic Dawn. My museum is our legacy.
Wrong or right, the Mythic Dawn changed Tamriel's history. They deserve study.
Scholars from around the world will come here one day.
These spells are difficult, even for one as skilled as I.
To even begin to inscribe them, I need something very special. A sigil stone from an Oblivion gate.
I do not have one in my possession. Nor do you, I'm sure. So we require a Daedra to retrieve one for us.
You will summon a Dremora, and order it to bring a sigil stone to you. I will then inscribe the spells for you.
You control nothing, mortal!
I serve no mortal!
Summoning an Unbound Dremora is not like other Conjuration spells. It will not simply do your bidding.
You must first prove you are in control.
Here is the spell you need. Go to the top of the Hall of Attainment; I have prepared a place for you there.
Because I have no wish to be harmed.
If only that were true.
I will teach you the spell to summon the Dremora, but know that it will only work in a place properly prepared for the event.
Once you have summoned the Dremora, you will order it to deliver to you a sigil stone from an Oblivion gate.
Once I have the sigil stone, I shall inscribe the spells that you seek.
Every blade I make honors Malacath. Every rivet, every plate of armor is for him.
Only a true Orc knows how to temper Orichalcum properly.
The old traditions are passed down from one generation to the next. The secrets of smithing are passed down as well.
Lob will one day take my place, and bring honor to our tribe as I have.
I learn much from Garakh every day. I must learn if I am to take her place.
I've worked with metal long enough that I almost never burn myself anymore.
My weapons will someday be as strong as my mother's, and Malacath will be pleased with me.
The days of staying within Largashbur's walls are over.
Malacath demands much of us now. We will not fail him again.
Gularzob has been chosen as chief. He will one day be great, but he has much to learn.
Largashbur will not soon forget what you have done. We have a second chance thanks to you.
The ways of the mystic are known to me. I have remedies and cures, should you need them.
It is good to finally know that Malacath has not forsaken us.
We have learned from our mistakes. Malacath's needs come first, and we will serve him faithfully.
I will do my best to lead the tribe, but I don't know why Malacath chose me.
I am honored to have been chosen to lead. But I know that being picked by Malacath does not mean that I am ready.
Yamarz betrayed us all. I will never allow that to happen again, so long as I rule.
Do not raise arms against my people, and you shall always have a place here, Champion of Malacath.
Atub is wise beyond her years. I take her advice seriously, and you should as well.
I am... surprised that Gularzob was named chief.
We are all blood here. One family, one tribe.
I will serve Gularzob as best I can, even though I am his elder.
A Nord nobleman I served was stabbed in the night. Wasn't me, but I knew I'd be blamed.
So I ran. Joined the Forsworn. Started killing. Got caught. Now I'm here.
Innocent? So was I, for the first one. The other murders were all me, though.
Getting the shakes, huh? All right. Take it. Old gods keep you.
Smart.
Ah, you're a clever one aren't you? Now, here's your shiv. Promise you won't ever use it on me, okay?
Because life was better under the old ways. No Nords and their laws.
Small blade. Easy to hide. I mean, sure, you could just swing a pickaxe into someone's face, but people tend to see that coming.
Got a problem with a prisoner? Get a shiv. Hear Grisvar has a spare, if you could get him to part with it.
Ah, you want protection? I can get you what you need.
Maybe you can do something for me, first? Duach has a bottle of Skooma. Finest distilled Moon Sugar. I'm shaking just thinking about it.
What are you in for, new blood?
All right, but if you change your mind. Not like we're going anywhere.
They come in here once a week to clean out the bodies, grab any ore we've mined, and beat down the troublemakers.
That's the only time when we get food, too. And if there's not enough ore mined up, we don't get any.
Petty thieving, huh? Just like poor Grisvar. Hopefully you'll learn your lesson faster than he has.
One day, the Forsworn will paint the walls of Markarth in your kinsmen's blood. Best you not be there on that day, Nord.
One day, the Forsworn will paint the walls of Markarth in their blood.
Easy there. I didn't mean nothing. Take it.
That's it.
You give me one more look, and I cut you open. That Skooma's mine.
Violent one, huh? Best keep that to yourself, new blood. Others find out, they'll consider that a challenge.
Here's your shiv. Promise you won't ever use it on me, okay?
I hear you're in for life. Sad. I'd get a shiv if I was you, or maybe some Skooma to drown your troubles.
My advice? Serve your time at the pickaxe and get out. You don't want to end up getting a shiv in the guts over a bottle of Skooma.
Wait for me.
You wouldn't understand, Housecarl. You ain't a Nord.
Now, let me show you something else I found... very intriguing... I think your employers may be interested as well...
My... associate here will be pleased to see your handiwork. She discovered its location, by means she has so far declined to share with me.
Hmm? Ah, yes, the Jarl's protege! Back from Bleak Falls Barrow? You didn't die, it seems.
You have a visitor.
That is where your job ends and mine begins. The work of the mind, sadly undervalued in Skyrim.
Just send me a copy when you've deciphered it.
You'll have to see the Jarl about that. Maybe his steward, Avenicci. I'm sure one of them will pay you appropriately.
You went into Bleak Falls Barrow and got that? Nice work.
Here he comes! Find cover and make every arrow count!
So your information was correct after all. And we have our friend here to thank for recovering it for us.
I'd advise you all to trust in the strength of your sword arm over tales and legends.
But I don't need some mythical Dragonborn. Someone who can put down a dragon is more than enough for me.
Oh, I thought you were already on your way to Bleak Falls Barrow...
What are you still doing here? The Jarl said retrieving that stone tablet is a priority. Remember?
I've been all across Tamriel. I've seen plenty of things just as outlandish as this.
Skooma addict. Get lost.
Now that's what I call a punch. You got me.
Still alive, prisoner? Keep close to me if you want to stay that way.
Stay close to the wall!
That a boy. You're doing great.
Haming, you need to get over here. Now!
That's it, son. Make me proud.
Gods guide you, Hadvar.
Get up, papa! Get up!
Then we got nothin' more to talk about.
Argonian Ale! I can almost taste it!
Well, let's see... travel-stained clothes, worn soles, blank and unintelligent expression... Yes, in fact you do.
Good. You're clearly better suited than I am to carry out such a menial task.
Oh, all right then, Listener. Cicero will just carry on with what he was doing. Until you say otherwise.
Ooh! Listener! That tickles!
Would you like to buy a lady a drink?
You tempt me mightily, Alva. But I won't cuckhold another man, even on murderer like Hroggar.
Hroggar doesn't have to know if we sit together. Or drink together. I doubt he would even care.
If you leave that kinslayer Hroggar, I would be happy to keep your company.
I'm bored, Benor. Nobody wants to have any fun with me.
After all you've put that man through. You should be more loyal to him.
Forget about Hroggar. He's always so tired lately. I need someone with a little fire in their blood.
I understand.
Of course.
How much?
It can't be helped.
Not my problem.
It would seem so.
It's all in this note.
You've done well.
Maybe you can help me.
Thank you. Here, this is for you.
Just bring it back to me.
I can pay you.
Well, aren't you the handsome one. You and I should spend some time together.
Well, aren't you the pretty one. I bet all the men chase after you.
I... I'm not one for talk. Try to keep to myself.
In a fight, if you see a big wind up, get out of the way or bash him with your shield. Attacks like that go right through a regular block.
When the other guy has his shield up, regular blows are useless. Wind up and hit him with a really powerful attack to get past that shield.
Even if your opponent doesn't have a shield, those big, powerful blows can stagger him. Leaves him open for a quick follow up blow.
Using two weapons means you've got no defense. You can't block anything. But if you hit him with both at the same time, he may not get up again.
Only a fool lets himself get surrounded. Back up and make them come at you one at a time.
Those big, powerful blows are tiring. Pace yourself until you've got the stamina to do one.
Any fool can just flail about, swinging his weapon as fast as he can. Any fool can die. Keep your guard up and wait for an opening.
Don't just stand still to do your big wind-up blows. Which way you're moving changes what happens and can be a big advantage.
What would Hroggar say if he heard I was drinking with you?
Never!
I am not your plaything, worm!
I'll tear out your heart first!
Well it stops now, and that's final!
Right away, milord.
Right away, milady.
Very well.
Welcome to the Vilemyr Inn. If there's anything I can get you, just let me know.
Finally, someone comes in. Kick off your boots, stay awhile. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with. I got nothing but time these days.
Thank you, milord.
Thank you, milady.
Hey, Talen... get off your lazy tail and take care of the customer!
Frabbi, a customer needs a drink!
Keep your scales on.
Too lazy to give him a drink yourself? Oaf.
That's so sweet of you.
I'll tell you what... I'll play a special song, just for you, for no charge at all.
Why thank, you... you're very kind. Still interested?
I play the lute. If you'd like to hear something, it only costs five gold.
My business is falling apart, my apprentice never listens to me and now there's talk of dragons.
If I hadn't sunk every bit of gold I had into my mill, I would have picked up and left Ivarstead long ago.
Pardon me, milord. Would you care to hear me play my lute?
Pilgrim or not, if I were you, I'd move right through our backwards little town.
Can you believe it? Another pile of logs ruined.
I hope life's treating you well, friend.
No. This coven has existed for thousands of years. Namira is a goddess. She has been worshipped since time began.
I'm honored to be leading the latest generation of Namira's faithful. Those who have embraced what they are.
There's a cave here. Bears if we're lucky. Trolls if we're not.
Oh, look. A Nordic ruin.
A dwarven city. A place of mystery and death.
Then my answer is the Hall of the Dead is closed. By order of the Jarl. Understand?
What a sight....
I smell blood.
Well then, you're talking to the right person. I'm the only one left in Tamriel that can get my hands on it.
It's damn near impossible to find anymore. You want to buy it off of me?
I do. Still want to buy it?
I'm sorry you feel my illegal contraband is overpriced, perhaps you should bring it up at the next merchant guild meeting.
Look, you want it, I got it. You know the price, so talk to me when you want to cough up the gold.
Good. Here, take this key.
The Balmora Blue is locked in a chest under the docks near the Red Wave. Hope you like getting wet.
Sorry, guess it's too rich for your blood. Come back when you find the coin.
Not sure. I know that it starts with Moon Sugar, but all sorts of other ingredients are added to increase its potency.
Used to be a lucrative underworld commodity when Balmora was still standing. Now the stuff is beyond valuable.
It's also very illegal. Anyone caught with Balmora Blue looks forward to rotting in jail for a very long time.
There's pirates and then there's the crew of the Red Wave. They're in a class by themselves.
They usually make runs along the coast shipping all sorts of contraband to and from Morrowind.
Rumor has it they can get you anything for the right price.
I'm afraid not. How else can a poor, overworked sailor like myself expect to earn a living?
Oblivion take them. Every miserable last one of them.
Why didn't you say so immediately?!
My men are fearless. It's the Imperial milk drinkers I'm worried about.
No. Of course not. Just - don't let me down Cipius.
We'll need to set up water brigades to combat the flames.
You Imperials are efficient, I'll give you that.
How long until they arrive?
They have catapults.
Sir!
Not long. They're hiding in the country side.
But... sir...
This is it! Time to see what these Stormcloaks are made of.
Sir!
My scouts tell me they're loading them with fire.
Move it soldier. Spread the word. Go. Go. Go.
Sir, they're on the move. They'll be at the gates at any moment!
The men will be fighting in flames.
Already taken care of.
The Stormcloaks are here. In force. Get down to the front line. This is it. We must hold the city. Move it!
Sir.
Take a moment to breathe, soldier.
If you prefer I took my men and left...
Sir, I tried.
The outer walls are strong. If we can hold them there...
You'll be doing whatever Rikke tells you to do. And, I expect you to find creative ways to disrupt the Stormcloaks along the way.
You'll be doing whatever Galmar tells you to do, and causing as much mayhem as possible for the Empire and any Jarl who supports them.
What?!
Damnit. What's he waiting for?
The men are already gathering at the gates.
For the Empire!
Talos guide you.
Damnit. Where'd they get catapults? The city walls are already falling apart as it is.
So, he wants to take my city, walls intact.
Sir! I... I have... <breathing hard>
Breathe!
I don't want to die. I can't die here!
Weak. He's weak. You're strong. Crush him.
No. Kill him. Crush his bones. Tear at his flesh.
Madanach's guard. Big, even for an Orc. Heard he ripped a man's arm off and beat him to death with it. He's old-fashioned like that.
Come out! We know you're here!
Fresh food. No wood rot on the furniture. Someone's been here. Recently.
But the people I asked say no one enters or leaves....
If you're asking, that means you're the new lifer. Tough luck, friend. Those guards sold you out but good.
No one talks to Madanach, I'm afraid. Not without getting past Borkul the Beast.
And you don't want to talk to Borkul the Beast.
You will kill. You will kill, or you will die!
That's it. Something's inside the house. Come on, we're getting to the bottom of this.
Stendarr's Mercy! This isn't an ordinary Daedra. We have to get help.
Get out of my head, Daedra!
You first. Come on. Let's go.
Wait. Did you hear that? I think it came this way.
There's another door. See if you can get it open.
The finest jewelry in all of Markarth.
Redguard craftsmanship in every piece.
The bloodiest beef in the Reach.
You want a cut of venison or mutton? Of course you do.
Now, if you'll both proceed to the armory to don your Nightingale Armor, we can begin the Oath.
Nord nonsense?! Why you puffed-up ignorant... these are our sacred traditions that go back to the founding of the First Empire!
Yes, I could use someone to fetch something for me.
Well, when I say fetch, I really mean delve into a dangerous ruin in search of an ancient stone tablet that may or may not actually be there.
So the Jarl thinks you can be of use to me? Oh yes, he must be referring to my research into the dragons.
Capable as he may be, I don't see any signs of him being this, what, "Dragonborn."
Capable as she may be, I don't see any signs of her being this, what, "Dragonborn."
Hrongar. Don't be so hard on Avenicci.
Whatever happened when you killed that dragon, it revealed something in you, and the Greybeards heard it.
If they think you're Dragonborn, who are we to argue?
You'd better get up to High Hrothgar immediately. There's no refusing the summons of the Greybeards. It's a tremendous honor.
Hrongar, calm yourself. What does any of this Nord nonsense have to do with our friend here?
Good. You're finally here. The Jarl's been waiting for you.
I envy you the chance to see this dragon up close!
That's the Greybeards' business, not ours.
The Dragonborn is said to be uniquely gifted in the Voice - the ability to focus your vital essence into a Thu'um, or Shout.
If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your gift.
I want to hear about this dragon.
It's just that... what do these Greybeards want with him?
It's just that... what do these Greybeards want with her?
I meant no disrespect, of course.
Off to Bleak Falls Barrow with you. The Jarl is not a patient man. Neither am I, come to think of it.
I don't know if you've heard, but Jarl Balgruuf said my work is a priority. Which means you should be on your way.
It's too bad they executed Roggvir before we got here.
The Greybeards have summoned the Dragonborn to High Hrothgar. Such a thing has not happened for... hundreds of years.
There was a fight down in the Ratway, but reports are... sketchy. Some say it was Thalmor Agents, others say Thieves Guild. Some say it was both.
Word is Elenwen's furious. Big party at the Thalmor Embassy, and one of the guests went someplace they shouldn't. Caused quite a commotion.
Have you heard? There's been another dragon attack. Kynesgrove, this time.
What could it mean? The return of the Dragonborn? And who among us could possibly hold that honor? And such power?
By the gods, it's true, isn't it? A dragon has attacked Whiterun. How could mere men bring down such a beast?
By Ysmir, you did it. You killed a dragon.
There's been talk amongst the guards. That you are... Dragonborn. But such a thing... Surely that's not possible...
The guards in Dragonsreach say you've aided the Jarl. On behalf of Whiterun, I thank you.
I've seen you helping the Jarl. If only all visitors to Whiterun were as noble.
It was you convinced the Jarl to trap that dragon in the keep. And by Ysmir, it actually worked.
There's a dragon. Here. In Dragonsreach. And I thought it was just a name.
We had that beast contained. And you released it! Why? Why would you do such a thing?
Is it true what they say? There was a dragon held captive in Whiterun, and you... you released it? By the gods, man, why?
Is it true what they say? There was a dragon held captive in Whiterun, and you... you released it? By the gods, woman, why?
The Greybeards have arranged a truce between the Stormcloaks and Imperials. How long can such a thing last?
The Greybeards have called a peace council, at High Hrothgar. And can you believe it - both Ulfric Stormcloak and General Tullius have agreed to go.
Word is spreading like magefire! The great evil has been vanquished! You have truly saved us all!
All hail the Dragonborn!
In the ancient tongue, you are Dovahkiin - Dragonborn!
By the gods. The Emperor's been murdered. On his own ship... moored in the inlet. It was... the Dark Brotherhood. There was nothing we could do...
That old woman who runs the orphanage has been murdered. Say, didn't I see you coming out of there...?
Been a murder, over in Riften. Some old lady who runs an orphanage. Those poor children must be heartbroken.
Hey! You're the one who killed Alain Dufont, up in Raldbthar. Oh yeah, word about that got around. Man was a piece of bandit trash, is what I hear.
The Emperor's cousin, killed. Right here in Solitude. I feel nothing but shame...
The cousin of the Emperor was murdered! In Solitude, at her own damn wedding. Not a chance we would have let that happen here.
You didn't hear this from me, but we've uncovered a plot to kill the Emperor! It gets better. The ringleader? One of the Emperor's own elite guard...
There was some ruckus over by that weird black door. You weren't involved in that craziness, were you?
Been lots of people coming and going through that creepy black door. And noises...  I'd say it sounds like construction, but that's crazy. Right?
There are some that say the Dark Brotherhood is back, and stronger than ever. That they've made Dawnstar their home. Tell me - am I wrong?
The guards have been saying things about you... That you're the one that... Well... They say the Dark Brotherhood... Well. Never mind. Sir.
The guards have been saying things about you... That you're the one that... Well... They say the Dark Brotherhood... Well. Never mind. Ma'am.
Wait, I know you. You went into the Emperor's Tower, just before... before his decoy was murdered...
We just received word that somebody tried to kill the Emperor! Turned out to just be his double, but can you imagine if he had succeeded?
They're saying the Dark Brotherhood has been destroyed. Wiped off the face off Tamriel. But that's not entirely true... is it?
You're the one all the guards are talking about. You went into that Sanctuary... wiped out the Dark Brotherhood! Friend, I owe you a drink.
Maybe you haven't heard, but the Thieves Guild is back. Windhelm is their city now, if you catch my meaning...
Markarth's a different place, now. Do business here, and you do business with the Thieves Guild.
A change has swept into Solitude, like a breeze off the ocean - the Thieves Guild has come...
Ain't no doubt about it. The Thieves Guild is back, and they've got Riften in their grip.
Welcome to Riften, home of the Thieves Guild. Or so they'd have you believe. It's all lies. They're just thugs... vermin, creeping around the Ratway.
The say the College has been snooping around Saarthal. Mages in a burial crypt. No good can come of that...
Some are saying the Psijic Monks have been seen in Skyrim. They're an ancient order. I had 'em figured for dead.
You hear the news? Those damn mages blew up Winterhold! Guess it was just a matter of time...
Word is the College of Mages is back to normal. If there's such a thing for that lot.
Your name's been cleared... by Thonar Silver-Blood. Consider yourself lucky.
So Madanach was behind those murders. Gods help us.
By Shor, is that... is that Azura's Star? How did you come to possess such a rare treasure?
Who did you have to kill to get that blade?
That mace... get it away from me. Get it away...
Ugh. Your breath is foul. What've you been eating?
As if Skyrim didn't have enough outsiders. Now we've got mercenaries from Hammerfell struttin' about...
From what I hear, you're the one gave the Thalmor a black eye. Sure know how to pick your enemies, don't you?
You've come to Dawnstar at a strange time, friend. Seems everyone in the town is having nightmares...
The damned nightmares have finally stopped. Don't suppose you'd know anything about that?
You've been seen in the company of the Companions. That's an honorable path you're on, friend.
I know of your deeds, and am honored to address a member of the Companions.
Uch. Been tending your hounds? You smell like wet dog.
Heard about what you did. Killing those foul witches. Well done, Companion.
Kodlak killed, Jorrvaskr attacked. How could this happen, within the walls of Whiterun?
I heard about the attack on Jorrvaskr, and the killing of your honored leader. You have my sympathies, Companion.
Kodlak White-Mane has been laid to rest, I know. May his soul live forever in the halls of Sovngarde.
You... you're the Harbinger. Of the Companions. It is my honor to stand before you.
I hear the East Empire Company has reopened, thanks in no small part to you. Well done. All of Skyrim benefits from their trade.
You did this city a great service by catching that killer. Well done.
Good thing you did for Nurelion, retrieving that phial for him before he died. Man was obsessed.
By Ysmir, you've helped save the Gildergreen. Words can't express what that means to this city.
That sapling you recovered means the Gildergreen will live again. Your deed honors the people of Whiterun.
You've actually done it. You've found the Sybil of Dibella. You have done Markarth a tremendous service.
You... you were talking to that murderous savage. And then he escaped. What are you hiding?
First Torygg and now Elisif. Damn those Stormcloaks. Let's hope that Erikur fares better than they did.
Filthy she-hound Elisif got what she deserved. Let her serve as "High Queen" of Oblivion, for all I care.
Releasing a captured dragon. I don't think I'll ever understand that one...
Yes, Dovahkiin? How can I help you?
You have vanquished a great evil from Skyrim. You have saved this land... and our very souls.
No matter what else happens, the guards will always be grateful for everything you've done.
Looks like it's really happening. A peace council, attended by both Ulfric and Tullius, up on High Hrothgar. Maybe there's hope for Skyrim yet.
This truce with the Stormcloaks won't last a fortnight. Bloody savages will kill us while we sleep.
